## CBS Group Draft Contract. 19112020 brj comments.docx

*File: `CBS Group Draft Contract. 19112020 brj comments.docx`*

CBS Group Pty Ltd and Downer

Environmental Energy Finance

Master Services Agreement

Master Agreement 

Parties

CBS GROUP* PTY LTD  ABN 20164870080 of Level 3, 480 Collins Street, MELBOURNE VIC 3000 

Downer* (Partner)

*Includes all agents, parties, contractors and suppliers acting for named Principals

CBS Group are accredited under various Environment Schemes and carries on a business involving capturing, ensuring compliance and submitting information as required for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates), the trading and sale of the Certificates, and consulting services associated with these activities.

Downer carries on a business that at times involves consulting and advice on  the procurement, design, installation, servicing and/or maintenance of efficient end-user equipment by their Customers which may be entitled for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates) under the Environmental Schemes.  These are Scheme Activities performed using Approved Products & Services.

CBS Group has agreed to provide Services to Downer and Downer’s Customers, to enable funding from Australian Environment Schemes.  CBS Group will work with Downer to collect evidence. Downer will receive payments based on the successful registration of certificates for the nominated projects (Scheme Activities)

Definitions

In this Agreement:

Accreditations means any and all accreditations, qualifications and approvals that Downer and its personnel must hold at law as required by the Scheme and as otherwise required by CBS Group;

Accredited (Certificate) Providers means the party holds an accreditation under one or more of the Environment Schemes.

ACL means the Australian Consumer Law Schedule of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth).

Activity (Scheme Activity) means the installation, upgrade, or modification or energy-efficiency or renewable-energy equipment or processes using Approved Products & Services that may be entitled to Environment Certificates under the Environment Schemes.

Agreement means this agreement (including any schedule or annexure), and the Forward Contracts;

Approved Products & Services means products, services and materials necessary for the provision of Downer Services that are expressly approved for use under the Environmental Schemes or as otherwise set outby CBS Group;

Business Day means Monday to Friday excluding public holidays;

Certificates means the Environmental Products, as appropriate;

Confidential Information means:

all business and financial information, sales and supply details, marketing strategies, customer and supplier listings, business listings, pricing information, information concerning CBS Group or its customers and business associates; 

all (if any) other information relating to CBS Group or its business which, by its nature, places or potentially places CBS Group at an advantage over its present or future business competitors, including but not limited to, the CBS Workbooks and CBS Tools, processes and workflows; and

any other information that would otherwise at law be considered secret or confidential information of CBS Group;

whether or not marked "Confidential" BUT does not include information which:

at the time of first disclosure by CBS Group to Downer is already in the public domain;

after disclosure by CBS Group, becomes part of the public domain otherwise than by disclosure in breach of the terms of this Agreement; and

Downer can prove that the information was in its possession before the time of first disclosure by or discovery from CBS Group.

Customer means the energy user (end-customer) to whom Downer provides the installation of efficient end-user equipment (Approved Products) in accordance with the Scheme;

Certificates see Environmental Products

Default Interest Rate means the rate per annum of two percent (2%) higher than the rate fixed for the time being under Section 2 of the Penalty Interest Rates Act 1983 (Vic);

Documentation means CBS Group’s checklists and forms relevant to the Scheme, the Requirements and the Specifications, as provided to Downer and updated from time to time;

Effective Date means the date of execution of this Agreement;

Encumbrance means:

any:

legal or equitable interest or power created, arising in or reserved in or over an interest in any property or asset; or

security for payment of money, performance of obligations or protection against default;

anything or preferential interest or arrangement of any kind giving a person priority or preference over claims or other persons with respect to any property or asset;

a PPSA Security Interest; or

an agreement or arrangement, regardless of whether or not it is enforceable, to grant or create anything referred to in (i), or (ii) above;

Environmental Products (Certificates) means certificates created under the Environmental Schemes, including but not limited to ESCs, VEECs, STCs, LGCs, and ACCUs as relevant;

Environmental Scheme (Schemes) means each of the Government Energy Efficiency, Renewable, and Carbon Abatement Schemes; including, but not limited to the ESS, VEU, REES, EEIS, RET, CFI, as relevant; ESS (NSW Energy Saving Scheme), VEET (Victorian Energy Efficiency Target), REES (South Australian Retailer Energy Efficient Scheme), EEIS (ACT Energy Efficiency Improvement Scheme), RET (Federal Renewable Energy Target), CFI (Federal Carbon Farming Initiative).

Evidence means any and all documents, requests, quotations, receipts, job sheets, time sheets, notes and confirmations relating to provision of Downer Services by Downer; 

Exclusivity Agreements means a list of customers, opportunities, projects or other group of activities that Downer and CBS Group agree to work exclusively with each other for the purpose of Environment Certificate creation under the Environment Schemes

Forward Contracts means any and all forward certificate delivery agreements between CBS Group and Downer entered in to, or otherwise in operation, during the Term;  

GST means any tax imposed on the supply of goods or services, consumption or value added tax, including without limitation any tax imposed in the A New Tax System (Goods and Services Tax) Act (Cth);

Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, the CBS Group Workbooks, the CBS Group  Tools, software, workflow, and all copyright, trade mark rights, design rights, patent rights and confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to CBS Group  or that relates to the CBS Group ’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it;

Invoice Date means the dates for invoicing the Services as set out under Item 2 of the Schedule;

Licence means the CBS Group ’s licence of CBS Group Tools and CBS Group Workbooks to Downer for the purposes of this Agreement as set out in clause 6 of this Agreement;

PPSA Security Interest means a security interest as defined in the Personal Property Securities Act 2009 (Cth);

Partner Confidential Information means:

all business and financial information, sales and supply details, marketing strategies, customer and supplier listings, business listings, pricing information, information concerning Downer its customers and business associates; 

any other information that would otherwise at law be considered secret or confidential information of Downer;

Partner Instructions means guidance from Downer regarding any aspects of the relationship, such as working with Downers customers.

Partner Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, and all copyright, trademark rights, design rights, patent rights and confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to Downer or that relates to Downer’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it; 

Partner Services means the services provided by Downer under clause 3, and such further services agreed from time to time; This includes services provided to end Customers, and services provided to CBS Group to facilitate registration of Environment Certificates.

Requirements means any and all processes, procedures, duties and obligations that apply to the Scheme and as otherwise set out in the CBS Group Workbooks;

Scheme see Environmental Scheme

Security Amount means the amount payable by Downer to CBS Group as set out in Item 2 of the Schedule;

Specifications means any and all specifications, standards and terms that set out the minimum standards required under the Scheme, and as otherwise set out in the CBS Group Workbooks;

Term means the period from the Effective Date until this Agreement is ended in accordance with clause 15; 

CBS Group Tools means CBS Group ’s systems, data collection tools and record keeping tools designed to assist Downer to meet obligations under the Scheme, including but not limited to workflows, forms, checklists, spreadsheets, software and related documents;

CBS Group Workbooks means the Workbooks, checklists, training materials, and guidelines authored by or on behalf of CBS Group  as published and updated from time to time, setting out, without limitation, the Accreditations, the Requirements, the Specifications, the Approved Products and the Documentation;

CBS Group Services means the services provided by CBS Group under clause 5; and

Year means the period commencing 1 July in one year and ending on 30 June in the following year.

Engagement

Partner engages CBS Group to provide CBS Group Services to Downer and to the end Customer, and Downer agrees to provide Partner Services to CBS Group for the Term on the terms of this Agreement

CBS Group and Downer may in writing agree to vary Downer Services or the CBS Group Services, or provide additional services, from time to time.  Any varied or additional services will be provided on the terms and conditions of this Agreement and such other terms as may be agreed.

Partner Services to CBS Group 

Downer Services incorporate all services provided by Downer to CBS Group as required to give effect to the Environmental Schemes.

During the Term of this Agreement, Downer must provide Downer Services on the terms set out in this Agreement.

Downer’s obligations

Downer warrants that it has the qualifications, experience and expertise appropriate and necessary to perform Downer Services offered to End Customers.

Downer warrants that it has the qualifications, accreditations, experience and expertise appropriate and necessary to provide Downer Services to CBS Group under this Agreement.

In providing Downer Services, Downer must, and must ensure that its staff must:

act honestly, ethically and with due care and skill;

comply with the Environmental Scheme Requirements as directed by CBS Group;

use Approved Products when a Customer is claiming Environmental Certificates;

ensure Downer Services are performed in accordance with the Scheme requirements and are eligible to create Environmental Products as appropriate and directed by CBS Group;

provide Evidence to CBS Group of provision of Downer Services to a particular Customer within 30 Business Days of completion of provision of a Scheme Activity for that Customer; 

submit to CBS Group for its approval any additional activities or services it wishes to include as Partner Services; 

not cause, assist it or otherwise allow the creation of any Encumbrance over any Certificate.

During the Term Downer must:

observe and comply with the CBS Group Workbooks, as modified from time to time, in every respect and ensure that its employees and contractors also do so;

ensure that all advertising materials it uses relating to the provision of Scheme Activities or otherwise relevant to this Agreement identifies CBS Group as the Accredited Person (Victoria) or Accredited Certificate Provider (New South Wales), as appropriate, under the Scheme;

effect and maintain throughout the Term, at its own expense, and ensure any subcontractors also maintain:

public liability insurance with a minimum cover of $10,000,000 per claim; and

product liability insurance with a minimum cover of $10,000,000 per claim;

not make any false or misleading representations about the Scheme, CBS Group, Downer, the CBS Group Services or Downer Services; 

undertake and participate in training for the Environmental Schemes, as directed by CBS Group;

upon request by CBS Group or a Scheme auditor, provide access to Documentation, Evidence and other information relevant to Downer Services and the Scheme for the purposes of inspection or audit, and provide its full assistance to CBS Group and the Scheme auditor in any inspection or audit or audit activity or the implementation of any direction arising from an audit finding;

facilitate and assist CBS Group to access the Customer and the Customer’s assets for the purpose of proving compliance with the Scheme requirements. 

This includes providing evidence, assurance, and facilitating visits or communication with the Customer for third-party auditors as well as the Scheme regulatory staff.

adhere to all applicable policies of CBS Group in force, as amended from time to time, including as set out in the CBS Group Workbooks, updated by CBS Group Newsletters;

comply with all occupational health and safety requirements relevant to the Scheme and generally at law, including but not limited to any requirement to obtain and maintain any registration, licences or permits; and

ensure its employees and contractors are paid in accordance with the law; and

Downer must not:

use the CBS Group Tools other than in providing Downer Services;

use the CBS Group Tools to create Certificates itself or assist third parties to create Certificates;

reverse engineer any of the CBS Group Tools; or

disclose to third parties the CBS Group Tools or the details of any CBS Group Tools.

CBS Group Services

During the Term, CBS Group must provide the following assistance to Downer as CBS Group considers reasonably necessary and appropriate:

Sales guides and quoting tools and estimates for value of Environment Certificates to allow Downer to estimate the value of an upgrade under the Environment Scheme; 

keep confidential details of all Downer sales opportunities and Customer details;

licence and provide the CBS Group Tools to assist Downer record the Evidence and other information necessary to meet the obligations under the Scheme;

provide Documentation to help guide Downer through the steps of the Scheme;

provide to Downer timely communication on changes to the CBS Group Workbooks, procedures or systems under the relevant Scheme or software upgrades by CBS Group:

keep confidential, or depersonalise prior to disclosure, details of all Partner Services proposed by Downer under the Agreement and only disclose the information required under the relevant Scheme;

provide and review quality assurance procedures to aid the accurate creation of Certificates;

perform a quality assurance procedure and alert Downer’s to any missing information within 30 Business Days of receipt of information confirming completion of Downer Services provided to a Customer;

provide training covering the relevant Scheme and the CBS Group Tools; and

retain all records of Downer Services for a period of at least six years or as specified by the Scheme requirements; and

With respect to the creation of Environmental Products, CBS Group must:

Register a Certificate creator under the relevant Scheme;

promptly create Certificates attributable to Downer Services following receipt of the relevant information; and

CBS Group must make payments to Downer based on Item 3

With respect to the creation of Environmental Products, if these are deemed ineligible for due to fraud or negligence by the Customer or Downer

Downer has no claim against CBS Group; and

Downer must indemnify and keep CBS Group indemnified from all claims, suits, demands, loss, damage, cost or liability in relation to the ineligible Environmental Products.

Licence of CBS Group Tools

Subject to the provisions set out in clause 16.1(a), CBS Group licences the CBS Group Tools to Downer during the Term of this Agreement.

Downer must only use the CBS Group Tools for the purposes of providing Downer Services under the terms of this Agreement.

CBS Group may, as it considers necessary, maintain, delete or modify the CBS Group Tools from time to time.

CBS Group Workbooks

CBS Group will publish or provide access to the CBS Group Workbooks, which among other things, sets out various Requirements, Specifications, and responsibilities of Downer, and its provision of Downer Services.

CBS Group may, in its absolute discretion, vary the CBS Group Workbooks from time to time by giving Downer at least 7 days’ notice in writing.

The CBS Group Workbooks form part of this Agreement, and where there is a conflict, the terms of the CBS Group Workbooks shall prevail to the extent of the inconsistency.

Any breach by Downer of any provision contained in the CBS Group Workbooks is deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.

The effective date of any policy or obligation contained in the CBS Group Workbooks shall be the date nominated in the CBS Group Workbooks or updated part or, if no date is nominated, then the first date that the particular amended CBS Group Manual is published on CBS Group ’s website.

The CBS Group Workbooks remain at all times the property of CBS Group, and must be returned to CBS Group immediately upon request or upon termination of this Agreement.

Downer must not copy, reproduce, retain, store, record, computerise, document or duplicate any part of the Workbooks other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement or with written permission from CBS Group.

Security

The Security Payment (Item 2) forms part of the Commission Agreements and is fully recouped via either:

( a) first disbursement of funds from sales or:

( b) a failure event 

Payment 

The Payment to Downer as specified in Item 3

Start-up costs and expenses

Downer must ensure that it has the necessary resources and staff to provide Downer Services.  

General Relationship

Downer is an independent of CBS Group and no agency, employment, partnership or other relationship will exist between Downer and CBS Group. Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed or construed to render CBS Group liable for any cost’s obligations or expenditures pursuant to any relevant employment laws.

Downer must not at any time represent to any other party that it is an employee, servant or agent of CBS Group, but for the purpose of complying with the Scheme:

Downer’s agents must at all times identify themselves to potential or existing Customers including identifying;

the company they represent, and 

that the Accredited Person or Accredited Certificate Provider, as appropriate depending on the Scheme, is CBS Group; and

Downer’s sales and installation documentation provided to the Customer must identify that the Accredited Person or Accredited Certificate Provider, as appropriate depending on the Scheme, is CBS Group.

Downer will have no authority to bind CBS Group legally with respect to any third party unless expressly authorised in writing to do so by CBS Group.

Downer must not:

incur any liability on behalf of CBS Group or in any way pledge or purport to pledge CBS Group ’s credit; and

accept any order nor make any contract binding upon CBS Group without CBS Group ’s prior written approval.

During the term, Downer retains the right to enter into any contract with any other person or persons, company or firm to perform services similar to those contemplated by this Agreement, provided that they do not interfere with Downer’s ability to comply with this Agreement.

Intellectual Property

Downer has no proprietary right or interest in the Intellectual Property.

Downer must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world the Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from or similar to the Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.

Any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from any party of the information comprised in the Intellectual Property whether attributable in whole or in part to Downer, will become and remain the property of CBS Group.

Downer hereby assigns and conveys to CBS Group any intellectual property rights that Downer may acquire arising from any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from the Intellectual Property.

CBS Group has no proprietary right or interest in Downer Intellectual Property.

CBS Group must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world Downer Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from or similar to the Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.

Confidential Information

Downer must:

maintain strict confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information, and must take all reasonable, physical and legal steps necessary to prevent its disclosure;

not divulge any aspect of the Confidential Information to any other person other than pursuant to this Agreement;

not use or exploit the Confidential Information other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement;

not make any written notes, copy, reproduce, retain, store, record, computerise, document or duplicate any part of the Confidential Information which appears in any written or computerised or other recorded form other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement;

not make any improvements or modifications to or derivations or discoveries from any of the Confidential Information other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; and

if it considers it may be necessary to disclose any of the Confidential Information to any other person, obtain prior written authorisation from CBS Group before making such disclosure.

If Downer is uncertain whether any information comprises part of the Confidential Information, then it must seek direction from CBS Group before divulging the information.

Downer may disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its officers and employees, and its legal, financial or other advisers and their respective employees and officers who:

have a need to know for the purposes of this Agreement, and only to the extent that each has a need to know;

are aware that the Confidential Information must be kept confidential; and

have agreed in writing to comply with substantially the same obligations in respect of the Confidential Information as those imposed on Downer under this Agreement.

The obligations on Downer in relation to Confidential Information survive termination of the Agreement.

CBS Group and any subcontractor’s engagement by CBS Group to fulfil CBS Group Services under this agreement must: 

maintain strict confidentiality in relation to Downer Confidential Information, as set out in the Schedule, and must take all reasonable, physical and legal steps necessary to prevent its disclosure; and

not divulge any aspect of Downer Confidential Information to any other person other than pursuant to this Agreement.

Partner’s Employees and Contractors

Downer warrants that it will make a full and detailed check of all personnel providing Downer Services to ensure that they are fully qualified, accredited and suitable to perform Downer Services.

Downer’s staff will be trained by Downer to diligently, efficiently and properly carry out Downer Services. 

Downer will be liable for any breach of this Agreement by its officers, employees, contractors and advisers.  Downer must provide CBS Group with details of any contactors it intends to utilise, for approval.

During the Term, Downer must:

ensure that its employees, agents or approved subcontractors are competent and have the necessary skills to provide Downer Services; and

adequately and properly manage, direct and control its employees, agents and subcontractors in providing Downer Services.

Downer may disclose Confidential Information to those of its officers and employees and its legal, financial or other advisers and their respective employees and officers who:

have a need to know (but only if and to the extent that they have a need to know) to assist Downer to properly market or sell its products and provide Downer Services;

are aware that Confidential Information must be kept confidential; and

in the case of employees and officers, have agreed in writing to comply with the terms of this Agreement as if they were a party to this Agreement.

Termination

Either party may terminate this Agreement upon the provision of 90 days’ notice to the other party in writing.

If either party ("Defaulting Party"):

commits a breach of this Agreement and fails to remedy such breach within fourteen (14) days of receipt of a notice in writing requiring such breach to be remedied; or

becomes insolvent or is made subject to an application for winding up or resolves to wind up voluntarily (other than for the purposes of a solvent reconstruction), or has a receiver (or similar functionary) or an administrator appointed in respect of any of its assets, or ceases to carry on business or enters into a composition or arrangement with its creditors generally; or

commits a breach of this Agreement or the Scheme, and such breach is deemed by the other party to involve fraud or dishonesty; or

there is a Change of Control in relation to the Default Party;

the other party may terminate this Agreement immediately by giving written notice of termination to the Defaulting Party.

Consequences of Termination

On termination of this Agreement, for whatever reason:

the Licence granted by CBS Group to Downer to use the CBS Group Tools is immediately terminated, and Downer must cease to use the CBS Group  Tools and as soon as reasonably practical return to CBS Group  or destroy any and all of the CBS Group  Tools;

the both parties must, as soon as reasonably practical, return to the other party or destroy any and all of any their Confidential Information and their Intellectual Property in its possession or control including any variation, modification or improvement; and

Upon termination of this Agreement:

Downer must not accept any new requests from Customers for it to provide them with any Partner Services under the terms of this Agreement; and

in respect of Partner Services scheduled at the time of termination but not yet provided (Scheduled Works), Downer must at CBS Group ’s election:

provide Downer Services to the Customer; or

assign the Scheduled Works to CBS Group, and CBS Group may arrange for an alternate service provider to provide the Scheduled Works to the Customer.

Termination of the Agreement shall not relieve a party of liability to the other party in respect of the rights or remedies of the other party which have accrued prior to termination.

Liabilities and Indemnities

Except as the terms of this Agreement specifically state, this Agreement does not include by implication any other term, condition or warranty in respect of the quality, merchantability, acceptability, fitness for purpose, condition, description or performance of the either parties services (CBS Group Services or Partner Services) offered to the other party or any contractual remedy for their failure.

Neither party is liable to the other party in any way arising under or in connection with the provision of the Services;

for any indirect or consequential losses or expenses suffered by either party or any third party, howsoever caused, including but not limited to loss of turnover, profits, business or goodwill or any liability to any other party.

Nothing in this Agreement is to be interpreted as excluding, restricting or modifying or having the effect of excluding, restricting or modifying the application of any State or Federal legislation applicable to the sale of goods or supply of services which cannot be excluded, restricted or modified.

Indemnity

Except as otherwise caused or contributed to by the other party, each party must indemnify and keep the other party indemnified from all claims, suits, demands, loss, damage, cost or liability in relation to or incidental to any of its acts or omissions under this Agreement, including but not limited to: 

any certificate surrenders, fine or penalty imposed on the indemnified party by reason of a negligent act or omission on the part of the indemnifying party.; 

Assignment

Downer must not assign, charge or deal with the Agreement without the prior written consent of CBS Group, which consent may be withheld in its absolute discretion.

CBS Group may assign its interests under this Agreement provided that any assignee must first covenant to be bound by CBS Group ’s rights and obligations hereunder.

Duration of Provisions

The covenants, conditions, provisions and warranties contained in this Agreement shall not merge or terminate upon completion of the transactions contemplated herein but to the extent that they have not been fulfilled and satisfied or are capable of having effect shall remain in full force and effect.

Parties to do all things necessary

The parties agree to do all things and execute all documents necessary to give effect to the terms of this Agreement.

Downer must cooperate in the execution of all documents necessary to complete any action to be taken under this Agreement, including in relation to termination.  

For the purposes of clause 21.2, if Downer is unable for any reason or if Downer fails, refuses or neglects to execute any such document upon the reasonable request of CBS Group , then Downer hereby appoints CBS Group as its irrevocable attorney in its name, and on its behalf, with the right to do any and all actions and things necessary to complete the action to be taken under this Agreement, including in relation to termination, and to execute and deliver all documents necessary or required to be completed.

Costs

Each party will bear its own legal and accounting costs incurred in respect of the preparation, negotiation and execution of this Agreement.

Severability

If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then it must be interpreted as narrowly as necessary to allow it to be enforceable or valid.

If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then:

the provision (or part of it) must be severed from this Agreement;

the remaining provisions (and remaining part of the provision) are valid and enforceable.

Victorian law and jurisdiction

The law of Victoria from time to time governs this Agreement.

The parties agree to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Victoria, the Federal Court of Australia, and of courts entitled to hear appeals from those courts.

Amending this Agreement

An amendment or change is only effective once it is:

made in writing; and

executed by all parties.

This is the entire Agreement

This is the entire Agreement of the parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement.

There is no other Agreement, understanding, warranty or representation, whether oral or written, binding the parties concerning any aspect of this Agreement.

No waiver

A party may exercise its rights at any time and does not waive those even if that party:

previously waived a breach or default of all or part of the same or other provision; or

delayed or omitted to exercise its rights.

A waiver is only effective:

if it is signed by the party granting the waiver; and

to the extent set out in the waiver.

Interpretation

When interpreting this Agreement:

headings are for convenience only and do not affect the interpretation of this Agreement;

the singular includes the plural and the plural includes the singular;

any gender includes any other gender;

any thing required to be done on a day that is not a Business Day, may be done on the next Business Day; 

reference to a person includes a natural person, corporation, partnership, association or other legal entity;

reference to legislation includes that legislation as amended or modified or re-enacted; and

reference to a provision or clause means a provision or clause in this Agreement.

Effective Date:   /   / 2020

The following list CBS Group and Downer agree to work exclusivity with each other in order to offer funding from Environment Certificates to the end.  CBS Group cannot pursue these opportunities with another partner nor direct with the end Customer and Downer cannot pursue these opportunities with another business offering Environment Certificate services.

ensure that each person engaged by Wattly would not, in doing the work for which they are engaged, be an Illegal Worker;

remove, or cause to be removed, any Illegal Worker from any involvement in carrying out the Services; and

Illegal Worker means a person who is an Unlawful Non-Citizen who is working without a visa, or a Non-Citizen who is performing work in breach of a Visa Work Condition;

Non-Citizen has the same meaning as under the Migration Act 1958 (Cth);

Unlawful Non-Citizen has the same meaning as under the Migration Act 1958 (Cth); and

Visa Work Condition means a condition (as set out in Schedule 8 of the Migration Regulations 1994 (Cth) attached to a visa restricting the work that the Non-Citizen may do in Australia.

bodily injury to any third party;

third party property damage; and

advertising liability

CBS Group must ensure that each policy required by this Agreement is in force prior to the Effective Date and maintained for the duration of the Agreement Term, except for professional indemnity insurance, which shall be maintained for 6 years after the conclusion of the Agreement Term.

## CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support Rev D(extract).docx

*File: `CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support Rev D(extract).docx`*

25 February 2020

Mr David Rees

T3, Triniti Business Campus, 

39 Delhi Road
North Ryde NSW 2113 

Dear Dave,

Re: Alternate Funding Mechanisms | Proposal for CBS Group Support

As we discussed last week, enclosed is an updated proposal for CBS Group to work with you to further qualify the potential for a range of alternate funding mechanisms to be used to support current operations and initiatives – initially focused upon the TfNSW Fleet Enhancement (HVAC Upgrade).  We have completed a review of the FE04 HVAC Energy Efficiency Report and updated the proposal accordingly.

Context

Situation

Downer has a number of innovations and improvements they want to propose to their clients for mutual benefit.   One recent example is the development of a suite of enhancements for the TfNSW Rolling Stock fleet that included improvements with measurable benefits in reduced maintenance, improved passenger experience & safety, and energy usage.  The HVAC Energy Efficiency initiative is a well-developed initiative with a great deal of pre-existing modelling already completed.

Complication

As is often the case, these innovation and improvement initiatives struggle to progress from concept to project execution.   Often this is due to challenges securing the up-front capital to fund the project, typically because;

the benefits are accrued by others – other departments, customers, the public, etc.

the benefits in operation expense cannot be accrued to create the required capital

Question

The focus upon CO2 emissions reductions has led to the establishment of a number of funding mechanisms designed to provide capex support for energy reduction initiatives.  Are we able to leverage these funding mechanisms to secure the upfront capital needed to mobilise the Sydney Fleet, HVAC Energy Efficiency inoitiative.   And if so, are we able to extend the learnings to determine what the boundaries are for other similar Downer initiatives.

Answer

We propose that Downer engage CBS Group to investigate the feasibility of these funding sources – using of the Fleet Enhancement HVAC Energy Efficiency Initiative (FE04) as the focus for the initial works, providing the basis for subsequent application of these mechanisms in other contexts.

Approach

We propose a 3 stage approach to the engagement as shown in the table below.

We look forward to the opportunity to progress this exciting initiative and would welcome an opportunity to meet and clarify anything outlined in this proposal.

Sincerely,

[Original Signed]

Jeff Dusting 

CBS Group Pty Ltd

Accepted on behalf of Downer

____________________

[Name / Signature / Date]



| Stage | Scope | Days Work Effort | Period | Location |

| 1 | Review the FE01 initiative and work with the Fleet Enhancement Project team to confirm the modelling that has been completed and the assumptions base for the FE04 initiative. | 1 | March 2020 | 1 Day in North Ryde Office |

| 2 | Complete analysis of the likelihood of successful funding against via the Federal Emissions Reduction Fund, Clean Energy Innovation Fund, NSW Energy Saving Scheme and via an Energy Performance Contract. | 2 | March 2020 | Offsite works |

| 3 | Develop presentation materials and support initial discussions with the State to determine the level of support for the approach (FE04)

Develop a paper / presentation that outlines the strategic opportunity for Downer to apply these funding mechanisms elsewhere in the business. | 3 | March 2020 | TBC |

| 5 | Present the outcomes and recommended next steps to Downer Leadership – as they relate to the Fleet Enhancement Program FE04, with recommendations regarding future initiatives. | 1 | April 2020 | TBC |

| 6 | Develop the applicable scheme documentation | [TBC] | April+ | TBC |

|  |  |  |  |  |

|  | Total | 7d | March – April 2020 | March – April 2020 |

## Carbon and Energy Saving Scheme Questions.docx

*File: `Carbon and Energy Saving Scheme Questions.docx`*

Carbon and Energy Saving Scheme Questions:

For ERF:

Broadly explain project, confirm it applies

Confirm whether the Chamber Tests are sufficient and the following details for submission:

Does the form of testing (climate models, static testing and dynamic/in-operation testing) satisfy the requirements?

What level of precision does the model need to be?

Are climate models and tests sufficient (if the model is sophisticated enough and validated)?

For ESS:

Broadly explain project, confirm eligibility

Confirm IHEAB energy savings calculation method is best suited

Confirm whether the Chamber Tests are sufficient and the following details for submission:

Does the form of testing (climate models, static testing and dynamic/in-operation testing) satisfy the requirements?

What level of precision does the model need to be?

Are climate models and tests sufficient (if the model is sophisticated enough and validated)?

For Downer:

What is the estimate of the peak period of the project or abatement? (required for ERF application)

Would we be able to install additional monitoring equipment? (reliant on response from ERF and ESS)

For the predicted energy savings, how does this compare to the overall power consumption of the site? It is a substantial decrease or small?

Is Downer or Sigma licensed to test these fans? Sufficient enough to meet F2015L01712 legislation for Carbon Credits or NSW legislation? *might be a question for ERF

For myself:

Peak period – what exactly do they mean?

More insight into the time period (if in the documentation)

Important notes:

If you utilise the Energy Savings Schemes, you are not eligible to claim any incentives under the Energy Reduction Fund and vice versa

For ERF:

Reverse auction format used to get a contract. Done through online bidding platform AusTender.

Three forms of contract which you can choose from at auction registration:

‘Standard’ contracts which has a delivery timeframe of 7 years (10 years for some).

‘Short-term’ contract which requires multiple Kyoto ACCU deliveries within a period of less than seven years. A project can have a maximum of three short-term contracts associated with it throughout the project’s crediting period and may cover part of the project’s abatement within the contracted period.

‘Immediate delivery’ contract in which the nominated ACCUS will be delivered within 10 days of auction. You can only bid for this contract if you have the required number of ACCUs already present in your account. You have a maximum of 10 immediate delivery contracts per project under the projects crediting period, and the minimum volume for immediate delivery contracts is 10% of the forward abatement estimate of the project or 15,000 ACCUs (whichever is lower).

ACCUs are considered ‘delivered’ under carbon abatement contract when they have been transferred from your ANREU account into the Clean Energy Regulator’s ANREU delivery account. To receive payment, an invoice must be sent to the Clean Energy Regulator

Payment is made to the bank account for your contract within 20 business days from our receipt of your invoice and the units have been delivered to the Clean Energy Regulators account.

From the above information, it appears funding cannot be upfront, and the ACCUs must be accredited and delivered before any form of payment. However, by utilising the immediate delivery contract (or short-term) you could increase the speed of payment – instead of receiving a lump sum at the end of the project accreditation period, you could cash in ACCUs periodically as the project progresses.

For ESS:

The energy saver for the point of the IHEAB ESS method can be either, the original energy saver which is the purchaser, or the nominated energy saver which is someone the original energy saver has nominated as the energy saver by completing a Nomination Form.

There are 3 main methods:

‘Deemed Energy Savings Methods’ which uses default factors and equations to calculate energy savings and includes the proposed IHEAB method. 

‘Metered Baseline Methods’ which calculates energy savings based on actual measured energy consumption before and after an activity has been implemented.

‘Project Impact Assessment with Measurement and Verification Method’ in which energy savings are calculated using a ‘measurement and verification’ approach which is ‘signed off’ by a measurement and verification professional (usually a third party). Energy saving certificates can be forward created for up to 10 years or created annually for this method.

Test reports for products you want to fit (to save energy) need to be submitted to IPART from an accredited laboratory or equivalent.

Three types of contracts; spot contract, forward contract, option contract (may need to hold an Australian Financial Services License when dealing with some contracts). These contracts are determined between an Accredited Certificate Provider and a buyer and there are currently no standard contracts or a recognised exchange for trading. For further information, refer: https://www.ess.nsw.gov.au/Home/About-ESS/Overview-of-the-ESS/Energy-Savings-Certificates

Audits will cost the company, and should be detracted from scheme potential earnings estimates along with non-refundable $2,500 application fee

## Commercial Scope Rev A.docx

*File: `Commercial Scope Rev A.docx`*

Scope

The diagram below provides a high level overview of the process activities associated with the generation of Energy Saving Certificates for energy saving modifications to the Sydney Trains fleet.  

For avoidance of doubt, with exception for the scope assigned to Downer Group listed below, all works and costs are the responsibility of CBS Group.

CBS Group is responsible for all works associated with the generation of Energy Saving Certificates for the modifications, including:

Development of energy saving models

Management of data, process and records in accordance with the requirements of the scheme

Appointment and fees associated audits, certificates, application and transactions.

Management, communication (reporting) and administration to Downer Group in accordance with an agreed project management plan.

Downer Group is responsible for the following:

Provision of train energy usage data as available from the train management system for all sets pre and post modification

Provision of modification documentation as needed to verify the nature of the modification, the impact and verify implementation.

Development of energy saving models

Management of data, process and records in accordance with the requirements of the scheme

### Slide 1
Alternate Revenue Sources
Downer Quarterly Business Update | October 2020
Commercial in Confidence – All Rights Reserved

### Slide 2
Alternate Revenue Sources
Innovation funding is problematic for our clients – benefits have an uncertain and long tail while requiring an upfront capex spend.

We worked with CBS Group to explore alternate revenue sources that to reduce the capex burden of our proposed innovations.

Amongst a range of possible revenue sources, accessing state and federal energy and climate change funding were assessed and found to be attractive.
Commercial in Confidence – All Rights Reserved

### Slide 3
Demonstration Project | NSW Fleet HVAC Upgrade
Background | Detailed analysis of the energy savings, and application of 15+ energy schemes to the proposed HVAC efficiency upgrades (Sydney Trains Fleet)

Key Finding | Downer is eligible to earn $7m+ in the sale of energy saving certificates arising from the planned software and hardware upgrades
Independent of TfNSW funding / the cost of the upgrades
Value varies (up / down) principally with actual verified energy savings, and the certificate sale price (open market)

ActionShared Value to Mitigate Downer Risk
Model development, verification, audit and program risk is taken by CBS
Revenue upside is shared
Key Details

Schedule
Works commence Dec 20
First funds received Mar 22
Works complete 25 (varies with TfNSW mod approval)

The numbers
$ Expected: $7m
$ Upside: $18m
$ @ Risk (Fees): $200k

FAQ
Scheme funds ‘energy saver’ – Downer in this case
First instance of scheme applied to HVAC in ‘mobile plant’

Commercial in Confidence – All Rights Reserved

Advance: $50k initiation Fee, plus $10k per month advance 18 months
Fixed and capped fee of 30% of certificate proceeds to fund: 
Rebate of advance fees ($230k) paid by Downer, ESC Partner and certificate issuer, mobile regression model development, engineering, audit fees, certificate registration fees, compliance with government processes, project management, delivery, certificate hedging and risk.

Commercial in Confidence – All Rights Reserved

| RFI | Notes/Comments | IPART documentary evidence requirement/s | Downer response |

| Train data set for selected signals on SGT B1 – or selected train, for the period March 2019 to March 2021 | Request Train DNA initial data for a single train as follows: 
·         Setpoint 
·         Lat/long 
·         Run# 
·         Date / Time of day 
·         Set, Car and HVAC unit identifier 
·         HVAC Mode 
·         Cabin temp and RH 
·         Door state 
·         Energy consumption per HVAC unit 
·         Passenger loan (kg) 
Noting that Chris H indicated that data spanning the Oct 2019 format change is accessible, if possible it is preferable to get the data set for the period March 2019 - March 2021. 
 
With regards to the energy consumption, can you confirm the units of measure available?  Is it True RMS power in kWh?   Or do we have access to Volts, Amps, and power factor? | IPART documentary evidence requires a geotagged Screenshot of the database query for all data extracted. | ·         Setpoint - Available
·         Lat/long - Available
·         Run# - Available
·         Date / Time of day - Available
·         Set, Car and HVAC unit identifier - Set number available, 
car number can be inferred from the signal ID
·         HVAC Mode - Available
·         Cabin temp and RH - Available
·         Door state - Available
·         Energy consumption per HVAC unit – Energy consumption available
 at a resolution down to the total of half one Train Set when you add the
 energy recorded the 4 meters on each half, refer to the attached 
drawing for an outline indicating how the power meters are wired.
·         Passenger loan (kg) - Available
 
Attached is train data from A80 for all of 2020 with a list of signal 
descriptions. I’ve also attached a smaller sample data set from B4 for 
information noting difference in signals available.
 
While data prior to Oct 2019 is available, the software currently used 
to query this data is not capable of collating larger quantities of data 
required and the database does not contain all the signals requested.
 
We are working on getting a script running that is capable of 
querying the database with data prior Oct 2019 but this may
 take some time.
 
Units are provided with the signal descriptions.
 I’ve attached the power meter data sheet indicating that it uses true rms.
 Voltage, current and power factor is available only on the B-sets.
 I have not included these since they are instantaneous measurements 
which fluctuate during the 10-minute measurement periods.
 
I’ve attached a geo tagged screenshot the script running, 
though I’m not sure how much value this gives. 
Unfortunately, the signal queries and other parameters are set 
throughout a series of scripts which would 
be difficult to capture in a screenshot. 
The python scripts would contain the details 
but I’m not sure how this could be geotagged. |

| Historical Annual HVAC energy use data 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019 if available for SGT B1 – or selected train |  | IPART documentary evidence requires a Screenshot of the database query | As mentioned above, we will need more time to put this together. |

| Request Maintenance Plan and/or procedures, including calibration tolerances and intervals for thermal couplings |  |  | We can provide this in a following email. |

    Dimensions:
    International Flights (excluding North and South America):
    Total dimensions for each piece must not exceed 158cm (62in). Dimensions for checked baggage are calculated by adding
    together the width, height and depth of the piece of baggage.
    International Flights to and from North & South America:
    Total dimensions of the 3 pieces must not exceed 405cm (159in) and total dimensions of the 2 pieces must not exceed
    270cm (106in) with no single piece exceeding 158cm (62in). Dimensions for checked baggage are calculated by adding together
    the width, height and depth of the piece of baggage.
    Domestic Flights:
    Total dimensions of each piece must not exceed 140cm (55in). Dimensions for checked baggage are calculated by adding
    together the width, height and depth of the piece of baggage.
    +Adults travelling with an infant (under 2 years) on Qantas operated services may check-in up to three infant items

    Interline travel: Qantas baggage allowance may not apply. If your travel on this ticket involves more than one
    airline, the baggage allowance of the most significant carrier in your itinerary will apply to each journey, except in
    relation to travel to, from or within the USA. Under US DOT regulations, if your travel to, from or within the USA
    involves more than one airline, the baggage rules of one airline will apply to all of the flights in your itinerary. This is
    determined by the first carrier in your itinerary. For travel between Australia and the USA, usually (both not always)
    Qantas' baggage allowance will apply to the whole itinerary.

    Member Allowances: Qantas Frequent Flyer and Qantas Club member baggage allowances apply to travel on
    Qantas and QantasLink operated flights with a QF flight number on your ticket, excluding flights to or from Lord Howe
    Island and Mount Hotham. These allowances also apply to Emirates operated flights with an EK flight number
    between and within Europe, the Middle East, North Africa, Asia and Australia. These benefits are not cumulative.

    Definitions: Domestic travel is travel that is not combined with any international flights on the same ticket.
    International travel is travel between two or more countries including any flight within those countries if it is combined
    with the international travel.

    General: No single item of checked baggage may exceed 32kg (70lb). Carriage of baggage is subject to space availability and
    any applicable aircraft weight restrictions, which vary. Baggage exceeding the specified allowance may need to be
    accommodated on a later flight.

.                                                                                                                 Key Conditions of Carriage
Carriage on Qantas or QantasLink is subject to our full Conditions of Carriage at qantas.com/carriage.
Some key conditions:
Travel Documents: It is your responsibility to comply with the requirements of the country to which you travel (for example, passports and
visas). Your contact details (for example, destination accommodation arrangements), and your fingerprints and/or photograph may be
required before entry into a foreign country.
Check-in: Deadlines apply and you may be refused carriage if you are late. View Qantas' check-in times at qantas.com/checkintimes.
Oversale - Denied Boarding: If you are denied boarding because your carrier has oversold an international flight on which you are booked, you may be
entitled to compensation in accordance with applicable regulations (for example, in the EU or USA), or carrier's policy. When required by applicable law or
regulation, the carrier must solicit volunteers before anyone is denied boarding involuntarily. For Qantas' policy - ask at our international check-in counters.
Insurance: Travel insurance is recommended.
Liability Limits: If your journey involves a stop in a country different from that of departure, then the Montreal Convention or the Warsaw Convention may
govern and limit the liability of all airlines for death or bodily injury and in respect of loss of or damage to baggage.

Approximate conversions of Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) are provided as a guide only and will be subject to change with currency conversion rates.


    International                                         Warsaw (limits are per person)                         Montreal (limits are per person)
    Death and bodily injury                                Qantas has waived its liability limits                No financial limit
    Checked baggage                                        250 francs/kg (approx. A$30/US$25)                    Special Drawing Rights (SDRs)
    Carry-on baggage (carrier fault)                       5,000 francs max. (approx. A$500/US$400)              1,131 SDRs (approx AU$1,950/US$1,800)

Delay: For damage occasioned by delay to your journey, where the Montreal Convention applies, the limit of liability is 4,694 SDRs (approx
AU$8,160/US$7,500) per passenger in most cases. Where the Warsaw Convention applies, the carrier may be liable for damage occasioned by delay, subject
to the Convention limit.
Defences: The Conventions provide certain defences to liability on which we may rely in some circumstances. For further information read the Liability section
at qantas.com/carriage.


    Domestic (no international sectors on itinerary) Australia (limits are per person)                           New Zealand (limits are per person)
    Death and bodily injury                                A$725,000                                             Refer to ^

    Checked baggage                                        A$1,600                                               NZ$1,500 per unit of baggage
    Carry-on baggage                                       A$160                                                 No liability except to extent our fault.

                                                           Your rights are limited by Qantas' Conditions of Carriage subject, in New Zealand, to rights under the
    Delay
                                                           Civil Aviation Act 1990.

^Claims in New Zealand courts for damages arising directly or indirectly out of bodily injury or death of a passenger may be barred in accordance with the
Injury Prevention, Rehabilitation, and Compensation Act (NZ) 2001 and we are not liable for loss or damage to carry-on baggage subject to your rights under
the Consumer Guarantees Act 1993.
Baggage General: Comply with your carrier's baggage allowances and do not include fragile or perishable articles, precious metals, jewellery, money, rare
items, business papers or other important documents or valuables (including cameras and electronic equipment) in your checked baggage. For Qantas'
baggage allowances visit qantas.com/baggage.
Delays and Cancellation: If travelling on Qantas, we will use all reasonable efforts to depart on time, but we do not guarantee flight times. If your flight is
delayed or cancelled, you may in some circumstances be entitled to assistance and/or compensation depending on your journey and applicable law.
Seating: Qantas does not guarantee you any particular seat even if your reservation is confirmed.
Specific Assistance: Qantas requires advance notice for some accommodations that passengers with disabilities may need, and passengers with disabilities
may need to check in earlier than other passengers. For details, visit qantas.com/fitness.
Taxes, Fees and Carrier Charges: The charges, surcharges and taxes included in your fare or shown separately on your ticket may not be levied by a
government authority but may be airport operator or carrier imposed. Details can be provided by your travel consultant.
If you've been charged a UK APD fee for passengers aged 12 - 15 travelling after 1 March 2016, you can obtain a refund of that amount.
Other Carriers/Non-Airline Transport: Except where you are travelling on a "QF" code, if Qantas issues a ticket or itinerary/receipt or checks baggage for
carriage on another carrier, it does so only as agent for the other carrier and their conditions of carriage will apply to those services. The air carrier's
conditions of carriage do not apply to any non-airline travel included in your booking. The operator's conditions of carriage may significantly limit or exclude
liability. Ask your travel consultant for details.
Time Limit for Action: Any action in court to claim damages relating to your carriage must be brought within two years from the date of arrival of the aircraft
or from the date on which the aircraft ought to have arrived.
Baggage Claims: There are time limits within which a claim must be made in writing to your carrier in circumstances of loss, damage or delay of your
baggage. Some limits are as short as three days. Check with your carrier's Baggage Claims.
Privacy: Our privacy statement is available at qantas.com/privacy.
Dangerous Goods: For safety reasons, dangerous articles must not be packed in checked or carry-on baggage. Prohibited articles include but are not limited
to: compressed gases, corrosives, explosives, flammable liquids and solids, radioactive materials, oxidising materials, poisons, infectious substances, and
briefcases with installed alarm devices. For the latest details on dangerous goods, visit qantas.com/dangerousgoods




    Qantas Airways Limited ABN 16 009 661 901, 10 Bourke Road, Mascot NSW 2020, Australia
    qantas.com

## ERF timeframe.docx

*File: `ERF timeframe.docx`*

Overarching ERF timeframe:

Step 1: Apply

Provide information about you

Provide a forward abatement estimate - rounded to nearest 50,000 tCO2-e for the crediting period (7 years for our project)

provide fit and proper person status

Open an ANREU account

Get eligible interest holder consent

Since we are claiming over 100,000 tCO2-e in a single report, might be subject to a variance/triggered audit

	Clean Energy regulator has 90 days to process project registrations, variations and crediting applications. Average processing timeframe is 6 weeks/30 business days

Step 2: Establishing a contract

Two types of contracts:

Fixed delivery contracts: agreeing to a set number of ACCUs at a set price for the duration of the contract. Schedule the ACCUs to be delivered across the duration of the contract. Contract starts when Clean Energy Regulator notifies you that you had a successful bid at auction and ends when the final delivery of ACCUs has occurred and all payments have been made

	Each date in schedule should allow for audits, reporting, etc.

	If you cannot delivery the agreed quantity for a fixed delivery contract, you are required to source the different from other projects you run or from the secondary market.

Option to delivery contract: 

provides the right, but not the obligation, to sell carbon abatement to the Commonwealth at an agreed price, within a set time. Allows holders to better manage their price and supply risks to encourage more abatement projects.

Option contracts may be used in negotiations with lenders or other parties to demonstrate what the Commonwealth will pay for abatement from a specified project, with no contractual barrier to seeking more lucrative contracts from other buyers.

Flexible duration of up to 10 years. Scheduled milestones that are not delivered against will lapse.

Could have both fixed and option contract at some time as long as combined contract does not exceed what could be generated by the project.

Parts:

Auction qualification

Before bidding in an auction must first qualify to participate.

Auction qualification forms must be completed through client portal at least 20 days prior to an auction. If approved, auction qualification is valid for 12 months (as long as no information changes).

Estimating abatement quantity

All Emissions Reduction Fund projects must provide a forward abatement estimate (FAE) in tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent (tCO2-e) at project registration

For larger projects (1,000,000 ACCUs +), should consider contacting the Clean Energy Regulator to discuss your project and types of evidence required in advance of the auction qualification deadline

The IAQ is indicative only and can be revised at auction registration when you provide the agreed quantity (the amount you agree to provide).

The agreed quantity (AQ) is the quantity of Kyoto ACCUs to be delivered over the duration of the contract and is provided to the Clean Energy Regulator at auction registration

Evidence required to provide at auction qualification is forecast modelling (for our project type) 

There are immediate delivery contract but must already have ACCUs in account

Forecast modelling:

Modelling that shows assumptions, inputs and forecasts demonstrating how abatement volumes are calculated. This may include outputs from calculators such as  FullCAM.

A detailed statement outlining assumptions and reasons for the quantity submitted.

Plans that describe the project and how it will be implemented.

Expert advice or partnerships that will support the project’s implementation.

Other information relevant to the project.

Auction registration

Commits you to delivery terms, number of ACCUs you will deliver and contract terms

When planning delivery schedule should consider: time it will take to set up project and start generating abatement, time needed to allow for auditing and reporting on your project, process to apply for ACCUs, allowing up to 3 months for units to be issued by the Clean Energy Regulator, how many deliveries of Kyoto Accus must be possible under a carbon abatement contract

Any changes to contract schedule after auction must be negotiated with Clean Energy Regulator

Length of delivery period varies due to conditions set out in contract. If contract does not contain any conditions precedent, then delivery period begins 10 business days after the final day of the auction

*there a calculator you can use

Bid at auction!

Via online portal

Contract lengths:

Standard contract: he most common standard contract length is seven years. Contracts associated with projects that have a crediting period of 10 years or more can apply for a standard contract length of 10 years

Short-term contract: requires multiple Kyoto ACCU deliveries within a period of less than seven years. Contracts associated with projects that have a crediting period of 10 years or more can apply for a short-term contract of less than 10 years.

A project can have a maximum of three short-term contracts associated with it throughout the project’s crediting period. A short-term contract may cover part of the project’s abatement within the contracted period.

Immediate delivery contract: where the nominated Kyoto ACCUs will be delivered within 10 days of the auction. You can only bid for an immediate delivery contract if you have the required number of Kyoto ACCUs in your Australian national register of emissions units account.

You can have a maximum of 10 immediate delivery contracts per project over the project’s crediting period.

The minimum volume for immediate delivery contracts is at least 10 per cent of the forward abatement estimate for the project supplying the Kyoto ACCUs or 15,000 Kyoto ACCUs (whichever is lower).

Reporting and auditing

Reports:

All participants must report on their projects at regular intervals

Reports may be accompanied by an audit report

Under the Emissions Reduction Fund, you may choose when to report on your project as long as you adhere to the minimum and maximum reporting periods (periods between reports). 

The first reporting period begins at the start of your project’s crediting period.  Each new reporting period begins immediately after the previous reporting period. All reporting periods must be within your project’s crediting period. 

In general, a reporting period can be as short as six months, as long as two years for emissions avoidance projects

Project report will be processed within 90 days

Audits:

Audits must be prepared by a registered category 2 greenhouse and energy auditor. Must be independent to prevent conflict of interest.

Minimum of 3 scheduled audits across 7-year crediting period

All audits will need to establish reasonable assurance that the abatement achieved and reported on is accurate.

Costs of audits are on project… - best to engage auditor early so can factor in pricing into bidding

Recommend choose different auditors (at least two) from different firms over the lifespan of the project. Clean Energy Regulator will have more faith in the project if this is adhered to, and project is less likely to be selected for additional audit if they meet this requirement.

Audit type frequency (info into what each type requires is on site):

Initial audits: must be submitted with first report for your project, 6 months to 2 years into crediting period.

Subsequent audits: must be submitted in line with the project audit schedule received at time of project registration. Intended to ensure your project is audited across periods of peak abatement, providing assurance over the maximum number of ACCUs issued across a crediting period. These audits will cover a minimum reporting period of 12 months.

Unscheduled additional audits: 3 triggers for these audits include a single report claiming over 100,000 tCO2-e abatement, + other reasons…

Clean energy regulator will set an audit schedule for your project and is provided at time of project registration. Sets out level of assurance, frequency, and scope of audits required for project.

For most projects, minimum of 3 scheduled audits across the 7 year period is required. Number will depend on project size in terms of average annual abatement expected to be generated (based on forward abatement estimate)

Applying for ACCUs (Claiming ACCUs)

Once project has generated carbon abatement can apply

Apply through Client Portal using the Emissions Reduction Fund Project Report and Crediting form

Will need to provide:

Eligible offsets report covering the reporting period

A signed application for ACCUs

An audit report (if required by your audit schedule)

Method specific supporting documentation

Clean energy regulator has up to 90 days from receipt of a complete and accurate application to process crediting applications

Once application for ACCUs has been approved, the ACCUS will be deposited in your ANREU account. 

Can either sell these on secondary market or be used to meet contractual obligations

Delivering units and receiving payment

Transfer ACCUs to the Clean Energy Regulator’s ANREU account, to receive payment send an invoice. Payment will be received within 20 business days from receipt of invoice provided it is complete and accurate

ANREU stands for Australian National Registry of Emissions Units

## Emissions Reduction Fund and NSW Energy Saving Scheme.docx

*File: `Emissions Reduction Fund and NSW Energy Saving Scheme.docx`*

Emissions Reduction Fund and NSW Energy Saving Scheme

Index

Figure 1. Comparing spot price and average Emissions Reduction Fund auction prices

Source: http://www.cleanenergyregulator.gov.au/Infohub/Markets/buying-accus/australian-carbon-credit-unit-market-updates/statement-of-opportunities-in-the-accu-market-–-march-2019 

*note ACCU spot prices on the secondary market are now published on private websites, at $15.35 as of 1 March 2019

Figure 2. Emissions Reduction Fund Schematic

Source: http://www.cleanenergyregulator.gov.au/ERF/About-the-Emissions-Reduction-Fund/emissions-reduction-fund-schematic 

Figure 6. ESS Scheme Pricing Calculation

Predicted energy savings of the SGT/Waratah Fleet per year=16.5-26.6 GWh.

Equates to 16500-26600 MWh.

Could claim 16500 to 26600 ESCs.

Latest ESC price (see figure 7.) values 1 x ESC at $25.70 as of 4/3/20.

Estimated return depending on time of sale = $424,050 to $683,620.

Figure 7. Latest ESC price

Source: https://www.demandmanager.com.au/certificate-prices/) 

Value 1 x ESC at $25.70

Overview:

From the FE04 HVAC Energy Efficiency Report there are two main advantages to the climate system in terms of savings; reduction of carbon emission and reduction of energy consumption. These four initiatives cater towards these advantages and together, allow Downer to maximise the reward for implementing these changes in the Waratah fleet.

The FED Emissions Reduction Fund rewards businesses for a reduction in CO2 emission by awarding an Australian Carbon Credit Unit (ACCU) per tonne of Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (tCO2-e) stored or avoided by the project. These Carbon Credits can then be sold to the government via a carbon abatement contract or sold on the secondary market. Via this method, an additional revenue stream of $166,320 to $268,128 could be added as a result of the HVAC upgrade. These prices are indicative and can vary depending on the demand set by the government via a carbon abatement contract, or the supply and demand in the secondary market.

A carbon abatement contract is not required to participate in the Emissions Reduction Fund. ACCUs can be issued to a project without one and sold on the market. Alternatively, a contract can be secured later via participation in an online auction.

This scheme is advantageous as it provides quantitative methods of calculating the additional revenue stream while offering multiple markets and potentially prices for these ACCUs to be sold. This initiative does not provide any form of upfront funding.

It should be noted that eligibility for this fund relies on three main requirements: the project does not commence before it has been registered with the Clean Energy Regulator and a response has been received in writing; the project is not required to be undertaken by or under a Commonwealth, State, or Territory Law; the project is not being undertaken under another Commonwealth, State or Territory government program in the absence of registration under the Emissions Reduction Fund. A Forward Abatement Estimate covering the crediting period for the project is required to secure a carbon abatement contract.

A Crediting period is the timeframe a project is able to claim ACCUs and is 7 years for FE04 HVAC project type. The Reporting period is the timeframe for which a report is prepared for submission to the Clean Energy Regulator and is at discretion of the individual responsible for the project. Maximum reporting period for emissions avoidance projects is every 2 years.

With these (and several other) requirements satisfied, the project outlined in the FE04 HVAC Energy Efficiency Report is suitable for this form of funding and should be applied for as ‘reductions in emission intensity of transport is a valid project type.

NSW Energy Saving Scheme awards financial incentives for organisations investing in energy saving projects that achieve reduced energy consumption by installing, improving, or replacing energy savings equipment. An Accredited Certificate Provider offers energy saving certificates (currency in the scheme) for each notional MWh of energy that investments save which can be sold back to electricity retailers, scheme participants and other intermediary agents.

There are multiple calculation methods that are eligible to determine the about of MWh saved and the method used in the FE04 HVAC Energy Efficiency Report will need to be verified as being an eligible calculation method or modified to utilise on of the eligible methods. Each MWh saved is valued at one ESC to be sold.

Organisations register for this scheme via the ESS Registry, an online database which records the registration, transfer, and surrender of energy savings certificates.

*penalty must be paid if don’t surrender certificates by time

My notes:

Assumptions Downer Made:

Will test changes by installing on an SGT

Energy saving benefits:

Design condition was at an ambient temperature of 35 degrees Celsius

Simulations in typical operation conditions to predict energy saving benefits

Key assumptions to predict fleet energy savings:

Model is based on average climatic data for the Sydney region and does not consider variations between the city and western suburbs

The model down not include pull-down, it is assumed that the train is at the correct operating temperature at the start of the device

Passenger loads are from existing operational data but in practice will vary between trains. 

Not included in the modelling, the predicted savings additional factors have been identified including:

HVAC operations during stabling or when the doors are open at terminal stops

Trains operating in all network sectors where ambient temperatures vary

Peak and off-peak conditions operating in tunnels and above ground

Condenser coils efficiency will change in partial cooling operation

CO2e savings:

Does not specify assumptions made

Downer say they predicted energy savings can be proved quantitatively

To mitigate the technical risk of energy savings not realised on EC fans:

Condenser fans and supply air fans power consumption monitored during climate chamber testing

Train trials on two cars over extended period

To mitigate the technical risk of energy savings not realised on condenser coil:

Confirm compressor power consumption during climate chamber testing

Train trials on two cars over extended period

Design phase overview (bottom part):

Testing and trials:

Climate chamber testing

Climate chamber testing is planned to validate the proposed power reduction

An existing HVAC unit will be upgraded by Sigma and then tested in a climate chamber

Following parameters will be verified in the type test:

Determination of refrigerant charge

Performance compared to the baseline

Power consumption

Testing and comparison of units (previous and upgraded) will be done and provide evidence for similarity of the HVAC performance on the train

Extensive performance testing on the train will not be required

Static train trials:

Upgrades will be made to a further 3 existing HVAC units and together with the first prototype installed across one motor and one trailer SGT cars

These static trials will validate the operation of the HEE HVAC units for installation on the train and before the train enters service

The single train will have HEE HVAC units and baseline units installed for direct comparison

Operational train trials (In-service)

Following static trials

In service trails will validate HVAC power consumption and ongoing operation in actual operational scenarios

Data collected from the HEE HVAC and baseline HVAC installed on the same train will enable a direct comparison of the results since the single train will experience the same environmental variables

Analysis of on-train power data will be provided by Downer’s own trainDNA software and additional data logging hardware installed on monitored HVACs. Data collected includes:

HVAC unit status

Power consumption

Operation trails over a 6 month period will run including one transitional season and one summer period

The operation trial HVAC will operate in all operational settings (peak/off-peak; running/terminal/stabling;20-24/7) and across a wide range of environmental conditions (city/suburuban temperatures; tunnels/above ground)

Additional performance monitoring equipment may be installed to support the trial

*test instructions in table 11

### Sheet: About

SIMPLE GANTT CHART by Vertex42.com | 

https://www.vertex42.com/ExcelTemplates/simple-gantt-chart.html | 

About this template | 

This template provides a simple way to create a Gantt chart to help visualise and track your project. Simply enter your tasks and start and end dates – no formulas are required. The bars in the Gantt chart represent the duration of the tasks and are displayed using conditional formatting. Insert new tasks by inserting new rows. | 

Guide for screen readers | 

There are 2 worksheets in this workbook. 

ProjectSchedule
About

The instructions for each worksheet are in the A column starting in cell A1 of each worksheet. They are written with hidden text. Each step guides you to the information in that row. Each subsequent step continues in cell A2, A3 and so on, unless otherwise explicitly directed. For example, instruction text might say “continue to cell A6” for the next step. 

This hidden text will not print.

To remove these instructions from the worksheet, simply delete column A. | 

Additional help | 

Click on the link below to visit vertex42.com and learn more about how to use this template, such as how to calculate days and workdays, create task dependencies, change the colours of the bars, add a scroll bar to make it easier to change the display week, extend the date range displayed in the chart etc. | 

How to use the simple Gantt chart | 

More project management templates | 

Visit Vertex42.com to download other project management templates, including different types of project schedules, Gantt charts, tasks lists etc. | 

Project management templates | 

About Vertex42 | 

Vertex42.com provides over 300 professionally designed spreadsheet templates for business, home and education – most of which are free to download. Their collection includes a variety of calendars, planners and schedules as well as personal finance spreadsheets for budgeting, debt reduction and loan amortisation. | 

Businesses will find invoices, timesheets, inventory trackers, financial statements and project planning templates. Teachers and students will find resources such as timetables, mark books and registers. Organise your family life with meal planners, checklists and exercise logs. Each template is thoroughly researched, refined and improved over time through feedback from thousands of users. |
