  Possible Solution


El. Eq. = .00067 (6) (15 x15)

  = 0.90 inch call it ~ 1.0 inch

For speeds entering siding between
10-15 MPH, an elevation of 1.0 inch
would be more appropriate.



                                      4
     Vehicle Dynamics Models
• Generally used to model one vehicle operating
  over a section of track (1000 ft. typical)
• Can simulate multiple types of car defects or
  wear
• Can simulate multiple types of rail geometry
  perturbations
• Can simulate at any speed
• Can predict wheelset lateral, vertical forces and
  L/V ratios (At a minimum)
• Generally called MBS (Multi‐Body Simulation)
  models

                                                      42
   Leading MBS Simulation Models
• VAMPIRE
   – Developed by British Rail Starting in 1970s
   – Now managed by Delta Rail of Derby England
• NUCARSTM
   – Developed by AAR/TTCI in mid 1980’s
   – First release 1987, many revisions since
• SIMPACK
   – Developed in Germany as MBS package at German
     Aerospace Research (DLR)
   – In 1995 first release with rail version; Siemens involved
     in effort
   – Claims to do vehicle dynamics and train dynamics

                                                                 43
Leading MBS Simulation Models
•   ADAMS Rail
     – Started with MBS software MSC.ADAMS as platform
     – In 1993 Dutch Rail began effort to customize for rail applications
     – In 1996, MEDYNAs development team joined up with MSC.ADAMS/RAIL
     – Now Marketed by MSC Software
•   Universal Mechanism (UM)
     – Developed as MBS open platform by Laboratory of Computational
        Mechanics
        Bryansk State Technical University, Russia
     – Has Rail capabilities, claims to do vehicle and train dynamics
•   GENSYS
     – Started in Sweden in 1980’s with ASEA
     – In 1992 full MBS version released for rail vehicles




                                                                        44
    University of Manchester
          Benchmark
• Completed ~1998
• Compared NUCARS, VAMPIRE, ADAMS
  RAIL, GENSYS, SIMPACK
• In general, all models were in close
  agreement on predicting wheel/rail forces
• NUCARS and VAMPIRE had fastest run
  times

                                                                                          Distance along the Track (feet)
           Case 2.
Derailment of Locomotive with
Asymmetrical Wheel Wear on
       switch point rail
                          Background

•   The train was operating at 28 mph in dynamic brake #3 at the time of
    the derailment.
•   Locomotive was SD90MAC equipped with HTCR (radial steering)
    trucks.
•   The wheels of the #4 axle revealed asymmetric flange wear. L4 is 3
    tape sizes smaller (~3mm, 0.118‐in) than R4. The L4 flange wear is
    greater than the R4.
•   L4 does not "take the gauge" for thin flange.
•   Track observations showed joints in both running rails with vertical
    deflection (pumping) 5‐ft ahead of the POD at the points.
•   Gauge face wear and head‐crushing were also evident in the 5‐ft.
    ahead of the switch.
•   The L4 wheel of Locomotive, climbed the point end of the point rail of
    a crossover switch. The switch was lined for the diverging route from
    Main #2 to Main #1.
               Wheel Profiles
                                                           L4 Wheel Profile -
                                                           significant flange wear -
                                                           approx. 79º flange angle




                         R4 Wheel Profile - almost
                         no flange wear - approx.
                         74º maximum flange angle


These profiles confirm that Axle 4 has been “crowding” consistently
toward the Left side, causing asymmetric wear to the wheel flanges

                   Conclusions
• Simulation modeling is mature and well validated
• Simulation is more cost effective than physical testing
• Simulation is excellent tool for design and analysis
• Simulation modeling is well suited to help solve a variety
  of wheel/rail interaction issues
• Simulation is only a tool; there is as much art as there is
  science in mastering simulation analysis
• Don’t let simulation ever supplant common sense and
  experience

## The NSW Energy Savings Scheme.docx

*File: `The NSW Energy Savings Scheme.docx`*

The NSW Energy Savings Scheme

The NSW Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) seeks to reduce energy consumption in NSW by creating financial incentives for organisations to invest in energy saving projects. Energy savings are achieved by installing, improving or replacing energy savings equipment.

The Energy Savings Scheme is governed by NSW legislation. It places a mandatory obligation on Scheme Participants to obtain and surrender energy savings certificates, which represent energy savings.  The policy framework is the responsibility of the Department of Planning, Industry and Environment (DPIE). The Energy Savings Scheme is legislated to run until 2025 or until there is an equivalent national energy efficiency scheme. 

The ESS Rule

The Energy Savings Scheme Rule of 2009 or the ESS Rule sets out how energy saving certificates can be created and prescribes:

•	Types of eligible activities

•	Types of ineligible activities

•	Eligible applicants

•	Detailed calculation methodologies.

The Rule is amended from time to time. Rule changes occur to keep the Scheme up to date and to manage changes in the market and the emergence of new technologies.

However, the broad requirement is that an activity must reduce energy consumption without reducing production levels or quality of service.  Specifically, eligibility can be defined as either:

•	Modifying End-User Equipment

•	Modifying usage of End-User Equipment

•	Replacing End-User Equipment

•	Installing new End-User Equipment

•	Removing End-User Equipment.

Key Roles within the ESS

Key Roles within the ESS include 

IPART as the Scheme administrator and regulator, 

Original or Nominated Energy Savers: the owner or controller of the energy savings

Accredited Certificate Providers (ACPs) who can create and register Energy Savings Certificates.  

Scheme Participants purchase ESCs and surrender them to the Scheme Regulator to meet obligations they have under the Scheme

And the Audit Services Panel who perform some of the Scheme Administrators statutory functions under The Electricity Supply (General) Regulation 2014 (s55 and s56) to ensure the ongoing compliance of Scheme Participants and Accredited Certificate Providers. 

Audits are regularly conducted to protect the integrity of the scheme by verifying that certificates represent real energy savings.

Energy savings certificates (ESC) and Pricing

An energy savings certificate (ESC) is a tradeable certificate created under Division 7 of Part 9 of the Electricity Supply Act 1995. Each ESC represents one notional megawatt hour (MWh) of energy and acts as a tradeable certificate created under the Energy Savings Scheme. 

The ESC price varies depending on market conditions. There is no maximum price for an ESC. However, the penalty price acts as a practical maximum price. If a Scheme Participant does not surrender the required number of certificates in a given year (excluding any shortfall it is allowed to carry forward to the next compliance year), it must pay a penalty.

The ESS Registry 

The Registry is an online database which records the registration, transfer and ultimate surrender of energy savings certificates.  The functions of the Registry include:

Facilitating the registration and transfer of energy savings certificates

Listing details and tracking ownership of energy savings certificates

Allowing Scheme Participants to surrender energy savings certificates to meet mandatory obligations or company-specific offset schemes.

When a certiﬁcate is created, the Registry records information about that certiﬁcate including the entity, activity and activity type associated with it, and the vintage and creation date.  The Registry also tracks the certiﬁcate status (live, surrendered, or forfeited) and ownership history.  One certiﬁcate represents one notional megawatt hour attributable to energy savings arising from a recognised energy savings activity. 

The role of CBS Group

CBS Group propose to act as Downer’s representative and to partner with

Accredited Certificate Providers (ACPs) to create energy savings certificates that can be sold to meet energy savings obligations. 

Noting:

ACPs can only create certificates from recognised energy savings activities for which they are accredited for;

Energy savings certificates can only be created for projects that were implemented in association with an ACP. 

A nominated energy saver must be nominated as at the implementation date.

CBS Group will implement your energy savings activity in accordance with the Act, Regulation and the ESS Rule and comply with any accreditation notices specified.  CBS will take on the responsibility of complying with the requirements of the ESS. 

This will include:

•	Maintaining and storing records of the energy savings projects 

•	Reporting to the Energy Savings Scheme administrator on a regular basis

•	Having the energy savings projects audited by an auditor approved by IPART.

Specifically, these obligations will include:

Program Establishment

Establish and complete ACP project requirements and complete Energy Saver nomination of CBS Group.  Define the Program ‘Tranches’ Working with the Fleet Engineering team to optimise the HVAC modification program and ESS framework to maximise net revenue (cost) of the modification for Downer. 

This may include assessment of additional modifications for inclusion in the scheme submission. Appoint the ACP upon agreement of the proposed measurement and verification methodology for this project

Documentation and Records Management

Define and document the project, scope of HVAC modifications, and proposed Analysis and Verification approach. Establishing defined record keeping arrangements.  The specific records kept depend on the energy savings activity, but must include the:

•	Location in which the energy savings activity occurred

•	Energy savings (calculated in accordance with the ESS Rule) arising from that activity

•	Methodology, data and assumptions used to calculate those energy savings.

Our record keeping arrangements that will:

•	Facilitate the collection of the required records

•	Allow accurate records to be provided to the Scheme Administrator or to an auditor at any time

•	Retain the records for at least 6 years after ESC creation. To fulfil the requirements, CBS will provide:

•	A document register that lists the records that must be collected and retained to support their ESC creation

•	Processes and/or procedures for keeping records including step-by-step details of how records will be obtained, processed, maintained and controlled and the identity and the position(s) of the person(s) responsible for each of these steps in a process/procedure document

•	Quality assurance controls to ensure records are accurate.

ESC Application and ESC Audit Management

Manage the application and fulfilment process including the provision of mandated measurement and verification reporting* Maintaining defined record keeping arrangements, arranging for audits of our energy savings projects and advising IPART if there are any changes to the energy savings projects.

Stakeholder Management

Prepare and delivery briefings to Downer and support for TfNSW briefings on the proposed application

Fixed Costs

Certificate registration fees

1 January 2020 to 31 December 2020 fee is $0.88 per certificate

The application to register Energy Savings Certiﬁcates must be accompanied by the application fee. The application fee will be adjusted for movement in the Consumer Price Index each year. 

---

It is agreed as follows.

Definitions and Interpretation

Definitions

The following definitions apply unless the context requires otherwise.

Accredited Certificate Provider or ACP means a person accredited as an energy savings certificate provider under Part 1 of Schedule 4A of the Act and whose accreditation is in force.

ACP's Fee means an amount equal to 30% of the ESC Sale Proceeds, to cover the fees of NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor, and any registration fees payable in connection with an application by NGE to the Scheme Administrator for registration as an Accredited Certificate Provider, or the registration of the creation or transfer of any HVAC Energy Savings Certificates.

Act means the Electricity Supply Act 1995 (NSW).

Amended Variation Letter means the letter issued by the Supplier to TfNSW, titled 'SGT – DD – Clause 22.2 – Updated Supplier-Initiated Variation Proposal – FE04 HVAC Efficiency – Software Upgrade Proposal – Additional Information' (Reference No. SGT-DD-150036-DEDI-TFNSW-CON-000086) and dated 9 November 2020.

Amended Variation Order means the variation order issued by TfNSW to the Supplier, titled 'SGT – DD – Variation Order 030 (VO030) – Fleet Enhancements (Development) HVAC Software (FE04) (Amended)' (Reference No. SGT-150036-TFNSW-DEDI-CON-000065) and dated 24 November 2020, which amends the Original Variation Order.

Associate means, in relation to a person, any director, officer, agent, consultant, contractor or employee of that person.

Bank Bill Rate means the Australian Bank Bill Swap Reference Rate (Bid) administered by ASX Benchmarks Pty Limited (or any other person which takes over the administration of that rate) for a three month period displayed on page BBSY of the Thomson Reuters Screen (or any replacement Thomson Reuters page which displays that rate) or on the appropriate page of such other information service which publishes that rate from time to time in place of Thomson Reuters, as of 10:30am (Sydney time) on the first day of the three month period during which the default interest is payable. If such page or service ceases to be available, the party who is owed default interest may specify:

another page or service displaying the relevant rate; and

if such rate is not available, a rate that is calculated utilising another source or method as reasonably selected by that party.

Business Day means a day which is not a Saturday, Sunday or a public holiday in Sydney.

Corporations Act means the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth).

Deed means this Side Deed.

Default Interest Rate means the Bank Bill Rate plus 2% per annum calculated daily.

Effective Date means the date of this Deed.

Energy Saver has the meaning given to that term in section 5.2 of the Rule.

Energy Saver Nomination Form means the form attached at Schedule 1 to this Deed.

Energy Savings Certificate or ESC means an energy savings certificate created under Part 1 of Schedule 4A of the Act.

ESC Sale Proceeds means the proceeds from the sale of the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates.

ESS Activities has the meaning given to that term in clause 4.1.

ESS Audit Services Panel means the list of Audit Services Panel members qualified to conduct ESS audits, which is available on IPART's website at https://www.energysustainabilityschemes.nsw.gov.au/Auditors-and-MV-Professionals/Audit-Panel.

ESS Variation Proposal means the Supplier-initiated variation proposal issued by the Supplier to TfNSW, titled 'SYDNEY GROWTH TRAINS – TLS DEED – Clause 25.2 – Supplier's Initiated Variation Notice – Register 'FE04 – HVAC Energy Efficiency' with NSW Energy Savings Scheme – Updated Proposal' (Reference No. DOWRAI-LTTR-10325) and dated 7 October 2021.

HVAC Energy Consumption Data has the meaning given to that term in clause 6(a)(i).

HVAC Energy Savings Certificates has the meaning given to that term in clause 4.1(b)(ii)(B).

HVAC Software Update means an update to the software in each of the HVAC Units in the SGT Fleet, to reduce the energy consumption of the HVAC Units in the SGT Fleet, undertaken pursuant to the Amended Variation Order.

HVAC Units means the heating, venting and air conditioning units, which form part of the climate control system.

Insolvent means, in relation to a party:

it stops or suspends or threatens to stop or suspend payment of all or a class of its debts;

it is insolvent within the meaning of section 95A of the Corporations Act;

it must be presumed by a court to be insolvent by reason of an event set out in section 459C(2) of the Corporations Act;

it fails to comply with a statutory demand (within the meaning of section 459F(1) of the Corporations Act);

it has an administrator or small business restructuring practitioner appointed or any step preliminary to the appointment of an administrator or small business restructuring practitioner is taken;

it has a controller (within the meaning of section 9 of the Corporations Act) or similar officer appointed to all or any of its property; or

it has proceedings commenced, a resolution passed or proposed in a notice of meeting, an application to, or order of, a court made or other steps taken against or in respect of it for its winding up, deregistration or dissolution or for it to enter an arrangement, compromise or composition with or assignment for the benefit of its creditors, a class of them or any of them.

M&V Professional is a person who is approved by the Scheme Administrator on the basis that such person meets the criteria set out in section 7A.15 of the Rule to the satisfaction of the Scheme Administrator.

Net ESC Sale Proceeds means the ESC Sale Proceeds minus the ACP's Fee.

NGE means Northmore Gordon Environmental Pty Ltd (ABN 45 160 805 649).

Payment Trigger Date means the earlier of:

the date on which the Supplier receives the Net ESC Sale Proceeds from NGE;

the date on which the Supplier should have received the Net ESC Sale Proceeds from NGE; and

10 Business Days after the date of the sale of the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates.

Recognised Energy Saving Activity means an activity in respect of which an Energy Savings Certificate may be created under Part 1 of Schedule 4A of the Act.

Regulation means the Electricity Supply (General) Regulation 2014 (NSW).

Rule means the Energy Savings Scheme Rule of 2009.

Scheme Administrator means the Independent Pricing and Regulatory Tribunal (IPART).

Scheme Auditor means any:

person chosen by NGE from the ESS Audit Services Panel; 

person nominated by NGE and approved by the Scheme Administrator; or

other person the Scheme Administrator may require,

from time to time.

SGT Fleet means the 41 Waratah Series 2 trains, which are maintained by the Supplier as part of the Sydney Growth Trains project.

SGT Project Delivery Deed means the document titled 'Delivery Deed – Sydney Growth Trains Project (Contract Number ISD-16-5312A)' dated 1 December 2016 between TfNSW, Rail Corporation New South Wales (ABN 59 325 778 353) and the Supplier.

SGT TLS Deed means the document titled 'TLS Deed – Sydney Growth Trains Project (Contract Number ISD-16-5312B)' dated 1 December 2016 between TfNSW, Rail Corporation New South Wales (ABN 59 325 778 353) and the Supplier, as amended from time to time.

Original Variation Proposal means the Supplier-initiated variation proposal issued by the Supplier to TfNSW, titled 'SGT – DD – Clause 22.2 – Updated Supplier Initiated Variation Proposal – FE04 HVAC Efficiency – Software Upgrade Proposal' (Reference No. SGT-150036-DEDI-TFNSW-CON-000074) and dated 10 September 2020.

[Supplier's Fee means an amount equal to [*], to cover the Supplier's internal costs of carrying out the ESS Activities.] [Note to Downer: Sydney Trains is still considering the Supplier's Fee. We would be grateful if you could detail what is covered by the Supplier's Fee that is over and above what is already covered by the ACP's Fee.]

Original Variation Order means the variation order issued by TfNSW to the Supplier, titled 'SGT – DD – Variation Order 030 (VO030) – Fleet Enhancements (Development) HVAC Software (FE04)' (Reference No. SGT-150036-TFNSW-DEDI-CON-000045) and dated 5 August 2020.

Sydney Trains Share of Proceeds means an amount equal to 50% x (Net ESC Sale Proceeds – Supplier's Fee).

Interpretation

Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation. 

Mentioning anything after includes, including, for example, or similar expressions, does not limit what else might be included.

Nothing in this Deed is to be interpreted against a party solely on the ground that the party put forward this Deed or a relevant part of it.

The following rules apply unless the context requires otherwise.

The singular includes the plural, and the converse also applies.

A gender includes all genders.

If a word or phrase is defined, its other grammatical forms have a corresponding meaning.

A reference to a person includes a corporation, trust, partnership, unincorporated body or other entity, whether or not it comprises a separate legal entity.

A reference to a clause or Schedule is a reference to a clause of, or Schedule to, this Deed.

A reference to an agreement or document (including a reference to this Deed) is to the agreement or document as amended, supplemented, novated or replaced, except to the extent prohibited by this Deed or that other agreement or document.

A reference to writing includes any method of representing or reproducing words, figures, drawings or symbols in a visible and tangible form.

A reference to a party to this Deed or another agreement or document includes the party's successors, permitted substitutes and permitted assigns (and, where applicable, the party's legal personal representatives).

A reference to legislation or to a provision of legislation includes a modification or re-enactment of it, a legislative provision substituted for it and a regulation or statutory instrument issued under it.

A reference to conduct includes an omission, statement or undertaking, whether or not in writing.

A reference to an agreement includes any undertaking, deed, agreement and legally enforceable arrangement, whether or not in writing, and a reference to a document includes an agreement (as so defined) in writing and any certificate, notice, instrument and document of any kind.

A reference to dollars or $ is to Australian currency.

A reference to an asset includes any real or personal, present or future, tangible or intangible property or asset (including intellectual property) and any right, interest, revenue or benefit in, under or derived from the property or asset.

A reference to time is to Sydney time.

A reference to an amount for which a person is contingently liable includes an amount that that person may become actually or contingently liable to pay if a contingency occurs, whether or not that liability actually arises.

A month means a calendar month.

Terms defined in legislation

Words that are defined in the Act, Regulation or Rule and not in clause 1.1 and that are used in this Deed have the same meaning in this Deed as in the Act, Regulation or Rule (as applicable), unless the context requires otherwise.

Consideration

Each party acknowledges having received valuable consideration for entering into and performing this Deed, including the payment of $10 from Sydney Trains to the Supplier (receipt of which is acknowledged by the Supplier).

Implementation of the Amended Variation Order

Subject to paragraphs (b) and (c), the parties agree that the Amended Variation Order will be implemented by the Supplier in accordance with the terms of the SGT TLS Deed.

The parties acknowledge that, as at the date of this Deed, the Amended Variation Order has not been implemented, and agree that the Amended Variation Order will not be implemented prior to the date on which the following conditions are satisfied:

NGE's application for accreditation as the certificate provider in relation to the implementation of the HVAC Software Update as a Recognised Energy Saving Activity has been approved by the Scheme Administrator;

Sydney Trains has nominated NGE as the Energy Saver under section 5.2(b) of the Rule in relation to the implementation of the HVAC Software Update, by executing the Energy Saver Nomination Form; and

all other steps which are necessary to enable Energy Savings Certificates to be created from the energy savings arising from the implementation of the HVAC Software Update have been taken.

The Supplier agrees to implement the Amended Variation Order as soon as reasonably practicable after the conditions set out in paragraphs (b)(i)-(iii) have been satisfied.

Implementation of the ESS Variation Proposal

Supplier obligations

The Supplier agrees to implement the ESS Variation Proposal in accordance with the terms of this Deed and the Act, Regulation and Rule.

In implementing the ESS Variation Proposal, the Supplier must:

appoint:

NGE as the Accredited Certificate Provider;

an M&V Professional; and

a Scheme Auditor;

procure that NGE:

applies to the Scheme Administrator as soon as reasonably practicable after the Effective Date to become accredited as a certificate provider in relation to the implementation of the HVAC Software Update as a Recognised Energy Saving Activity;

creates Energy Savings Certificates in respect of the energy savings arising from the implementation of the HVAC Software Update (HVAC Energy Savings Certificates) promptly after the energy savings occur and in any event within the timeframe prescribed by the Act, Regulation or Rule (as applicable), and maximises the number of HVAC Energy Savings Certificates created;

agrees to not create any Energy Savings Certificates in respect of the implementation of the HVAC Software Update, other than pursuant to the terms of this Deed;

applies to the Scheme Administrator for registration of the creation of the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates promptly after the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates are created;

sells the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates promptly after the registration of the creation of the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates, and maximises the price received for the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates; 

applies to the Scheme Administrator for a transfer of the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates to the relevant purchaser; and

transfers the Net ESC Sale Proceeds from the sale of the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates to the Supplier within 5 Business Days after receiving the ESC Sale Proceeds;

provide any information to NGE, the M&V Professional, the Scheme Auditor and the Scheme Administrator, as each may require;

establish a Baseline Energy Model and Operating Energy Model (as applicable)  in accordance with the requirements in section 7A of the Rule, and have this approved by the M&V Professional in accordance with the Rule;

collect all necessary data and information to support the verification of performance;

verify performance in accordance with the processes and requirements set out in the Rule;

compile any supporting evidence that may be required in accordance with the Rule; and

obtain audit confirmation of the energy savings,

(the ESS Activities).

The Supplier agrees to provide:

information regarding the ESS Activities and the implementation of the Amended Variation Order and ESS Variation Proposal; and

subject to the Supplier obtaining Sydney Trains' prior written consent, reasonable access to the SGT Fleet (as the implementation site for the energy savings activity),

to a representative of the ESS or a member of the ESS Audit Services Panel upon request by them.

Appointed parties

The Supplier must use all reasonable endeavours to procure that NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor do all things necessary (including executing agreements and documents) to implement and give full effect to the ESS Variation Proposal.

The Supplier will be responsible and liable for:

the performance of NGE's, the M&V Professional's and the Scheme Auditor's activities; and

the fees and other costs of NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor (including any fees or costs payable to the Scheme Administrator in connection with an application for accreditation or the registration of the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates),

in connection with the implementation of the ESS Variation Proposal.

The Supplier warrants that NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor appointed by the Supplier are reputable, and have sufficient experience, expertise and ability to perform the relevant activities contemplated by this Deed and under the Act, Regulation and Rule.

Sydney Trains obligation to cooperate

Sydney Trains must use all reasonable endeavours to cooperate with and provide such reasonable assistance as is necessary to support the Supplier in implementing the ESS Variation Proposal.

Energy Saver nomination

The parties acknowledge it is intended that, in relation to the implementation of the HVAC Software Update, Sydney Trains will be:

the Purchaser, as defined in section 10.1 of the Rule, being the person who purchases the goods or services that enable the relevant energy savings to be made; and

the Energy Saver under sections 5.2(a) and 7A.18 of the Rule, being the person defined as the Energy Saver in the Project Impact Assessment with Measurement and Verification Method (PIAM&V Method).

The Supplier agrees to provide any information necessary to give effect to paragraph (a), including in relation to its arrangements with Sydney Trains and the implementation of the Amended Variation Order and the ESS Variation Proposal, upon request by Sydney Trains, a representative of the ESS or a member of the ESS Audit Services Panel.

As the person defined as the Energy Saver in the PIAM&V Method, Sydney Trains must nominate NGE as the Energy Saver under section 5.2(b) of the Rule, in relation to the implementation of the HVAC Software Update, by executing the Energy Saver Nomination Form.

By executing the Energy Saver Nomination Form, Sydney Trains entitles NGE, as the Accredited Certificate Provider, to:

create and own all energy savings arising from the implementation of the HVAC Software Update; and

create and register the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates,

until such time as the nomination is revoked.

The nomination of NGE as the Energy Saver:

takes effect from the date the Energy Saver Nomination Form is signed by Sydney Trains and remains in force until the nomination is revoked by Sydney Trains; and

may be revoked by Sydney Trains at any time.

Calculation of energy savings

The parties acknowledge that the energy savings arising from the implementation of the HVAC Software Update will be calculated using the PIAM&V Method under the Rule.

ESC Sale Proceeds

Distribution of ESC Sale Proceeds

The Supplier will be responsible and liable for the recovery of the Net ESC Sale Proceeds from NGE.

Within 2 Business Days after the Payment Trigger Date, the Supplier will pay to Sydney Trains the Sydney Trains Share of Proceeds.

Liability of the Supplier

The Supplier will always be solely liable for the costs of implementing the ESS Variation Proposal, including the ACP's Fee and its costs of carrying out the ESS Activities, including in the event that:

the implementation of the Amended Variation Order and the ESS Variation Proposal does not result in any energy savings or the creation of any Energy Savings Certificates;

the ESC Sale Proceeds are insufficient to cover the ACP's Fee; or

the Net ESC Sale Proceeds are insufficient to cover the Supplier's Fee.

The Supplier agrees that it assumes the full risk of, and will have no claim against Sydney Trains arising out of or in connection with, the implementation of the ESS Variation Proposal and the arrangements contemplated under this Deed.

No Liability for Fees

The parties acknowledge that the ACP's Fee and the Supplier's Fee will be reimbursed from the ESC Sale Proceeds, and Sydney Trains will never be liable to pay the ACP's Fee or the Supplier's Fee, or for any costs incurred by the Supplier in implementing the ESS Variation Proposal (including any fees or other costs incurred by NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor), including in the event that:

the implementation of the Amended Variation Order and the ESS Variation Proposal does not result in any energy savings or the creation of any Energy Savings Certificates;

the ESC Sale Proceeds are insufficient to cover the ACP's Fee; or

the Net ESC Sale Proceeds are insufficient to cover the Supplier's Fee or any costs incurred by the Supplier in implementing the ESS Variation Proposal.

Sydney Trains Share of Proceeds

The Supplier acknowledges that:

the Sydney Trains Share of Proceeds will not be subject to any deduction for fees (including registration fees), costs, charges, expenses or taxes; and

any fees (including registration fees), costs, charges, expenses or taxes incurred by the Supplier, NGE, the M&V Professional or the Scheme Auditor are included in, and accounted for by, the ACP's Fee and the Supplier's Fee.

Reporting and Records

The Supplier must prepare monthly reports setting out the:

energy consumption data for each of the HVAC Units in the SGT Fleet (HVAC Energy Consumption Data);

energy savings arising from the implementation of the HVAC Software Update;

methodology, data and assumptions used to calculate the energy savings and the notional megawatt hours attributable to the energy savings;

number of HVAC Energy Savings Certificate created, registered and sold during that month; and

ESC Sale Proceeds arising from the sale of any HVAC Energy Savings Certificate during that month.

The Supplier must provide to Sydney Trains any other information reasonably requested by Sydney Trains in connection with the ESS Activities and the implementation of the ESS Variation Proposal.

The Supplier must, and must use reasonable endeavours to procure that NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor, maintain all records in relation to the information set out in paragraphs (a)(i)-(v) for 7 years. This paragraph (c) survives the expiry or termination of this Deed.

Sydney Trains may, upon providing reasonable prior notice to the Supplier:

carry out inspections of the ESS Activities undertaken by the Supplier, as well as the activities of NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor that are carried out in connection with the ESS Activities; and

audit the records maintained by the Supplier in accordance with paragraph (c).

Audits

The Supplier agrees to:

undertake all audit and verification processes in accordance with the requirements of the Act, Regulation and Rule (as applicable);

comply with the requirements of the Act, Regulation and Rule in relation to any audit or verification process; and

not hinder or obstruct any audit or verification process that is required to be carried out by a person other than the Supplier pursuant to the Act, Regulation and Rule (as applicable).

The Supplier agrees that it:

it assumes the full risk of, and will have no claim against Sydney Trains arising out of or in connection with, any audit or verification process (including the results of such audit or verification process) conducted pursuant to the terms of this Deed, and under the Act, Regulation and Rule (as applicable), whether conducted by the Supplier or another person; and

bears its own costs of complying with its obligations under this clause 7 and the requirements of the Act, Regulation and Rule in relation to any audit or verification process.

This clause 7 survives the expiry or termination of this Deed.

Access to HVAC Energy Consumption Data

Sydney Trains grants to the Supplier a non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable licence to use the HVAC Energy Consumption Data (with a right to sublicence to NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor and their Associates) solely for the purposes of performing the ESS Activities in accordance with the terms of this Deed.

The Supplier must ensure that any sublicence granted by the Supplier to NGE, the M&V Professional or the Scheme Auditor and their respective Associates to use the HVAC Energy Consumption Data, is on substantially the same terms as the licence granted by Sydney Trains to the Supplier under paragraph (a).

The licence granted by Sydney Trains under paragraph (a) will expire upon termination or expiry of this Deed.

The parties acknowledge that the HVAC Energy Consumption Data is readily available and can be provided to NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor remotely, without the need to physically access the SGT Fleet, or any of the Supplier's or Sydney Trains' systems.

Indemnity

The Supplier indemnifies Sydney Trains against any claim, loss, damage, liability, cost and expense (including any penalties) that may be incurred or sustained by Sydney Trains caused by or arising out of any breach of this Deed by the Supplier, or any negligence, fraud or wilful misconduct of, or provision of false or misleading information by, the Supplier or its Associates (which will include NGE, the M&V Professional and the Scheme Auditor) in connection with the ESS Activities and ESS Variation Proposal.

Set off

Sydney Trains may set off from any amounts due and payable to the Supplier by Sydney Trains under any agreement between the parties, any amount which the Supplier fails to pay to Sydney Trains in accordance with the terms of this Deed.

Existing Arrangements

Interaction with the existing arrangements

Nothing in this Deed will limit or otherwise affect the Supplier's obligations or create any additional rights for the Supplier, under any other agreement between the parties, including under the SGT Project Delivery Deed and the SGT TLS Deed.

Order of precedence

In the event of any inconsistency, ambiguity, discrepancy or conflict between any of the following documents, the document higher in the list will prevail to the extent of any inconsistency, ambiguity, discrepancy or conflict:

the SGT TLS Deed;

the SGT Project Delivery Deed; and

this Deed.

Compliance with ESS Requirements

If either party discovers that:

a term of this Deed does not comply with;

an additional step is required to meet; or

the activities contemplated in this Deed do not meet,

the requirements for the ESS set out in the Act, Regulation and Rules, then the Supplier must, at its own cost, take all steps and do all things necessary (including, where agreed by Sydney Trains, amending this Deed in accordance with clause 17.2) to ensure that the requirements for the ESS set out in the Act, Regulation and Rules are met, giving effect to the following principles:

the parties intend that the implementation of the HVAC Software Update in the SGT Fleet will generate energy savings that can be independently verified under the ESS framework, and allow for the creation, registration and sale of Energy Savings Certificates;

the Supplier is responsible for all activities associated with the implementation of the HVAC Software Update and all arrangements for participation in the ESS to achieve the objectives set out in paragraph (d); and

Sydney Trains will receive a portion of the proceeds from the sale of Energy Savings Certificates arising from the implementation of the HVAC Software Update, equal to the Sydney Trains Share of Proceeds.

GST

Recovery of GST

If GST is payable, or notionally payable, on a supply made under or in connection with this Deed, the party providing the consideration for that supply must pay as additional consideration an amount equal to the amount of GST payable, or notionally payable, on that supply (the GST Amount). Subject to the prior receipt of a tax invoice, the GST Amount is payable at the same time that the other consideration for the supply is provided. If a tax invoice is not received prior to the provision of that other consideration, the GST Amount is payable within 10 days after the receipt of a tax invoice. This clause does not apply to the extent that the consideration for the supply is expressly stated to be GST inclusive or the supply is subject to reverse charge.

Liability net of GST

Where any indemnity, reimbursement or similar payment under this Deed is based on any cost, expense or other liability, it shall be reduced by any input tax credit entitlement, or notional input tax credit entitlement, in relation to the relevant cost, expense or other liability.

Adjustment events

If an adjustment event occurs in relation to a supply made under or in connection with this Deed, the GST Amount will be recalculated to reflect that adjustment and an appropriate payment will be made between the parties.

Survival

This clause will not merge upon completion and will continue to apply after expiration or termination of this Deed.

Definitions

Unless the context requires otherwise, words and phrases used in this clause that have a specific meaning in the GST law (as defined in the A New Tax System (Goods and Services Tax) Act 1999 (Cth)) shall have the same meaning in this clause.

Notices Provided by the Supplier

The Supplier must promptly notify Sydney Trains if it becomes aware of any of the following:

NGE's accreditation as a certificate provider generally, or in respect of the implementation of the HVAC Software Update, is suspended or cancelled by the Scheme Administrator;

NGE is required to surrender any Energy Savings Certificates to the Scheme Administrator, or the Scheme Administrator cancels any Energy Savings Certificates in respect of which NGE is registered as the owner, under section 45 of Part 1 of Schedule 4A of the Act;

NGE has contravened a civil penalty provision (as prescribed by the Regulation) and is required to pay a monetary penalty pursuant to a civil penalty order under section 63B of Part 1 of Schedule 4A of the Act;

a compliance officer has issued a penalty notice to NGE under section 71C of Part 1 of Schedule 4A of the Act; or

the Scheme Administrator has refused NGE's application for:

accreditation as a certificate provider generally, or in respect of the implementation of the HVAC Software Update;

registration of the creation of any HVAC Energy Savings Certificates; or

a transfer of the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates to a purchaser of the HVAC Energy Savings Certificates.

Termination

Sydney Trains may terminate this Deed immediately by written notice to the Supplier if:

the Supplier commits a breach of this Deed (unless the breach is capable of remedy, in which case if the Supplier fails to remedy the breach within 10 Business Days after being required in writing to do so);

the Supplier fails to pay any sum due and payable to Sydney Trains under this Deed and that failure is not cured within 5 Business Days;

the Supplier is or becomes Insolvent; or

the SGT Project Delivery Deed or the SGT TLS Deed is terminated or expires. 

Termination of this Deed does not affect any accrued rights or remedies of either party.

Interest on Overdue Amounts

If the Supplier fails to pay any amount payable by it under this Deed on its due date, interest shall accrue on the overdue amount from the due date up to the date of actual payment (both before and after judgment) at the Default Interest Rate. Any interest accruing under this clause shall be immediately payable by the Supplier on demand by Sydney Trains.

Default interest (if unpaid) arising on an overdue amount will be compounded with the overdue amount at the end of each month but will remain immediately due and payable.

Miscellaneous

Entire agreement

This Deed contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to its subject matter. It sets out the only conduct, representations, warranties, covenants, conditions, agreements or understandings (collectively Conduct) relied on by the parties and supersedes all earlier Conduct by or between the parties in connection with its subject matter. Neither party has relied on or is relying on any other Conduct in entering into this Deed and completing the transactions contemplated by it.

Amendment

This Deed may be amended only by another deed executed by all the parties.

No agency or partnership

Nothing in this Deed is to be construed as constituting an agency, partnership, joint venture, or any other form of association between the parties in which one party may be liable for the acts or omissions of any other party. No party has the authority to incur any obligation or make any representation or warranty on behalf of, or to pledge the credit of, any other party.

No merger

The rights and obligations of the parties will not merge on the completion of any transaction contemplated by this Deed. They will survive the execution and delivery of any assignment or other document entered into for the purpose of implementing a transaction.

No waiver

A failure to exercise or a delay in exercising any right, power or remedy under this Deed does not operate as a waiver. A single or partial exercise or waiver of the exercise of any right, power or remedy does not preclude any other or further exercise of that or any other right, power or remedy. A waiver is not valid or binding on the party granting that waiver unless made in writing.

Costs and duty

Each party must bear its own costs arising out of the negotiation, preparation and execution of this Deed. All duty (including stamp duty and any fines, penalties and interest) payable on or in connection with this Deed and any instrument executed under or any transaction evidenced or contemplated by this Deed must be borne by the Supplier.

Execution and counterparts

This Deed may be executed in counterparts.

Further assurances

Each party must do anything necessary (including executing agreements and documents) to give full effect to this Deed and the transactions contemplated by it.

Governing law and jurisdiction

This Deed is governed by the laws of New South Wales. In relation to it and related non-contractual matters each party irrevocably submits to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of courts with jurisdiction there and waives any right to object to the venue on any ground.

Severability of provisions

Any provision of this Deed that is prohibited or unenforceable in any jurisdiction is ineffective as to that jurisdiction to the extent of the prohibition or unenforceability. That does not invalidate the remaining provisions of this Deed nor affect the validity or enforceability of that provision in any other jurisdiction.

Remedies cumulative

The rights, powers and remedies provided to the parties in this Deed are in addition to, and do not exclude or limit, any right, power or remedy provided by law or equity or by any agreement.

Survival of provisions

All representations and warranties in this Deed survive the execution and delivery of this Deed and the completion of transactions contemplated by it.

Each indemnity in this Deed is a continuing obligation, separate and independent from the other obligations of the parties and survives the expiry or termination of this Deed.

Energy Saver Nomination Form

Executed and delivered as a Deed.

[Note: Sydney Trains execution block to be confirmed.]

| This Deed is made on | This Deed is made on |

| Parties | Parties |

|  | Sydney Trains (ABN 38 284 779 682) of Level 8, 231 Elizabeth Street, Sydney NSW 2000 (Sydney Trains). |

|  | Downer EDI Rail Pty Ltd (ABN 92 000 002 031) of 39 Delhi Road, North Ryde NSW 2113 (the Supplier). |

| Recitals | Recitals |

|  | The Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) was established by the NSW Government in 2009 to create a financial incentive for the implementation of activities that reduce energy consumption. |

|  | Transport for NSW (TfNSW) and the Supplier entered into the SGT Project Delivery Deed and the SGT TLS Deed with Rail Corporation New South Wales (ABN 59 325 778 353) on 1 December 2016. |

|  | On 5 August 2020, TfNSW issued the Original Variation Order to the Supplier under the SGT TLS Deed for the implementation of an update to the software in each of the HVAC Units in the SGT Fleet, to reduce the energy consumption of the HVAC Units in the SGT Fleet. |

|  | On 10 September 2020, the Supplier issued the Original Variation Proposal to TfNSW under the SGT TLS Deed, in relation to the implementation of the update to the software in each of the HVAC Units in the SGT Fleet. |

|  | On 9 November 2020, the Supplier issued the Amended Variation Letter to TfNSW amending the Original Variation Proposal by changing the scope and increasing the price of the Original Variation Proposal. |

|  | On 24 November 2020, TfNSW accepted the Supplier's amendments to the Original Variation Proposal as set out in the Supplier's letter dated 9 November 2020, by issuing the Amended Variation Order to the Supplier under the SGT TLS Deed. |

|  | On 7 October 2021, the Supplier issued the ESS Variation Proposal to TfNSW under the SGT TLS Deed, proposing a variation to enable the parties to participate in the ESS and independently verify, and create and sell Energy Savings Certificates arising from, the energy savings achieved through the implementation of the update to the software in each of the HVAC Units in the SGT Fleet pursuant to the Amended Variation Order. |

|  | Pursuant to a licence arrangement, Sydney Trains has custody of, and is responsible for the care, control and maintenance of, the SGT Fleet, and engages the Supplier to carry out maintenance on the SGT Fleet under the SGT TLS Deed. |

|  | This Deed sets out the terms on which the Supplier will implement the ESS Variation Proposal. |



| Executed as a deed in accordance with section 127 of the Corporations Act 2001 by Downer EDI Rail Pty Ltd: |
