## CBS Group-Wattly - EP - Partner Payment Agreement ALL Certs v12- 2Mar21.docx

*File: `Wattly Agreements/CBS Group-Wattly - EP - Partner Payment Agreement ALL Certs v12- 2Mar21.docx`*

ENVIRONMENTAL PRODUCTS – PARTNER PAYMENT AGREEMENT

NORTHMORE GORDON ENVIRONMENT  PTY LTD ACN 160 805 649 of 132 Cremorne Street, Cremorne Victoria 3121 (Wattly)

CBS GROUP PTY LTD  ABN 20164870080 of Level 3, 480 Collins Street, MELBOURNE VIC 3000  (Partner)

SCHEDULES

PROJECT SCHEDULE 1: SYDNEY TRAINS HVAC UPGRADES – WARATAH & SGT FLEETS under NSW ESS

Future schedules to be added for other projects

APPENDIX A – REGISTER OF OPPORTINUTIES FOR EXCLUSIVITY

PPENDIX A – REGISTER OF OPPORTINUTIES FOR EXCLUSIVITY

The following list Wattly and the Partner agree to work exclusivity with each other in order to offer funding from Environment Certificates to the end Customer.  Wattly cannot pursue these opportunities with another partner nor direct with the end Customer and the Partner cannot pursue these opportunities with another business offering Environment Certificate services.

* Expiry Date - if contract not signed with the Customer to proceed by this date or exclusivity not agreed to be extended by that date.

BACKGROUND

Wattly is accredited under various Environment Schemes and carries on a business involving the capturing, ensuring compliance and submitting information required for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates), the trading and sale of the Certificates, and consulting services associated with these activities.

The Partner carries on a business involving the consulting and advisory services for the procurement, design, installation, servicing and/or maintenance of efficient end-user equipment by their Customers which may be entitled for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates) under the Environmental Schemes.  These are Scheme Activities performed using Approved Products & Services.

Wattly has agreed to provide Wattly Services to the Partner and the Partner’s Customers to enable the Customers to take advantage of funding from the Environment Schemes.  Wattly will work with the Partner’s Customer to collect evidence from the Customer.  The Partner will need to provide Wattly certain Evidence and may need to assist Wattly in obtaining evidence from the Customer.  The Partner will receive commission payments based on successful registration of certificates for Customer projects (Scheme Activities)

OPERATIVE PART

Definitions

In this Agreement:

Accreditations means any and all accreditations, qualifications and approvals that the Partner and its personnel must hold at law as required by the Scheme and as otherwise set out in the Wattly Manuals;

Accredited (Certificate) Providers means the party holds an accreditation under one or more of the Environment Schemes.

ACL means the Australian Consumer Law Schedule of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth).

Activity (Scheme Activity) means the installation, upgrade, or modification or energy-efficiency or renewable-energy equipment or processes using Approved Products & Services that may be entitled to Environment Certificates under the Environment Schemes.

Agreement means this agreement (including any schedule or annexure), and the Forward Contracts;

Approved Products & Services means products, services and materials necessary for the provision of the Partner Services that are expressly approved for use by the Partner under the Environmental Schemes or as otherwise set out in the Wattly Manuals;

Business Day means Monday to Friday excluding public holidays;

Certificates means the Environmental Products, as appropriate;

Change of Control means, in relation to the Partner, the happening of an event or circumstance where a person presently unable to do any of the following becomes able to do so, either alone or in association with others directly or indirectly:

control the composition of more than one half of the Partner’s board of directors;

be in a position to cast, or control the casting of, more than one half of the maximum number of votes that are able to be cast at a general meeting of the members of the Partner or its holding company; or

have a beneficial interest in more than one half of the shares issued in the Partner or its holding company;

Confidential Information means:

all business and financial information, sales and supply details, marketing strategies, customer and supplier listings, business listings, pricing information, information concerning Wattly or its customers and business associates; 

all (if any) other information relating to Wattly or its business which, by its nature, places or potentially places Wattly at an advantage over its present or future business competitors, including but not limited to, the Wattly Manuals and Wattly Tools, processes and workflows; and

any other information that would otherwise at law be considered secret or confidential information of Wattly;

whether or not marked "Confidential" BUT does not include information which:

at the time of first disclosure by Wattly to the Partner is already in the public domain;

after disclosure by Wattly, becomes part of the public domain otherwise than by disclosure in breach of the terms of this Agreement; and

the Partner can prove that the information was in its possession before the time of first disclosure by or discovery from Wattly.

Customer means the energy user (end-customer) to whom the Partner provides the installation of efficient end-user equipment (Approved Products) in accordance with the Scheme, and does not mean Wattly;

Certificates see Environmental Products

Default Interest Rate means the rate per annum of two percent (2%) higher than the rate fixed for the time being under Section 2 of the Penalty Interest Rates Act 1983 (Vic);

Documentation means Wattly’s checklists and forms relevant to the Scheme, the Requirements and the Specifications, as provided to the Partner and updated from time to time;

Effective Date means the date of execution of this Agreement;

Encumbrance means:

any:

legal or equitable interest or power created, arising in or reserved in or over an interest in any property or asset; or

security for payment of money, performance of obligations or protection against default;

anything or preferential interest or arrangement of any kind giving a person priority or preference over claims or other persons with respect to any property or asset;

a PPSA Security Interest; or

an agreement or arrangement, regardless of whether or not it is enforceable, to grant or create anything referred to in (i), or (ii) above;

Environmental Products (Certificates) means certificates created under the Environmental Schemes, including but not limited to ESCs, VEECs, STCs, LGCs, and ACCUs as relevant;

Environmental Scheme (Schemes) means each of the Government Energy Efficiency, Renewable, and Carbon Abatement Schemes; including, but not limited to the ESS, VEU, REES, EEIS, RET, CFI, as relevant;  ESS (NSW Energy Saving Scheme), VEET (Victorian Energy Efficiency Target), REES (South Australian Retailer Energy Efficient Scheme), EEIS (ACT Energy Efficiency Improvement Scheme), RET (Federal Renewable Energy Target), CFI (Federal Carbon Farming Initiative).

Evidence means any and all documents, requests, quotations, receipts, job sheets, time sheets, notes and confirmations relating to provision of the Partner Services by the Partner; 

Exclusivity Agreements means a list of customers, opportunities, projects or other group of activities that the Partner and Wattly agree to work exclusively with each other for the purpose of Environment Certificate creation under the Environment Schemes

Forward Contracts means any and all forward certificate delivery agreements between Wattly and the Partner entered in to, or otherwise in operation, during the Term;  

GST means any tax imposed on the supply of goods or services, consumption or value added tax, including without limitation any tax imposed in the A New Tax System (Goods and Services Tax) Act (Cth);

Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, the Wattly Manuals, the Wattly Tools, software, workflow, and all copyright, trade mark rights, design rights, patent rights and confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to Wattly or that relates to the Wattly’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it;

Invoice Date means the dates for invoicing the Services as set out in Item 2 of the Schedule;

Licence means the Wattly’s licence of Wattly Tools and Wattly Manuals to the Partner for the purposes of this Agreement as set out in clause 6 of this Agreement;

PPSA Security Interest means a security interest as defined in the Personal Property Securities Act 2009 (Cth);

Partner Confidential Information means:

all business and financial information, sales and supply details, marketing strategies, customer and supplier listings, business listings, pricing information, information concerning the Partner its customers and business associates; 

any other information that would otherwise at law be considered secret or confidential information of the Partner;

any other Items listed in the Item 5 Partner Confidential Information in this contract.

Partner Instructions (Item 8) means guidance from the Partner regarding any aspects of the relationship, such as working with the partners customers.

Partner Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, and all copyright, trade mark rights, design rights, patent rights and confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to the Partner or that relates to the Partner’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it; and any other Items listed in Item 6 in this contract.

Partner Services means the services provided by the Partner under clause 3, and such further services agreed from time to time;  This includes services provided to end Customers, and services provide to Wattly to facilitate Wattly’s registration of Environment Certificates on behalf the Customer.

Requirements means any and all processes, procedures, duties and obligations that apply to the Scheme and as otherwise set out in the Wattly Manuals;

Scheme see Environmental Scheme

Security Amount means the amount payable by the Partner to Wattly as set out in Item 2 of the Schedule;

Specifications means any and all specifications, standards and terms that set out the minimum standards required under the Scheme, and as otherwise set out in the Wattly Manuals;

Term means the period from the Effective Date until this Agreement is ended in accordance with clause 15; 

Wattly Tools means Wattly’s systems, data collection tools and record keeping tools designed to assist the Partner to meet obligations under the Scheme, including but not limited to workflows, forms, checklists, spreadsheets, software and related documents;

Wattly Manuals means the manuals, checklists, training materials, and guidelines authored by or on behalf of Wattly as published and updated from time to time, setting out, without limitation, the Accreditations, the Requirements, the Specifications, the Approved Products and the Documentation;

Wattly Services means the services provided by Wattly under clause 5; and

Year means the period commencing 1 July in one year and ending on 30 June in the following year.

Engagement

Partner engages Wattly to provide Wattly Services to the Partner and to the end Customer, and the Partner agrees to provide Partner Services to Watly for the Term on the terms of this Agreement

Wattly and the Partner may in writing agree to vary the Partner Services or the Wattly Services, or provide additional services, from time to time.  Any varied or additional services will be provided on the terms and conditions of this Agreement and such other terms as may be agreed.

Partner Services to Wattly

The Partner Services incorporate all services provided by the Partner to Wattly and required to give effect to the Environmental Schemes.

During the Term of this Agreement, the Partner must provide the Partner Services on the terms set out in this Agreement.

The Partner’s obligations

The Partner warrants that it has the qualifications, experience and expertise appropriate and necessary to perform the Partner Services offered to End Customers (Item 7).

The Partner warrants that it has the qualifications, accreditations, experience and expertise appropriate and necessary to provide the Partner Services to Wattly under this Agreement.

In providing the Partner Services, the Partner must, and must ensure that its staff must:

act honestly, ethically and with due care and skill;

comply with the Environmental Scheme Requirements as directed by Wattly;

use Approved Products when a Customer is claiming Environmental Certificates;

ensure the Partner Services are performed in accordance with the Scheme requirements and are eligible to create Environmental Products as appropriate and directed by Wattly;

provide Evidence to Wattly of provision of the Partner Services to a particular Customer within 30 Business Days of completion of provision of a Scheme Activity for that Customer; 

submit to Wattly for its approval any additional activities or services it wishes to include as Partner Services; 

not cause, assist it or otherwise allow the creation of any Encumbrance over any Certificate.

During the Term the Partner must:

observe and comply with the Wattly Manuals, as modified from time to time, in every respect and ensure that its employees and contractors also do so;

ensure that all advertising materials it uses relating to the provision of Scheme Activities or otherwise relevant to this Agreement identifies Wattly as the Accredited Person (Victoria) or Accredited Certificate Provider (New South Wales), as appropriate, under the Scheme;

effect and maintain throughout the Term, at its own expense, and ensure any subcontractors also maintain:

public liability insurance with a minimum cover of $10,000,000 per claim; and

product liability insurance with a minimum cover of $10,000,000 per claim;

not make any false or misleading representations about the Scheme, Wattly, the Partner, the Wattly Services or the Partner Services; 

undertake and participate in training for the Environmental Schemes, as directed by Wattly;

upon request by Wattly or a Scheme auditor, provide access to Documentation, Evidence and other information relevant to the Partner Services and the Scheme for the purposes of inspection or audit, and provide its full assistance to Wattly and the Scheme auditor in any inspection or audit or audit activity or the implementation of any direction arising from an audit finding;

facilitate and assist Wattly to access the Customer and the Customer’s site for the purpose of proving compliance with the Scheme requirements. 

This includes providing evidence, assurance, and facilitating visits or communication with the Customer for third-party auditors as well as the Scheme regulatory staff.

adhere to all applicable policies of Wattly in force, as amended from time to time, including as set out in the Wattly Manuals, updated by Wattly Newsletters;

comply with all occupational health and safety requirements relevant to the Scheme and generally at law, including but not limited to any requirement to obtain and maintain any registration, licences or permits; and

ensure its employees and contractors are paid in accordance with the law; and

The Partner must not:

use the Wattly Tools other than in providing the Partner Services;

use the Wattly Tools to create Certificates itself or assist third parties to create Certificates;

reverse engineer any of the Wattly Tools; or

disclose to third parties the Wattly Tools or the details of any Wattly Tools.

Wattly Services

During the Term, Wattly must provide the following assistance to the Partner as Wattly considers reasonably necessary and appropriate:

Sales guides and quoting tools and estimates for value of Environment Certificates to allow the Partner to estimate the value of a Customers upgrade under the Environment Scheme; 

keep confidential details of all Partner sales opportunities and Customer details;

licence and provide the Wattly Tools to assist the Partner’s Customers to record the Evidence and other information necessary to meet the obligations under the Scheme;

provide Documentation to help guide the Partner’s Customer through the steps of the Scheme;

provide to the Partner timely communication on changes to the Wattly Manuals, procedures or systems under the relevant Scheme or software upgrades by Wattly;

keep confidential, or depersonalise prior to disclosure, details of all Partner Services proposed by the Partner under the Agreement and only disclose the information required under the relevant Scheme;

provide and review quality assurance procedures to aid the accurate creation of Certificates;

perform a quality assurance procedure and alert the Partner’s Customer to any missing information within 20 Business Days of receipt of information confirming completion of the Partner Services provided to a Customer;

provide training covering the relevant Scheme and the Wattly Tools; and

retain all records of the Partner Services for a period of at least six years or as specified by the Scheme requirements; and

With respect to the creation of Environmental Products, Wattly must:

register as a Certificate creator under the relevant Scheme;

promptly create Certificates attributable to the Partner Services following receipt of the relevant information; and

Wattly must either make payment to the Partner’s Customers based on 

With respect to the creation of Environmental Products, if these are deemed ineligible for due to fraud or negligence by the Customer or Partner

the Partner has no claim against Wattly; and

the Partner must indemnify and keep Wattly indemnified from all claims, suits, demands, loss, damage, cost or liability in relation to the ineligible Environmental Products.

Licence of Wattly Tools

Subject to the provisions set out in clause 16.1(a), Wattly licences the Wattly Tools to the Partner during the Term of this Agreement.

The Partner must only use the Wattly Tools for the purposes of providing the Partner Services under the terms of this Agreement.

Wattly may, as it considers necessary, maintain, delete or modify the Wattly Tools from time to time.

Wattly Manuals

Wattly will publish or provide access to the Wattly Manuals, which among other things, sets out various Requirements, Specifications, and responsibilities of the Partner, and its provision of the Partner Services.

Wattly may, in its absolute discretion, vary the Wattly Manuals from time to time by giving the Partner at least 7 days notice in writing.

The Wattly Manuals form part of this Agreement, and where there is a conflict, the terms of the Wattly Manuals shall prevail to the extent of the inconsistency.

Any breach by the Partner of any provision contained in the Wattly Manuals is deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.

The effective date of any policy or obligation contained in the Wattly Manuals shall be the date nominated in the Wattly Manuals or updated part or, if no date is nominated, then the first date that the particular amended Wattly Manual is published on Wattly’s website.

The Wattly Manuals remain at all times the property of Wattly, and must be returned to Wattly immediately upon request or upon termination of this Agreement.

The Partner must not copy, reproduce, retain, store, record, computerise, document or duplicate any part of the Manuals other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement or with written permission from Wattly.

Security

This clause is not applicable to Referral or Commission Agreements.

Payment 

The Payment to the Customer is payable in accordance with the terms agreed on a per Customer and per Project basis or as specified in Item 4 Pricing. 

Commission Payment is payable to the Partner in accordance with the terms agreed in Item 4 Pricing

Start-up costs and expenses

The Partner must ensure that it has the necessary resources and staff to provide the Partner Services.  

General Relationship

The Partner is an independent of Wattly and no agency, employment, partnership or other relationship will exist between the Partner and Wattly.  Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed or construed to render Wattly liable for any costs obligations or expenditures pursuant to any relevant employment laws.

The Partner must not at any time represent to any other party that it is an employee, servant or agent of Wattly, but for the purpose of complying with the Scheme:

the Partner’s agents must at all times identify themselves to potential or existing Customers including identifying;

the company they represent, and 

that the Accredited Person or Accredited Certificate Provider, as appropriate depending on the Scheme, is Wattly; and

the Partner’s sales and installation documentation provided to the Customer must identify that the Accredited Person or Accredited Certificate Provider, as appropriate depending on the Scheme, is Wattly.  

The Partner will have no authority to bind Wattly legally with respect to any third party unless expressly authorised in writing to do so by Wattly.

The Partner must not:

incur any liability on behalf of Wattly or in any way pledge or purport to pledge Wattly’s credit; and

accept any order nor make any contract binding upon Wattly without Wattly’s prior written approval.

During the term, the Partner retains the right to enter into any contract with any other person or persons, company or firm to perform services similar to those contemplated by this Agreement, provided that they do not interfere with the Partner’s ability to comply with this Agreement.

Intellectual Property

The Partner has no proprietary right or interest in the Intellectual Property.

The Partner must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world the Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trade marks or designs derived from or similar to the Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.

Any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from any party of the information comprised in the Intellectual Property whether attributable in whole or in part to the Partner, will become and remain the property of Wattly.

The Partner hereby assigns and conveys to Wattly any intellectual property rights that the Partner may acquire arising from any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from the Intellectual Property.

Wattly has no proprietary right or interest in the Partner Intellectual Property.

Wattly must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world the Partner Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from or similar to the Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.

Confidential Information

The Partner must:

maintain strict confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information, and must take all reasonable, physical and legal steps necessary to prevent its disclosure;

not divulge any aspect of the Confidential Information to any other person other than pursuant to this Agreement;

not use or exploit the Confidential Information other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement;

not make any written notes, copy, reproduce, retain, store, record, computerise, document or duplicate any part of the Confidential Information which appears in any written or computerised or other recorded form other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement;

not make any improvements or modifications to or derivations or discoveries from any of the Confidential Information other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; and

if it consider it may be necessary to disclose any of the Confidential Information to any other person, obtain prior written authorisation from Wattly before making such disclosure.

If the Partner is uncertain whether any information comprises part of the Confidential Information then it must seek direction from Wattly before divulging the information.

The Partner may disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its officers and employees, and its legal, financial or other advisers and their respective employees and officers who:

have a need to know for the purposes of this Agreement, and only to the extent that each has a need to know;

are aware that the Confidential Information must be kept confidential; and

have agreed in writing to comply with substantially the same obligations in respect of the Confidential Information as those imposed on the Partner under this Agreement.

The obligations on the Partner in relation to Confidential Information survive termination of the Agreement.

Wattly and any subcontractors engagement by Wattly to fulfil Wattly Services under this agreement must: 

maintain strict confidentiality in relation to the Partner Confidential Information, as set out in the Schedule, and must take all reasonable, physical and legal steps necessary to prevent its disclosure; and

not divulge any aspect of the Partner Confidential Information to any other person other than pursuant to this Agreement.

Partner’s Employees and Contractors

The Partner warrants that it will make a full and detailed check of all personnel providing the Partner Services to ensure that they are fully qualified, accredited and suitable to perform the Partner Services.

The Partner’s staff will be trained by the Partner to diligently, efficiently and properly carry out the Partner Services. 

The Partner will be liable for any breach of this Agreement by its officers, employees, contractors and advisers.  The Partner must provide Wattly with details of any contactors it intends to utilise, for approval.

During the Term, the Partner must:

ensure that its employees, agents or approved subcontractors are competent and have the necessary skills to provide the Partner Services; and

adequately and properly manage, direct and control its employees, agents and subcontractors in providing the Partner Services.

The Partner may disclose Confidential Information to those of its officers and employees and its legal, financial or other advisers and their respective employees and officers who:

have a need to know (but only if and to the extent that they have a need to know) to assist the Partner to properly market or sell its products and provide the Partner Services;

are aware that Confidential Information must be kept confidential; and

in the case of employees and officers, have agreed in writing to comply with the terms of this Agreement as if they were a party to this Agreement.

Termination

Clause not used.

If either party ("Defaulting Party"):

commits a breach of this Agreement and fails to remedy such breach within fourteen (14) days of receipt of a notice in writing requiring such breach to be remedied; or

becomes insolvent or is made subject to an application for winding up or resolves to wind up voluntarily (other than for the purposes of a solvent reconstruction), or has a receiver (or similar functionary) or an administrator appointed in respect of any of its assets, or ceases to carry on business or enters into a composition or arrangement with its creditors generally; or

commits a breach of this Agreement or the Scheme, and such breach is deemed by the other party to involve fraud or dishonesty; or

there is a Change of Control in relation to the Default Party;

the other party may terminate this Agreement immediately by giving written notice of termination to the Defaulting Party.

Consequences of Termination

On termination of this Agreement, for whatever reason:

the Licence granted by Wattly to the Partner to use the Wattly Tools is immediately terminated, and the Partner must cease to use the Wattly Tools and as soon as reasonably practical return to Wattly or destroy any and all of the Wattly Tools;

the both parties must, as soon as reasonably practical, return to the other party or destroy any and all of any their Confidential Information and their Intellectual Property in its possession or control including any variation, modification or improvement; and

the Partner must render its account for all Partner Services properly provided up until the date of termination.  Wattly must make payment, provided it is reasonably satisfied with the accuracy of the account and the quality and compliance of the Partner Services, within 7 days of receipt of the account.

Upon termination of this Agreement:

the Partner must not accept any new requests from Customers for it to provide them with any Partner Services under the terms of this Agreement; and

in respect of Partner Services scheduled at the time of termination but not yet provided (Scheduled Works), the Partner must at Wattly’s election:

provide the Partner Services to the Customer; or

assign the Scheduled Works to Wattly, and Wattly may arrange for an alternate service provider to provide the Scheduled Works to the Customer.

Termination of the Agreement shall not relieve a party of liability to the other party in respect of the rights or remedies of the other party which have accrued prior to termination.

Liabilities and Indemnities

Except as the terms of this Agreement specifically state, this Agreement does not include by implication any other term, condition or warranty in respect of the quality, merchantability, acceptability, fitness for purpose, condition, description or performance of the either parties services (Wattly Services or Partner Services) offered to the other party or any contractual remedy for their failure.

Neither party is liable to the other party in any way arising under or in connection with the provision of the Services;

for any indirect or consequential losses or expenses suffered by either party or any third party, howsoever caused, including but not limited to loss of turnover, profits, business or goodwill or any liability to any other party.

Nothing in this Agreement is to be interpreted as excluding, restricting or modifying or having the effect of excluding, restricting or modifying the application of any State or Federal legislation applicable to the sale of goods or supply of services which cannot be excluded, restricted or modified.

Indemnity

Except as otherwise caused or contributed to by the other party,  each party must indemnify and keep the other party indemnified from all claims, suits, demands, loss, damage, cost or liability in relation to or incidental to any negligent acts under this Agreement, including but not limited to: 

any certificate surrenders, fine or penalty imposed on the indemnified party by reason of a negligent act on the part of the indemnifying party.

Retrospective Certificate Forfeiture.  Should the Regulator of an Environmental Scheme change their interpretation of the eligibility of an activity completed by the Partner and require certificate surrender then the forfeiture portion from the Partner would be proportional to the amount received (relative to Wattly fees) for that activity.

Assignment

The Partner must not assign, charge or deal with the Agreement without the prior written consent of Wattly, which consent may be withheld in its absolute discretion.

Wattly may assign its interests under this Agreement provided that any assignee must first covenant to be bound by Wattly’s rights and obligations hereunder.

Duration of Provisions

The covenants, conditions, provisions and warranties contained in this Agreement shall not merge or terminate upon completion of the transactions contemplated herein but to the extent that they have not been fulfilled and satisfied or are capable of having effect shall remain in full force and effect.

Parties to do all things necessary

The parties agree to do all things and execute all documents necessary to give effect to the terms of this Agreement.

The Partner must cooperate in the execution of all documents necessary to complete any action to be taken under this Agreement, including in relation to termination.  

For the purposes of clause 21.2, if the Partner is unable for any reason or if the Partner fails, refuses or neglects to execute any such document upon the reasonable request of Wattly, then the Partner hereby appoints Wattly as its irrevocable attorney in its name, and on its behalf, with the right to do any and all actions and things necessary to complete the action to be taken under this Agreement, including in relation to termination, and to execute and deliver all documents necessary or required to be completed.

If at any time after the date this Agreement is signed any legislation or scheme rules that apply to this Agreement are repealed, terminated or materially amended (“Change of Law Event”) and as a result no person or entity (including the Regulator) can legally create or transfer Environmental Certificates, then the parties must meet in good faith as soon as practicable after becoming aware of the Change of Law Event to negotiate a replacement arrangement to preserve the parties’ financial position under this Agreement to the extent possible.

Costs

Each party will bear its own legal and accounting costs incurred in respect of the preparation, negotiation, and execution of this Agreement.

Severability

If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then it must be interpreted as narrowly as necessary to allow it to be enforceable or valid.

If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then:

the provision (or part of it) must be severed from this Agreement;

the remaining provisions (and remaining part of the provision) are valid and enforceable.

Victorian law and jurisdiction

The law of Victoria from time to time governs this Agreement.

The parties agree to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Victoria, the Federal Court of Australia, and of courts entitled to hear appeals from those courts.

Amending this Agreement

An amendment or change is only effective once it is:

made in writing; and

executed by all parties.

This is the entire Agreement

This is the entire Agreement of the parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement.

There is no other Agreement, understanding, warranty or representation, whether oral or written, binding the parties concerning any aspect of this Agreement.

No waiver

A party may exercise its rights at any time and does not waive those even if that party:

previously waived a breach or default of all or part of the same or other provision; or

delayed or omitted to exercise its rights.

A waiver is only effective:

if it is signed by the party granting the waiver; and

to the extent set out in the waiver.

Interpretation

When interpreting this Agreement:

headings are for convenience only and do not affect the interpretation of this Agreement;

the singular includes the plural and the plural includes the singular;

any gender includes any other gender;

any thing required to be done on a day that is not a Business Day, may be done on the next Business Day; 

reference to a person includes a natural person, corporation, partnership, association or other legal entity;

reference to legislation includes that legislation as amended or modified or re-enacted; and

reference to a provision or clause means a provision or clause in this Agreement.

EXECUTED AS A DEED

## Wattly NG Proposal for ESC creation from PIAM.docx

*File: `Wattly Agreements/Wattly NG Proposal for ESC creation from PIAM.docx`*

Proposal for ESC creation from PIAM&V

Prepared for CBS Group Pty Ltd for Downer Sydney Trains HVAC Upgrade

CONTACT DETAILS

Hamish McGovern
hamish@wattly.com
0416 296 827

Alan Wang
alan@wattly.com
0434 126 577

9th September 2020

V1.0 - Valid for six weeks

Our Understanding

Sydney Trains is planning to implement and commission a range of Energy Conservation Measures (ECM) in the form of HVAC upgrades across the fleet of trains operating across the Greater Sydney Metropolitan Area. Downer will be performing these upgrades over a period of 4-5 years.  CBS Group is looking to access funding from the NSW Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) to provide additional capital and reduce the net cost for Downer throughout the upgrade cycle.  

The Energy Saving Certificates (ESCs) under the ESS provide incentive funding for large scale energy efficiency projects in NSW. The funding may be accessed yearly in arrears after the energy savings are realized using a Metered Baseline Method (MBM) or in a lump sum using forward creation of up to 10 years using the Project Impact Assessment with Measurement and Verification (PIAM&V) method.   The Sampling Method is a sub-method of PIAM&V that allows for multiple site ESC creation using a sampling method where there is a population of similar ECMs.  This method is the recommended option for Sydney Trains upgrades.

The exact value of ESCs to the project is uncertain as the ESC price at time of creation following the project, and the final quantity to be created are not fully known until the measurements and modelling has been performed. Nevertheless, for this project the potential value is substantial. 

Wattly will oversee the accreditations required to create ESCs and has a range of forward-contracting options to hedge the ESC price that can help to firm up this value, or we can simply trade ESCs on the wholesale market at the time of creation. Our team have extensive experience in the measurement and verification techniques required for this process, and creation of ESCs in NSW.

For this project the following system details have been provided

Per Fleet Energy Savings:	26.6 GWH per year

Upgrade period:  Late 2020 to 2025	

Number of Fleets: 2

Estimated Number of ESCs: Up to 500,000 based on 10 years (discounted) forward creation

The wholesale ESC price has averaged around $22.00 for the past five years

Hence total funding over five years could be as higher as $11 M (minus costs)

Lifetime for the upgraded HVAC equipment & train fleets:  At least 10 years

NSW Energy Saving Scheme - ESS

The NSW ESS reduces electricity consumption by creating financial incentives for businesses to invest in energy savings projects. Energy savings are achieved by Accredited Certificate Providers (ACP) assisting businesses to upgrading or installing more efficient equipment.  The nominated ACP registers a number of Energy Savings Certificates proportional to the energy savings achieved in the upgrade and sells the certificates to Scheme Participants (Energy Retailers such as AGL, Origin, EA, etc.) who have an obligation under the Scheme to meet energy savings targets.

Businesses performing the energy saving upgrades (the original energy savers), must legally nominate the ACP as the energy saver to provide the right to register the Energy Saving Certificates.  

Energy Savings from the upgrade are "brought forward" for up to 10 years of operation and the energy savings for this period are registered as Energy Saving Certificates (ESC).  An ESC represents a nominal MWh (Megawatt hour) of energy saved.

Replaced equipment cannot be reused and must be permanently decommissioned.  This is to ensure that the old inefficient equipment is not used elsewhere after it is removed.

The Independent Pricing and Regulatory Tribunal of NSW (IPART) is both the Scheme Administrator and Scheme Regulator.

The business paying for the energy saving upgrade much nominate (sign the Nomination Form) BEFORE the energy saving upgrade is fully installed (commissioned)

The current legislated end date of the NSW ESS is the end of 2025.  The ESS has strong bipartisan support and the current government is expanding the program and rebrand it as the NSW Energy Security Safeguard.  We expect that the program will be rolled forward to at least 2030.

Experts in M&V Energy Saving Certificates

Our team has generated funding from energy savings certificates across NSW and VIC from a wide range of upgrade projects including.

Train fleet LED Lighting upgrades (using a sampling method)

Ventilation fan upgrades for a large metropolitan road tunnel

Gas, Biogas and Biomass boiler upgrade for many large energy users

Chiller upgrades for a nationwide poultry producer and numerous commercial buildings

Air blower upgrades from Yeast manufacturing

Data Centre upgrades to UPS, HVAC and Chillers at a number of large sites

Swirl Burner and Electric Arc Furnaces efficiency improvements for smelting

Range of measure for national paper, packaging and recycling company

City wide LED street lighting upgrade

ESC creation method recommended

The Wattly team is accredited for a range of methods of ESC creation in NSW, and our role is to optimize the process by selecting the best method(s). 

The PIAM&V Sampling Method will avoid performing Measurement & Verification (M&V) on every train set upgraded.  Note that HVAC has seasonal variation 12 months of data (before and after the upgrade) is the required measurement period to determine the energy savings.

The options for using this upgrade.

Perform Sampling Method M&V on a tranche of trains set to and claiming the energy savings for the population of trains in that tranche.  This method will allow shorter claiming periods and maintain claims under the 50,000 ESC limit

Perform Sampling Method M&V across the entire fleet and claim energy savings for train sets upgraded on a periodic basis (e.g., quarterly).  This method is desirable in that it may reduce the total amount of M&V but will hit limitations on total ESC creation under the current guidelines.

Metered Baseline Method.  This method involves measuring the energy savings at the boundary of the train network and claiming the actual measure energy savings each year in arrears with a regression model accounting for production variations.  This method is likely to be difficult to implement due to an aggregated view of the entire network and the potential for unexplained variations in the network. 

At this stage option 1 above is the preferred option at this stage to ensure fastest time to funds whilst keeping M&V work to a minimum.  As further exploratory work is performed the other options will remain under consideration to ensure the option that is most efficient and provides the highest level of funding for Downer will be pursed.


The diagram below explains graphically how the regression analysis and modelling calculate what the energy usage would have been had the upgrade not occurred.  The energy savings is the difference between the model baseline and the reduced figure that is actual measured.  

Figure 1: How savings are calculated for a ‘Normal Year’ using modelling under the PIAM&V method.

Rolling tranche / batch upgrade 

The timeline below will allow a sampling M&V approach to be used on batches of train sets allowing the certificates to be realized as soon as possible and by limiting the number of trains sets per tranche avoid hitting the 50,000 forward creation limits for a single project.

Offer

Wattly is offering to forward-create ESCs for up to 10 years of energy savings (discounted in accordance with decay factors and the PIAM&V method).  Payment for ESC will be made in following the registration and sale of the ESCs.  This is estimated to be approximately 16 months after the upgrade of the sampled train sets in each tranche.

Payment, Fees and Term

Redacted.

Conditions

* Watttly fees include external auditor fees.

** Government fees have increased by $0.02 every two years

% fees are based on the ESC wholesale price achieved on the sale of the ESCs

All prices are exclusive of GST

Term of Exclusive Engagement:  to 2030 (ESS end date of 2025 expected to be extended)

Payment will be made upon receipt of an invoice with 60-day payment terms.

Wattly reserves the right to terminate the agreement or renegotiate if the data provided is insufficient to build a strong regression model of the energy savings in accordance with the ESS requirements.

ESC Hedging, Sale and Payment

Through Wattly's trading experience and access to the Environment Certificate markets we can offer hedging services for the ESC price.  On agreement with the customer, Wattly will offer hedging services for difference parcels throughout the duration of the project.  This allows for the lock-in of the ESC price for up to 18 months.

Key assumptions

Wattly is nominated as the Accredited Certificate Provider for the purpose of the Energy Savings Scheme

A Nomination Form is signed prior to commissioning and normal operation of the ECM

The project is found to have a statistically valid baseline energy model that can be used to predict future performance 

ESCs will be created when energy use within the measurement boundary has been measured for a period of up to 12 months (though we keep this period as short as possible)

If at any time after the date this Agreement is signed any legislation or scheme rules that apply to this Agreement are repealed, terminated or materially amended (“Change of Law Event”) and as a result no person or entity (including the Regulator) can legally create or Transfer ESCs or the ESC Contract Quantity, then the parties must meet in good faith as soon as practicable after becoming aware of the Change of Law Event to negotiate a replacement arrangement to preserve the parties’ financial position under this Agreement to the extent possible.

        Proposal for ESC creation from PIAM&V
        Prepared for CBS Group Pty Ltd for Downer Sydney Trains HVAC Upgrade

1 Our Understanding
Sydney Trains is planning to implement and commission a range of Energy Conservation Measures (ECM) in
the form of HVAC upgrades across the fleet of trains operating across the Greater Sydney Metropolitan Area.
Downer will be performing these upgrades over a period of 4-5 years. CBS Group is looking to access funding
from the NSW Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) to provide additional capital and reduce the net cost for Downer
throughout the upgrade cycle.

The Energy Saving Certificates (ESCs) under the ESS provide incentive funding for large scale energy efficiency
projects in NSW. The funding may be accessed yearly in arrears after the energy savings are realized using a
Metered Baseline Method (MBM) or in a lump sum using forward creation of up to 10 years using the Project
Impact Assessment with Measurement and Verification (PIAM&V) method. The Sampling Method is a sub-
method of PIAM&V that allows for multiple site ESC creation using a sampling method where there is a
population of similar ECMs. This method is the recommended option for Sydney Trains upgrades.

The exact value of ESCs to the project is uncertain as the ESC price at time of creation following the project,
and the final quantity to be created are not fully known until the measurements and modelling has been
performed. Nevertheless, for this project the potential value is substantial.

Wattly will oversee the accreditations required to create ESCs and has a range of forward-contracting options
to hedge the ESC price that can help to firm up this value, or we can simply trade ESCs on the wholesale
market at the time of creation. Our team have extensive experience in the measurement and verification
techniques required for this process, and creation of ESCs in NSW.

For this project the following system details have been provided

    •    Per Fleet Energy Savings: 26.6 GWH per year
    •    Upgrade period: Late 2020 to 2025
    •    Number of Fleets: 2
    •    Estimated Number of ESCs: Up to 500,000 based on 10 years (discounted) forward creation
    •    The wholesale ESC price has averaged around $22.00 for the past five years
    •    Hence total funding over five years could be as higher as $11 M (minus costs)
    •    Lifetime for the upgraded HVAC equipment & train fleets: At least 10 years


1.1 NSW Energy Saving Scheme - ESS
The NSW ESS reduces electricity consumption by creating financial incentives for businesses to invest in energy
savings projects. Energy savings are achieved by Accredited Certificate Providers (ACP) assisting businesses to
upgrading or installing more efficient equipment. The nominated ACP registers a number of Energy Savings
Certificates proportional to the energy savings achieved in the upgrade and sells the certificates to Scheme
Participants (Energy Retailers such as AGL, Origin, EA, etc.) who have an obligation under the Scheme to meet
energy savings targets.

Businesses performing the energy saving upgrades (the original energy savers), must legally nominate the ACP
as the energy saver to provide the right to register the Energy Saving Certificates.

Energy Savings from the upgrade are "brought forward" for up to 10 years of operation and the energy savings
for this period are registered as Energy Saving Certificates (ESC). An ESC represents a nominal MWh
(Megawatt hour) of energy saved.

Replaced equipment cannot be reused and must be permanently decommissioned. This is to ensure that the
old inefficient equipment is not used elsewhere after it is removed.



                                                                                                         Page 2 of 11
COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENCE




The Independent Pricing and Regulatory Tribunal of NSW (IPART) is both the Scheme Administrator and
Scheme Regulator.

The business paying for the energy saving upgrade much nominate (sign the Nomination Form) BEFORE the
energy saving upgrade is fully installed (commissioned)

The current legislated end date of the NSW ESS is the end of 2025. The ESS has strong bipartisan support and
the current government is expanding the program and rebrand it as the NSW Energy Security Safeguard. We
expect that the program will be rolled forward to at least 2030.


1.2 Experts in M&V Energy Saving Certificates
Our team has generated funding from energy savings certificates across NSW and VIC from a wide range of
upgrade projects including.

    •    Train fleet LED Lighting upgrades (using a sampling method)
    •    Ventilation fan upgrades for a large metropolitan road tunnel
    •    Gas, Biogas and Biomass boiler upgrade for many large energy users
    •    Chiller upgrades for a nationwide poultry producer and numerous commercial buildings
    •    Air blower upgrades from Yeast manufacturing
    •    Data Centre upgrades to UPS, HVAC and Chillers at a number of large sites
    •    Swirl Burner and Electric Arc Furnaces efficiency improvements for smelting
    •    Range of measure for national paper, packaging and recycling company
    •    City wide LED street lighting upgrade



2 ESC creation method recommended
The Wattly team is accredited for a range of methods of ESC creation in NSW, and our role is to optimize the
process by selecting the best method(s).

The PIAM&V Sampling Method will avoid performing Measurement & Verification (M&V) on every train set
upgraded. Note that HVAC has seasonal variation 12 months of data (before and after the upgrade) is the
required measurement period to determine the energy savings.

The options for using this upgrade.

    1.   Perform Sampling Method M&V on a tranche of trains set to and claiming the energy savings for the
         population of trains in that tranche. This method will allow shorter claiming periods and maintain
         claims under the 50,000 ESC limit
    2.   Perform Sampling Method M&V across the entire fleet and claim energy savings for train sets
         upgraded on a periodic basis (e.g., quarterly). This method is desirable in that it may reduce the total
         amount of M&V but will hit limitations on total ESC creation under the current guidelines.
    3.   Metered Baseline Method. This method involves measuring the energy savings at the boundary of
         the train network and claiming the actual measure energy savings each year in arrears with a
         regression model accounting for production variations. This method is likely to be difficult to
         implement due to an aggregated view of the entire network and the potential for unexplained
         variations in the network.

At this stage option 1 above is the preferred option at this stage to ensure fastest time to funds whilst keeping
M&V work to a minimum. As further exploratory work is performed the other options will remain under
consideration to ensure the option that is most efficient and provides the highest level of funding for Downer



                                                                                                         Page 3 of 11
COMMERCIAL IN CONFIDENCE




will be pursed.

 PIAM&V - Project Impact                   Uses metering around the boundary of

 Assessment with Measurement and           the Energy Conservation Measure (ECM)
                                                                                        (10y discounted)
 Verification                              to measure and verify savings.

                                           Uses metering around the site (or sub-

                                           area) to measure and verify savings.

                      Gas Assessments for mining project development applications; Energy Audits of
                      manufacturing facilities, food processing facilities, commercial office buildings, hospitals
and aged care facilities; energy simulation modelling for BCA and NABERS Energy assessments; energy audits
of large industrial plants and food processing facilities for the Energy Efficiency Opportunity Program; and
Energy Management Program implementation.

5.1.7 Andrew Dawkins (B. Eng (Mechatronics), CMVP)
                    Andrew joined Wattly having undertaken postgraduate studies in Sustainable Energy.
                    Andrew is passionate about sustainability, climate change, and sees energy efficiency and
                    renewable technologies as key to Australia's transition to a sustainable future. Hailing
                    from Western Australia, Andrew has worked in the past in oil & gas and mining sectors.
                    Dissatisfaction working for these industries prompted him to relocate to Melbourne in
                    2014 in order to pursue an Engineering Masters of Sustainable Energy at RMIT University.

 BACKGROUND & Acknowledgements
      A. Downer carries on a business that at times involves advice and services on the procurement,
         design, installation, servicing and/or maintenance of efficient end-user equipment by their
         Customers which may be entitled for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates)
         under the Environmental Schemes.

      B. Downer and CBS Group have agreed to partner in the novel application of Environmental
         Schemes to mobile plant. A particular application of this partnership is the pursuit of Energy
         Saving Certificates from the NSW Energy Savings Scheme resulting from modifications to the
         Sydney Trains SGT and Waratah fleets maintained by Downer.

      C. Downer wishes to engage CBS Group to provide the services required to generate value from
         Energy Savings Certificates under the NSW Energy Savings Scheme, and CBS Group has
         agreed to provide the Services on the terms set out in this Agreement. CBS Group will work
         with Downer to collect evidence. Downer will receive payments based on the successful
         registration of certificates for the nominated projects (Scheme Activities)

      D. CBS Group has represented to Downer that it has the skill, experience, personnel, material
         and capacity to supply the Services in accordance with this Agreement.

 OPERATIVE PART
1.      DEFINITIONS
1.1     In this Agreement:
Agreement means this agreement (including any schedules, appendices or annexures);
Arbitration is a method of dispute resolution whereby a dispute raised under clause 19 is finally
resolved as follows:
        (a)      the arbitration will be finally resolved under the Resolution Institute Arbitration Rules;
        (b)      there will be one arbitrator, appointed in accordance with the Resolution Institute
                 Arbitration Rules;
        (c)      the arbitrator must give written reasons for their award;
        (d)      the seat of the arbitration will be Sydney, New South Wales;
        (e)      the arbitration will be conducted in English.
Business Day means Monday to Friday excluding public holidays;
CBS Group Failure Event Date means 5pm on the 15th day after the final Monthly Project

Management Fee was paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with Schedule Item 3 and 4.

                 Failure Event Date.
CBS Group Tools means CBS Group ’s systems, data collection tools and record keeping tools
designed to assist Downer to meet obligations under the Scheme, including but not limited to
workflows, forms, checklists, spreadsheets, software and related documents;
CBS Group Workbooks means the Workbooks, checklists, training materials, and guidelines
authored by or on behalf of CBS Group as published and updated from time to time, setting out,
without limitation, the Accreditations, the Requirements, the Specifications, the Approved Products
and the Documentation;
Certificates means the Environmental Products, as appropriate;
Certificate Registration means the process of registering ownership of the Certificates with the
Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) Registry following notification by the ESS Registry that the Certificates
are available for registration.

Customer means the energy user (end-customer) to whom Downer provides the installation of
efficient end-user equipment (Approved Products) in accordance with the Scheme;
Certificates see Environmental Products

Effective Date means the date of execution of this Agreement by all Parties;

Environmental Products (Certificates) means certificates created under the Environmental
Schemes, including but not limited to ESCs, VEECs, STCs, LGCs, and ACCUs as relevant;
Environmental Scheme (Schemes) means each of the Government Energy Efficiency, Renewable,
and Carbon Abatement Schemes; including, but not limited to the ESS, VEU, REES, EEIS, RET, CFI,
as relevant; ESS (NSW Energy Saving Scheme), VEET (Victorian Energy Efficiency Target), REES
(South Australian Retailer Energy Efficient Scheme), EEIS (ACT Energy Efficiency Improvement
Scheme), RET (Federal Renewable Energy Target), CFI (Federal Carbon Farming Initiative).
Evidence means all data and documents necessary to satisfy the requirements of the Energy Savings
Scheme.

that Downer and CBS Group agree to work exclusively with each other for the purpose of Environment

Relevant Person means any officer, employee, consultant, representative, subcontractor, sub-

2.      FUNDAMENTAL TERMS
2.1     Downer engages CBS Group to:
        (a)      provide the Services to Downer, and meet any applicable Legal Requirements and
                 Downers health and safety policies, procedures and requirements; and
        (b)      make payments to Downer as required by and in the manner specified in Schedule Item 4;
2.2     Downer agrees to satisfy Downer’s Obligations for the Term on the terms of this Agreement;
2.3     CBS Group is providing the Services as an independent contractor. CBS Group (including its
        employees and agents) is not in partnership or in a joint venture relationship with Downer and is
        not an employee, agent or servant of Downer. CBS Group will not and will ensure that its
        employees and agents to not, bind or commit, or purport to bind or commit, Downer in any way.
3.      DOWNER’S OBLIGATIONS
During the Term:
3.1     Downer must:
        (a)      pay the Fees for Services and Reimbursable Expenses when due; and
        (b)      give reasonable assistance, information, instructions and access to Downer’s premises
                 as reasonably required by CBS Group to enable CBS Group to provide the Services.
3.2     Downer will use best endeavours to:
        (a)      facilitate the support of the Customer to assist in meeting the requirements of the
                 Environmental Scheme as advised by CBS from time to time;
        (b)      provide relevant technical information and support for the Project delivered to the
                 Customer in order to assist in meeting the requirements of the Environmental Scheme;
        (c)      upon request by CBS Group or a Scheme auditor, provide access to Documentation,
                 Evidence and other information relevant to Downer’s Obligations and the Environmental
                 Scheme for the purposes of inspection or audit, and provide its full assistance to CBS
                 Group and the Scheme auditor in any inspection or audit or audit activity or the
                 implementation of any direction arising from an audit finding;
3.3     Downer must not:
        (a)      use the CBS Group Tools other than in satisfying Downer’s Obligations in accordance
                 with this Agreement;
        (b)      use the CBS Group Tools to create Certificates itself or assist third parties to create
                 Certificates;
        (c)      reverse engineer any of the CBS Group Tools; or
        (d)      disclose to third parties the CBS Group Tools or the details of any CBS Group Tools.
4.      CBS GROUP WARRANTIES
4.1      CBS Group acknowledges and agrees that in providing the Services;
        (a)      it has the requisite skills, experience and expertise and has reviewed, investigated,
                 analysed and understood all of the information provided to it; and
        (b)      it will at all times comply with all applicable Legal Requirements.
4.2     CBS Group must co-operate with Downer and any of its employees or other consultants
        engaged by Downer to complete the Services and co-ordinate the performance of the Services
        with any services being performed by other Consultants.

        (a)      are completed and delivered to Downer before the conclusion of the Agreement Term or
                 at such other times as may be specified in this Agreement;
        (b)      are fit for the Intended Purpose;
        (c)      comply with all applicable Legal Requirements; and
        (d)      do not infringe any General Intellectual Property Rights of any person.
4.4     CBS Group must:
        (a)      ensure that each person engaged by CBS Group would not, in doing the work for which
                 they are engaged, be an Illegal Worker;
        (b)      remove, or cause to be removed, any Illegal Worker from any involvement in carrying out
                 the Services; and
        (c)      when requested in writing, provide evidence within 14 days that it has taken all
                 reasonable steps to ensure that it has complied with, and is complying with, its obligations
                 under this clause 4.4.
                 Illegal Worker means a person who is an Unlawful Non-Citizen who is working without a
                 visa, or a Non-Citizen who is performing work in breach of a Visa Work Condition;
                 Non-Citizen has the same meaning as under the Migration Act 1958 (Cth);
                 Unlawful Non-Citizen has the same meaning as under the Migration Act 1958 (Cth); and
                 Visa Work Condition means a condition (as set out in Schedule 8 of the Migration
                 Regulations 1994 (Cth) attached to a visa restricting the work that the Non-Citizen may do
                 in Australia.
5.      LICENCE OF CBS GROUP TOOLS
5.1     Subject to the provisions set out in clause 16.1(a) of this Agreement, CBS Group licences the
        CBS Group Tools to Downer during the Term of this Agreement.
5.2     Downer must only use the CBS Group Tools for the purposes of satisfying Downer’s Obligations
        under the terms of this Agreement.
5.3     CBS Group may, as it considers necessary, maintain, delete or modify the CBS Group Tools
        from time to time.
6.      PAYMENT AND VARIATIONS
6.1     Payments to CBS Group
        (a)      CBS Group must submit claims for payment of the Fees for Services strictly at the time
                 stated in, and strictly in accordance with Schedule Item 4 of this Agreement. CBS Group
                 is not entitled to make, and must not make, any claim for payment of the Fees for
                 Services otherwise than in accordance with Schedule Item 4. Each claim for payment of
                 the Fees for Services must be accompanied by details in support of the claim, certificates
                 of currency for each policy of insurance required to be held by CBS Group and such other
                 information as Downer may reasonably request.
        (b)      Within 10 Business Days of receipt of a claim for payment made by CBS Group in
                 accordance with clause 6.1, Downer will issue to CBS Group a payment schedule setting
                 out the amount it proposes to pay to CBS Group and the reasons for any difference.
        (c)      Provided that it has received a tax invoice for the amount assessed pursuant to clause
                 (b), subject to clause 6.1(f), Downer will pay to CBS Group the amount of the assessment
                 by the time for payment identified in Schedule Item 4.
        (d)      The Parties may mutually agree to a Variation to this Agreement, with such Variations
                 executed by both Parties in writing.
        (e)      Subject to clause 6.1(f), where the Parties have agreed a Variation in accordance with
                 clause (d), the adjustment (if any) to the Fees for Services will be as follows:
                 (i)      to the extent that the Parties have agreed the amount, then the amount agreed; or
                 (ii)     otherwise, using prices included in this Agreement and otherwise arriving at a
                          reasonable amount having regard to any increase or decrease in the costs that have
                          been or will be incurred by CBS Group as a direct result of the Variation.

7.      FAILURE EVENT
7.1     If a CBS Group Failure Event occurs, CBS Group must pay to Downer the amounts as specified
        in Schedule Item 4.
8.      DOWNER RESOURCES
8.1     Downer must ensure that it has the necessary resources and staff to satisfy Downer’s
        Obligations.
9.      GENERAL RELATIONSHIP
9.1     The Parties are independent of each other and no agency, employment, partnership or other
        relationship will exist between the Parties. Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed or
        construed to render either Party liable for any of the other Party’s costs obligations or
        expenditures pursuant to any relevant employment laws.
9.2     Downer retains the right to enter into any contract with any other person or persons, company or
        firm to perform services similar to those contemplated by this Agreement, provided that they do
        not interfere with Downer’s ability to comply with this Agreement.
10.     INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY & DOWNER INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
10.1 Downer has no proprietary right or interest in the Intellectual Property.
10.2 Downer must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world the
     Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from or
     similar to the Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.
10.3 Any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from any party of the information
     comprised in the Intellectual Property whether attributable in whole or in part to Downer, will
     become and remain the property of CBS Group.
10.4 CBS Group has no proprietary right or interest in Downer Intellectual Property.
10.5 CBS Group must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world
     Downer Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from
     or similar to the Downer Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.
10.6 Any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from any Party of the information
     comprised in the Downer Intellectual Property whether attributable in whole or in part to CBS
     Group, will become and remain the property of Downer.
11.     GST
11.1 Unless otherwise expressly stated, prices, or other sums payable or consideration to be
     provided under or in accordance with this Agreement are exclusive of GST.
11.2 If a Party makes a taxable supply under or in connection with this Agreement, the other Party
     must pay to the supplier at the same time, and in addition to the GST-exclusive consideration,
     an amount equal to the GST payable on that supply.
11.3 The supplier must, as a precondition to the payment of GST, give the other Party a tax invoice
     by the end of the month in which the supply is made.
11.4 If an adjustment event arises in connection with a supply made under this Agreement, the
     supplier must give the other Party an adjustment note in accordance with the GST law.
11.5 If this Agreement requires one Party to pay for, reimburse or contribute to any expense, loss or
     outgoing suffered or incurred by the other Party, the amount required to be paid, reimbursed or

        contributed by the first Party will be reduced by the amount of input tax credits (if any) to which
        the other Party is entitled in respect of the reimbursable expense.
11.6 In this clause 11:
        (a)      words and expressions which have a defined meaning in the A New Tax System (Goods
                 and Services Tax) Act 1999 (Cth) (GST Act) have the same meaning as in the GST Act;
                 and
        (b)      if that GST Act does not exist for any reason, GST Act means any Act imposing or relating
                 to the imposition or administration of a goods and services tax in Australia and any
                 regulation made under that Act.
12.     PROGRESS REPORTS AND MEETINGS
12.1 Upon reasonable request by Downer, but not less than on a monthly basis, the Parties must
     participate in a meeting to review and discuss the following (for the avoidance of doubt the list
     below is not exhaustive):
     (a)    CBS Group’s program for the provision of the Services;
     (b)    CBS Group’s progress in supplying the Services, relative to the dates by which they are
            required to be performed under this Agreement;
     (c)    Downer’s compliance with its obligations and any issues that Downer can reasonably be
            required to rectify to allow CBS Group to perform the Services;
     (d)    any additional projects that may be added to Schedule Item 6 to generate value for both
            Parties; and
     (e)    any other matter related to this Agreement which is notified to CBS Group by Downer from
            time to time.
12.2 The Parties must record and maintain minutes of all meetings held in accordance with clause
     12.1, which must record at a minimum:
     (a)    the date the meeting was held;
     (b)    the names of the individuals who attended the meeting;
     (c)    any actions agreed to be performed by the Parties arising from the meeting; and
     (d)    the agreed timeframe to perform those actions recorded in accordance with clause 12.2(c).
12.3 CBS Group must deliver a report to Downer whenever CBS Group considers the services will not
     be provided within the time prescribed by this Agreement.
12.4 Upon request by Downer, CBS Group and Downer, at Downer’s discretion, must meet to review
     the progress of the performance of this Agreement including the matters described in clause 12.1.
13.     INSURANCE
13.1 Unless Downer agrees otherwise in writing, CBS Group must maintain:
     (a)     worker's compensation insurance as required by applicable law where the Services are
             provided;
     (b)     professional indemnity insurance providing coverage for CBS Group’s professional
             responsibilities to Downer with an insured amount of $[10,000,000]; and
     (c)     public and products liability insurance with an insured amount of $20,000,000 for any one
             occurrence, which covers the liability of CBS Group in respect of:
                    (A)    bodily injury to any third party;
                    (B)    third party property damage; and
                    (C) advertising liability
13.2 CBS Group must ensure that the public and products liability insurance:
     (a)     Provides that Downer is protected by a “Principal’s” indemnity extension covering Downer’s
             vicarious liability for the acts or omissions of other “Named Insureds” under such policy;
             and
     (b)     Includes a cross liability clause in which the insurer agrees that the policy applies as if a
             separate policy was issued to each “Named Insured” (with the exception of limits of liability).
13.3 CBS Group must ensure that each policy required by this Agreement is in force prior to the
     Effective Date and maintained for the duration of the Agreement Term, except for professional
     indemnity insurance, which shall be maintained for 6 years after the conclusion of the Agreement
     Term.
13.4 CBS Group must, when requested by Downer, promptly satisfy Downer that each contract of
     insurance it is required to procure under this clause 13 is current by providing to Downer
     certificates of currency or other evidence reasonably required by Downer.

14.1 Both Parties must:
