## FINAL_CBS Group Draft Contract 2021.02.17 Clean.docx

*File: `Downer CBS Contract/FINAL_CBS Group Draft Contract 2021.02.17 Clean.docx`*

CBS Group Pty Ltd and Downer

Environmental Energy Finance

Master Services Agreement

Master Agreement 

Parties

CBS GROUP* PTY LTD  ABN 20164870080 of Level 3, 480 Collins Street, MELBOURNE VIC 3000 (CBS Group)

DOWNER* EDI RAIL PTY LTD ACN 000 002 031 of Triniti Business Campus, 39 Delhi Road, NSW 2113 (Downer)

*Includes all agents, parties, contractors and suppliers acting for named Principals

Downer carries on a business that at times involves advice and services on the procurement, design, installation, servicing and/or maintenance of efficient end-user equipment by their Customers which may be entitled for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates) under the Environmental Schemes.

Downer and CBS Group have agreed to partner in the novel application of Environmental Schemes to mobile plant.  A particular application of this partnership is the pursuit of Energy Saving Certificates from the NSW Energy Savings Scheme resulting from modifications to the Sydney Trains SGT and Waratah fleets maintained by Downer.

Downer wishes to engage CBS Group to provide the services required to generate value from Energy Savings Certificates under the NSW Energy Savings Scheme, and CBS Group has agreed to provide the Services on the terms set out in this Agreement.  CBS Group will work with Downer to collect evidence. Downer will receive payments based on the successful registration of certificates for the nominated projects (Scheme Activities)

CBS Group has represented to Downer that it has the skill, experience, personnel, material and capacity to supply the Services in accordance with this Agreement.

DEFINITIONS

In this Agreement:

Agreement means this agreement (including any schedules, appendices or annexures);

Arbitration is a method of dispute resolution whereby a dispute raised under clause 19 is finally resolved as follows:

the arbitration will be finally resolved under the Resolution Institute Arbitration Rules;

there will be one arbitrator, appointed in accordance with the Resolution Institute Arbitration Rules;

the arbitrator must give written reasons for their award;

the seat of the arbitration will be Sydney, New South Wales;

the arbitration will be conducted in English.

Business Day means Monday to Friday excluding public holidays;

CBS Group Failure Event Date means 5pm on the 15th day after the final Monthly Project Management Fee was paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with Schedule Item 3 and 4.

CBS Group Failure Event occurs when: 

Downer has satisfied its obligations to perform any agreed actions recorded in accordance with clause 12.2(c) and 12.2(d); and

CBS Group fails to obtain and sell any Environmental Products before the CBS Group Failure Event Date.

CBS Group Tools means CBS Group ’s systems, data collection tools and record keeping tools designed to assist Downer to meet obligations under the Scheme, including but not limited to workflows, forms, checklists, spreadsheets, software and related documents;

CBS Group Workbooks means the Workbooks, checklists, training materials, and guidelines authored by or on behalf of CBS Group as published and updated from time to time, setting out, without limitation, the Accreditations, the Requirements, the Specifications, the Approved Products and the Documentation;

Certificates means the Environmental Products, as appropriate;

Certificate Registration means the process of registering ownership of the Certificates with the Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) Registry following notification by the ESS Registry that the Certificates are available for registration.

Certificate Sale means the sale of registered Certificates to scheme participants.

Confidential Information means:

all business and financial information, sales and supply details, marketing strategies, customer and supplier listings, business listings, pricing information, information concerning each respective Party or its customers and business associates; 

all (if any) other information relating to each Party or its respective business which, by its nature, places or potentially places that Party at an advantage over its present or future business competitors.  To avoid ambiguity, in the case of CBS Group, this will include, but not be limited to, the CBS Workbooks and CBS Tools, processes and workflows; and

any other information that would otherwise at law be considered secret or confidential information of respective Party;

whether or not marked "Confidential" BUT does not include information which:

at the time of first disclosure by one Party to the other Party is already in the public domain;

after disclosure by a Party, becomes part of the public domain otherwise than by disclosure in breach of the terms of this Agreement; and

the receiving Party can prove to the disclosing Party that the information was in its possession before the time of first disclosure by or discovery from the other Party.

Customer means the energy user (end-customer) to whom Downer provides the installation of efficient end-user equipment (Approved Products) in accordance with the Scheme;

Certificates see Environmental Products

Dispute means any dispute or difference between the Parties to this Agreement arising from or in connection with this Agreement including any dispute or difference arising as a consequence of a failure by CBS Group to reach agreement on any matter;

Documentation means CBS Group’s checklists and forms relevant to the Scheme, the Requirements and the Specifications, as provided to Downer and updated from time to time;

Downer Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, and all copyright, trademark rights, design rights, patent rights and confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to Downer or that relates to Downer’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it; 

Downer’s Obligations means the obligations of Downer prescribed in clause 3, and such further services agreed from time to time;

Effective Date means the date of execution of this Agreement by all Parties;

Energy Savings Nomination means an approved Energy Savings Scheme nomination form completed by the Original Energy Saver.

Environmental Products (Certificates) means certificates created under the Environmental Schemes, including but not limited to ESCs, VEECs, STCs, LGCs, and ACCUs as relevant;

Environmental Scheme (Schemes) means each of the Government Energy Efficiency, Renewable, and Carbon Abatement Schemes; including, but not limited to the ESS, VEU, REES, EEIS, RET, CFI, as relevant; ESS (NSW Energy Saving Scheme), VEET (Victorian Energy Efficiency Target), REES (South Australian Retailer Energy Efficient Scheme), EEIS (ACT Energy Efficiency Improvement Scheme), RET (Federal Renewable Energy Target), CFI (Federal Carbon Farming Initiative).

Evidence means all data and documents necessary to satisfy the requirements of the Energy Savings Scheme.  

Exclusivity Agreements means a list of customers, opportunities, projects or other group of activities that Downer and CBS Group agree to work exclusively with each other for the purpose of Environment Certificate creation under the Environment Schemes

Fees for Services means the amount payable by Downer to CBS Group as set out in Schedule Item 3;

GST means any tax imposed on the supply of goods or services, consumption or value added tax, including without limitation any tax imposed in the A New Tax System (Goods and Services Tax) Act (Cth);

General Intellectual Property Right means all present and future rights conferred by law (whether statute, common law or equity) in or in relation to copyright trademarks, designs, patents, inventions and other results of intellectual activity in any field whether or not registrable, registered or patentable.

Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, the CBS Group Workbooks, the CBS Group Tools, software, workflow, and all copyright, trade mark rights, design rights, patent rights and confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to CBS Group or that relates to the CBS Group ’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it;

Intended Purpose means the purpose or purposes for which the Services are intended, including any purpose(s) which is described in, or may be reasonably inferred from, this Agreement and any purpose notified by Downer to CBS Group (whether before or after the execution of this Agreement);

Legal Requirements means the rights conferred on authors of works by Part IX of the Copyright Act 1968 (Cth) and all present and future rights of integrity of authorship, rights of attribution of authorship, rights not to have authorship falsely attributed, rights to alter or delete all or part of the Services, rights to use the Services or an adaption of the Services and rights of a similar nature conferred by statute anywhere in the world;

Licence means the CBS Group ’s licence of CBS Group Tools and CBS Group Workbooks to Downer for the purposes of this Agreement as set out in clause 5 of this Agreement;

Original Energy Saver (OES) means the party that is paying or has paid for the activities that increase the energy efficiency without reducing production or service levels, and which will become the Original Energy Saver (OES) upon signing the approved Energy Savings Nomination form.

Probity Event means any event or thing which occurs before or after the date of this Agreement which:

has a material adverse effect on, or on the perception of, the character, integrity or honesty of a Relevant Person;

relates to a Relevant Person and has or may have a material adverse effect on the public interest or public confidence in the Services; or

involves a material failure of a Relevant Person or subcontractor to achieve or maintain:

reasonable standards of ethical behaviour

the avoidance of conflicts of interest which will have a material adverse effect on the ability of the Relevant Person or subcontractor (as applicable) to carry out and observe its obligations in connection with the Services; or

other standards of conduct that would otherwise be expected of a Party involved in a project of the same nature or type as the Project;

Project means the projects specified in Appendix D on which Downer and CBS have agreed to work exclusively together for the purposes of securing Energy Saving Credits. 

Reimbursable Expenses means expenses that are in addition to the Fees for Services for which Downer has agreed in writing to reimburse CBS Group.  If Downer has not provided prior written approval I relation to a Reimbursable Expense, Downer will have no liability for (and CBS Group will not be entitled to any payment for) that Reimbursable Expense.

Relevant Person means any officer, employee, consultant, representative, subcontractor, sub-subcontractor or agent of CBS Group.

Requirements means any and all processes, procedures, duties and obligations that apply to the Scheme and as otherwise set out in the CBS Group Workbooks;

Scheme see Environmental Scheme

Services means the services provided by CBS Group prescribed in Schedule Item 2;

Specifications means any and all specifications, standards and terms that set out the minimum standards required under the Scheme, and as otherwise set out in the CBS Group Workbooks;

Term means the period from the Effective Date until this Agreement is ended in accordance with clause 15; 

Variation means:

the inclusion of additional services as Services to be supplied by CBS Group under this Agreement;

a change in the character or quality of the Services to be supplied under this Agreement; 

an increase or decrease in the quantity of Services to be supplied under this Agreement; or

the inclusion of additional obligations as Downer Obligations to be satisfied by Downer under this Agreement.

FUNDAMENTAL TERMS

Downer engages CBS Group to:

provide the Services to Downer, and meet any applicable Legal Requirements and Downers health and safety policies, procedures and requirements; and

make payments to Downer as required by and in the manner specified in Schedule Item 4;

Downer agrees to satisfy Downer’s Obligations for the Term on the terms of this Agreement;

CBS Group is providing the Services as an independent contractor.  CBS Group (including its employees and agents) is not in partnership or in a joint venture relationship with Downer and is not an employee, agent or servant of Downer.  CBS Group will not and will ensure that its employees and agents to not, bind or commit, or purport to bind or commit, Downer in any way.

DOWNER’S OBLIGATIONS

During the Term:

Downer must:

pay the Fees for Services and Reimbursable Expenses when due; and

give reasonable assistance, information, instructions and access to Downer’s premises as reasonably required by CBS Group to enable CBS Group to provide the Services. 

Downer will use best endeavours to:

facilitate the support of the Customer to assist in meeting the requirements of the Environmental Scheme as advised by CBS from time to time; 

provide relevant technical information and support for the Project delivered to the Customer in order to assist in meeting the requirements of the Environmental Scheme;

upon request by CBS Group or a Scheme auditor, provide access to Documentation, Evidence and other information relevant to Downer’s Obligations and the Environmental Scheme for the purposes of inspection or audit, and provide its full assistance to CBS Group and the Scheme auditor in any inspection or audit or audit activity or the implementation of any direction arising from an audit finding;

Downer must not:

use the CBS Group Tools other than in satisfying Downer’s Obligations in accordance with this Agreement;

use the CBS Group Tools to create Certificates itself or assist third parties to create Certificates;

reverse engineer any of the CBS Group Tools; or

disclose to third parties the CBS Group Tools or the details of any CBS Group Tools.

CBS GROUP WARRANTIES

 CBS Group acknowledges and agrees that in providing the Services;

it has the requisite skills, experience and expertise and has reviewed, investigated, analysed and understood all of the information provided to it; and

it will at all times comply with all applicable Legal Requirements.

CBS Group must co-operate with Downer and any of its employees or other consultants engaged by Downer to complete the Services and co-ordinate the performance of the Services with any services being performed by other Consultants.

CBS Group must ensure that any Deliverables:

are completed and delivered to Downer before the conclusion of the Agreement Term or at such other times as may be specified in this Agreement;

are fit for the Intended Purpose;

comply with all applicable Legal Requirements; and

do not infringe any General Intellectual Property Rights of any person.

CBS Group must:

ensure that each person engaged by CBS Group would not, in doing the work for which they are engaged, be an Illegal Worker;

remove, or cause to be removed, any Illegal Worker from any involvement in carrying out the Services; and

when requested in writing, provide evidence within 14 days that it has taken all reasonable steps to ensure that it has complied with, and is complying with, its obligations under this clause 4.4.

Illegal Worker means a person who is an Unlawful Non-Citizen who is working without a visa, or a Non-Citizen who is performing work in breach of a Visa Work Condition;

Non-Citizen has the same meaning as under the Migration Act 1958 (Cth);

Unlawful Non-Citizen has the same meaning as under the Migration Act 1958 (Cth); and

Visa Work Condition means a condition (as set out in Schedule 8 of the Migration Regulations 1994 (Cth) attached to a visa restricting the work that the Non-Citizen may do in Australia.

LICENCE OF CBS GROUP TOOLS

Subject to the provisions set out in clause 16.1(a) of this Agreement, CBS Group licences the CBS Group Tools to Downer during the Term of this Agreement.

Downer must only use the CBS Group Tools for the purposes of satisfying Downer’s Obligations under the terms of this Agreement.

CBS Group may, as it considers necessary, maintain, delete or modify the CBS Group Tools from time to time.

PAYMENT AND VARIATIONS

Payments to CBS Group

CBS Group must submit claims for payment of the Fees for Services strictly at the time stated in, and strictly in accordance with Schedule Item 4 of this Agreement.  CBS Group is not entitled to make, and must not make, any claim for payment of the Fees for Services otherwise than in accordance with Schedule Item 4.  Each claim for payment of the Fees for Services must be accompanied by details in support of the claim, certificates of currency for each policy of insurance required to be held by CBS Group and such other information as Downer may reasonably request.

Within 10 Business Days of receipt of a claim for payment made by CBS Group in accordance with clause 6.1, Downer will issue to CBS Group a payment schedule setting out the amount it proposes to pay to CBS Group and the reasons for any difference.

Provided that it has received a tax invoice for the amount assessed pursuant to clause (b), subject to clause 6.1(f), Downer will pay to CBS Group the amount of the assessment by the time for payment identified in Schedule Item 4.

The Parties may mutually agree to a Variation to this Agreement, with such Variations executed by both Parties in writing.

Subject to clause 6.1(f), where the Parties have agreed a Variation in accordance with clause (d), the adjustment (if any) to the Fees for Services will be as follows:

to the extent that the Parties have agreed the amount, then the amount agreed; or

otherwise, using prices included in this Agreement and otherwise arriving at a reasonable amount having regard to any increase or decrease in the costs that have been or will be incurred by CBS Group as a direct result of the Variation.

Downer may deduct from any amount due to CBS Group (including in respect of a payment claim) any sum which Downer asserts is or may become payable by CBS Group to Downer, in connection with this Agreement.  Nothing in this clause will affect the right of Downer to recover from CBS Group the whole of any such sum or any balance that remains owing. 

Payments to Downer (Certificate Sale)

Upon the successful sale of Environmental Products (Certificates) by CBS Group, CBS Group must, within 1 Business Day, remit to Downer the proceeds of sales in accordance with Schedule Item 4;

FAILURE EVENT

If a CBS Group Failure Event occurs, CBS Group must pay to Downer the amounts as specified in Schedule Item 4.

DOWNER RESOURCES

Downer must ensure that it has the necessary resources and staff to satisfy Downer’s Obligations.

GENERAL RELATIONSHIP

The Parties are independent of each other and no agency, employment, partnership or other relationship will exist between the Parties. Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed or construed to render either Party liable for any of the other Party’s costs obligations or expenditures pursuant to any relevant employment laws.

Downer retains the right to enter into any contract with any other person or persons, company or firm to perform services similar to those contemplated by this Agreement, provided that they do not interfere with Downer’s ability to comply with this Agreement.

INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY & DOWNER INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

Downer has no proprietary right or interest in the Intellectual Property.

Downer must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world the Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from or similar to the Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.

Any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from any party of the information comprised in the Intellectual Property whether attributable in whole or in part to Downer, will become and remain the property of CBS Group.

CBS Group has no proprietary right or interest in Downer Intellectual Property.

CBS Group must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world Downer Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from or similar to the Downer Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.

Any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from any Party of the information comprised in the Downer Intellectual Property whether attributable in whole or in part to CBS Group, will become and remain the property of Downer.

GST

Unless otherwise expressly stated, prices, or other sums payable or consideration to be provided under or in accordance with this Agreement are exclusive of GST.

If a Party makes a taxable supply under or in connection with this Agreement, the other Party must pay to the supplier at the same time, and in addition to the GST-exclusive consideration, an amount equal to the GST payable on that supply.

The supplier must, as a precondition to the payment of GST, give the other Party a tax invoice by the end of the month in which the supply is made.

If an adjustment event arises in connection with a supply made under this Agreement, the supplier must give the other Party an adjustment note in accordance with the GST law.

If this Agreement requires one Party to pay for, reimburse or contribute to any expense, loss or outgoing suffered or incurred by the other Party, the amount required to be paid, reimbursed or contributed by the first Party will be reduced by the amount of input tax credits (if any) to which the other Party is entitled in respect of the reimbursable expense.

In this clause 11:

words and expressions which have a defined meaning in the A New Tax System (Goods and Services Tax) Act 1999 (Cth) (GST Act) have the same meaning as in the GST Act; and

if that GST Act does not exist for any reason, GST Act means any Act imposing or relating to the imposition or administration of a goods and services tax in Australia and any regulation made under that Act.

PROGRESS REPORTS AND MEETINGS

Upon reasonable request by Downer, but not less than on a monthly basis, the Parties must participate in a meeting to review and discuss the following (for the avoidance of doubt the list below is not exhaustive):

CBS Group’s program for the provision of the Services;

CBS Group’s progress in supplying the Services, relative to the dates by which they are required to be performed under this Agreement; 

Downer’s compliance with its obligations and any issues that Downer can reasonably be required to rectify to allow CBS Group to perform the Services;

any additional projects that may be added to Schedule Item 6 to generate value for both Parties; and

any other matter related to this Agreement which is notified to CBS Group by Downer from time to time.

The Parties must record and maintain minutes of all meetings held in accordance with clause 12.1, which must record at a minimum:

the date the meeting was held;

the names of the individuals who attended the meeting; 

any actions agreed to be performed by the Parties arising from the meeting; and

the agreed timeframe to perform those actions recorded in accordance with clause 12.2(c).

CBS Group must deliver a report to Downer whenever CBS Group considers the services will not be provided within the time prescribed by this Agreement.

Upon request by Downer, CBS Group and Downer, at Downer’s discretion, must meet to review the progress of the performance of this Agreement including the matters described in clause 12.1.

INSURANCE

Unless Downer agrees otherwise in writing, CBS Group must maintain:

worker's compensation insurance as required by applicable law where the Services are provided;

professional indemnity insurance providing coverage for CBS Group’s professional responsibilities to Downer with an insured amount of $[10,000,000]; and

public and products liability insurance with an insured amount of $20,000,000 for any one occurrence, which covers the liability of CBS Group in respect of:

bodily injury to any third party;

third party property damage; and

advertising liability

CBS Group must ensure that the public and products liability insurance:

Provides that Downer is protected by a “Principal’s” indemnity extension covering Downer’s vicarious liability for the acts or omissions of other “Named Insureds” under such policy; and

Includes a cross liability clause in which the insurer agrees that the policy applies as if a separate policy was issued to each “Named Insured” (with the exception of limits of liability).

CBS Group must ensure that each policy required by this Agreement is in force prior to the Effective Date and maintained for the duration of the Agreement Term, except for professional indemnity insurance, which shall be maintained for 6 years after the conclusion of the Agreement Term.

CBS Group must, when requested by Downer, promptly satisfy Downer that each contract of insurance it is required to procure under this clause 13 is current by providing to Downer certificates of currency or other evidence reasonably required by Downer.

CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION

Both Parties must:

maintain strict confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information, and must take all reasonable, physical and legal steps necessary to prevent its disclosure;

not divulge any aspect of the Confidential Information to any other person other than pursuant to this Agreement;

not use or exploit the Confidential Information other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement;

not make any written notes, copy, reproduce, retain, store, record, computerise, document or duplicate any part of the Confidential Information which appears in any written or computerised or other recorded form other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement;

not make any improvements or modifications to or derivations or discoveries from any of the Confidential Information other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; and

if it considers it may be necessary to disclose any of the Confidential Information to any other person, obtain prior written authorisation from the other Party before making such disclosure.

If a Party is uncertain whether any information comprises part of the Confidential Information, then it must seek direction from the other Party before divulging the information.

Either Party may disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its officers and employees, and its legal, financial or other advisers and their respective employees and officers who:

have a need to know for the purposes of this Agreement, and only to the extent that each has a need to know;

are aware that the Confidential Information must be kept confidential; and

have agreed in writing to comply with substantially the same obligations in respect of the Confidential Information as those imposed on Downer under this Agreement.

The obligations on each Party in relation to Confidential Information survive termination of the Agreement.

The Parties and any subcontractor’s engagement by the respective Parties to fulfil the Services or Downer’s Obligations (as applicable) under this agreement must: 

maintain strict confidentiality in relation to Confidential Information, as set out in the Schedule, and must take all reasonable, physical and legal steps necessary to prevent its disclosure; and

not divulge any aspect of Confidential Information to any other person other than pursuant to this Agreement.

TERMINATION

Subject to clause 15.6 and 15.7, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon the provision of 90 days’ notice to the other Party in writing.

If either Party ("Defaulting Party"):

commits a breach of this Agreement and fails to remedy such breach within fourteen (14) days of receipt of a notice in writing requiring such breach to be remedied; or

becomes insolvent or is made subject to an application for winding up or resolves to wind up voluntarily (other than for the purposes of a solvent reconstruction), or has a receiver (or similar functionary) or an administrator appointed in respect of any of its assets, or ceases to carry on business or enters into a composition or arrangement with its creditors generally; or

commits a breach of this Agreement or the Scheme, and such breach is deemed by the other Party to involve fraud or dishonesty; or

there is a Change of Control in relation to the Default Party;

the other Party may terminate this Agreement immediately by giving written notice of termination to the Defaulting Party.

Downer may terminate this Agreement immediately by giving notice to CBS Group if CBS Group is in breach of clauses 21 or 23, or a Suspected Slavery occurs.

Termination under this clause 15.3 will be effective immediately and will not affect any other rights available to Downer.

If Downer terminates this Agreement under clause 15.3, Downer is not required to make any further payment to CBS Group, and the rights and liabilities of the Parties shall be the same as they would be at common law if CBS Group had wrongfully repudiated this Agreement and Downer had elected to treat this Agreement as at an end and recover damages.

If CBS Group terminates this Agreement in accordance with clause 15.1, CBS Group must reimburse Downer for all amounts paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with clause 6.1 as at the date of termination of this Agreement.

If Downer terminates this Agreement in accordance with clause 15.1, Downer is not entitled to   claim reimbursement of amounts paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with clause 6.1 as at the date of termination of this Agreement.  

If Downer terminates this agreement in accordance with clause 15.2 or 15.3, CBS Group must reimburse Downer for all amounts paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with clause 6.1 as at the date of termination of this Agreement.

CONSEQUENCES OF TERMINATION

On termination of this Agreement, for any reason listed in Clause 15:

the Licence granted by CBS Group to Downer to use the CBS Group Tools is immediately terminated, and Downer must cease to use the CBS Group Tools and as soon as reasonably practical return to CBS Group or destroy any and all of the CBS Group Tools; and

both Parties must, as soon as reasonably practical, return to the other Party or destroy any and all of any their Confidential Information and their Intellectual Property in its possession or control including any variation, modification or improvement; and

Termination of the Agreement shall not relieve a Party of liability to the other Party in respect of the rights or remedies of the other Party which have accrued prior to termination.  For the avoidance of doubt, Downer’s right to proceeds from the sale of Environmental Products (Certificate) in accordance with clause 6.2 survive termination of this Agreement.

LIABILITIES AND INDEMNITIES

Except as the terms of this Agreement specifically state, this Agreement does not include by implication any other term, condition or warranty in respect of the quality, merchantability, acceptability, fitness for purpose, condition, description or performance of the either Parties services (the Services or Downer’s Obligations) offered to the other Party or any contractual remedy for their failure.

Neither Party is liable to the other Party in any way arising under or in connection with the provision of the Services;

for any indirect or consequential losses or expenses suffered by either Party or any third party, howsoever caused, including but not limited to loss of turnover, profits, business or goodwill or any liability to any other party.

Nothing in this Agreement is to be interpreted as excluding, restricting or modifying or having the effect of excluding, restricting or modifying the application of any State or Federal legislation applicable to the sale of goods or supply of services which cannot be excluded, restricted or modified.

INDEMNITY

Except as otherwise caused or contributed to by the other Party, each Party must indemnify and keep the other Party indemnified from all claims, suits, demands, loss, damage, cost or liability in relation to or incidental to any of its acts or omissions under this Agreement, including but not limited to: 

any fine or penalty imposed on the indemnified Party by reason of a negligent act or omission on the part of the indemnifying Party; 

SURRENDER OF CERTIFICATES

If Certificates are required to be surrendered for any reason, the Parties agree that the Certificates will be surrendered on the basis of ownership prescribed in Schedule Item 4.

DISPUTE RESOLUTION

Any Dispute between Downer and CBS Group may be notified by a Party to the other Party and the Parties must:

within 10 days of the notification of the Dispute, meet to negotiate, in good faith, a resolution of the Dispute; and

if negotiation fails to achieve a resolution of the Dispute within 10 days of the notification of Dispute, participate in a mediation to attempt to resolve the Dispute, in which case:

such mediation is to be conducted by a mediator who is independent of the Parties and appointed by agreement of the Parties or, failing agreement within seven days of receiving any Party’s notice of dispute, by a person appointed by the Chair of Resolution Institute,

(ACN 008 651 232,

Level 2, 13-15 Bridge Street, Sydney NSW 2000;

telephone: 02 9251 3366,

email: infoaus@resolution.institute)

the Resolution Institute Mediation Rules shall apply to the mediation; and

continue to perform their respective obligations under this Agreement notwithstanding the existence of the Dispute.

TAXES, EMPLOYEE BENEFITS AND WORKERS

CBS Group acknowledges that it has sole liability in relation to:

wages, salaries, allowances, incentive payments, bonuses, sick leave, long service leave, annual leave, personal leave, termination or redundancy payments and any other employment related liabilities; and

all group tax, payroll tax, PAYE, superannuation, workers compensation and other taxes, levies, deductions, charges, penalties, duties or withholdings,

in respect of CBS Group’s personnel.

CBS Group further acknowledges that it does not have (and its personnel do not have), pursuant to this Agreement or otherwise, any entitlement from Downer in relation to any form of employment benefit.  CBS Group will not communicate or otherwise represent to its personnel that any of CBS Group’s personnel have any entitlement from Downer in relation to any form of employment related benefit.

CBS Group indemnifies Downer from and against any liability for the Indemnified Taxes, together with any other amount determined by Downer to be reasonably necessary to compensate Downer for any income tax liability, or reduction in income tax losses available to be carried forward or available income tax credits or rebates, to ensure that the after tax position of Downer is the same (or substantially the same) as it would have been had Downer never been liable for any Indemnified Taxes.

If Downer is, becomes or reasonably forms the view that it may be or become, liable for the payment of any Indemnified Taxes, Downer may deduct the amount of its liability or prospective liability for the Indemnified Taxes from any amount due by Downer to CBS Group, whether under this Agreement or otherwise.

CONFLICT OF INTEREST AND BUSINESS ETHICS

CBS Group will at all times comply with:

all Legal Requirements relating to anti-bribery, anti-corruption and anti-money laundering, including the Criminal Code Act 1995 (Cth), the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of the United States of America, and the Bribery Act 2010 of the United Kingdom (and must not do anything, or omit to do anything, to cause Downer to be in breach of any such Legal Requirements); and

Downer’s ‘Standards of Business Conduct’ and ‘Anti Bribery and Corruption Policy’ as published from time to time.

CBS Group warrants that neither it, nor any Relevant Person, has been convicted of any offence, and has not been the subject of any investigation or enforcement proceedings by any governmental, administrative or regulatory body regarding any offence or alleged offence, under any applicable Legal Requirements (including the Criminal Code Act 1995 (Cth), the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of the United States of America, and the Bribery Act 2010 of the United Kingdom) relating to anti-bribery, anti-corruption and anti-money laundering.

At any time before the expiry of three years from the completion or termination of this Agreement, CBS Group must permit Downer, its nominee, or the Principal (in this clause, each the Auditor) to conduct an audit and investigation:

to verify CBS Group’s compliance with this clause 21 (irrespective of whether there has been a breach of suspected breach); and/or

in relation to a Probity Event (or suspected Probity Event)

As part of an audit or investigation under this clause, CBS Group must:

make available to the Auditor (and allow the Auditor to make and retain copies of) any documents, records and other information in the power, possession or control of CBS Group; and

meet with the Auditor and make available CBS Group’s personnel to meet with the Auditor and answer reasonable questions in relation to the subject matter of the audit.

CBS Group must give Downer notice immediately upon becoming aware of a Probity Event or any breach (or suspected breach) of this clause 21.

MODERN SLAVERY

CBS Group must take (and must ensure that each of its Relevant Persons take) all reasonable steps to ensure that no modern slavery, human trafficking, servitude, forced labour, bonded labour, child labour, slavery or forced marriage or deceptive recruiting for labour or services (Modern Slavery) is present in CBS Group’s, or its Relevant Persons’, supply chains, or in any part of their businesses.

CBS Group warrants that neither it nor any of its Relevant Persons:

has been convicted of any offence involving Modern Slavery or Modern Slavery-like practices; and

having made reasonable enquiries, has been or is, to the best of its knowledge, the subject of any investigation, inquiry or enforcement proceedings by any governmental, administrative or regulatory body regarding any offence of alleged offence of or in connection to any form of Modern Slavery.

CBS Group must implement due diligence procedures for its Relevant Persons to minimise the risk of Modern Slavery in its supply chains. 

CBS Group must implement a system (to a standard that is consistent with good industry practices) for monitoring and evaluating the effectiveness of CBS Group’s actions taken under clauses 23.1 and 23.3.

CBS Group must promptly notify Downer:

as soon as it becomes aware of any actual or suspected Modern Slavery in its or any of its Relevant Persons’ supply chain which is connected to this Agreement (Suspected Slavery); and

its approach to address and remediate the Suspected Slavery. 

CBS Group must, on request from Downer (from time to time), provide all assistance and information reasonably necessary for Downer to prepare a modern slavery statement and otherwise comply with its obligations at law and Legal Requirements.

CBS Group must maintain a complete set of records to trace the supply chain of all goods and services provided to Downer in connection with this Agreement, including:

the steps taken by CBS Group under clause 23.1; and

the due diligence processes taken under clauses 23.3 and 23.4.

Downer will at any time have the right to inspect and to copy the whole or part of any record referred to in clause 23.7.

Upon receiving a request in writing from Downer for a copy of any record referred to in clause 23.7, CBS Group must promptly provide a copy of that record to Downer. 

CBS Group must train all of its officers and employees on the risks of Modern Slavery and on Downer’s policies and procedures in relation to Modern Slavery, including the principles set out in Downer’s ‘Standards of Business Conduct’. 

CBS Group must keep a record of all training with respect to modern slavery completed by its officers and employees and shall make a copy of the training record available to Downer on request.

ASSIGNMENT

Neither Party may assign, charge or deal with the Agreement without the prior written consent of the other Party, which must not be unreasonably withheld.

Subject to 24.1, if a Party assigns its interests under this Agreement, the assigning Party must ensure that any assignee first covenants to be bound by the respective Party’s rights and obligations hereunder.

DURATION OF PROVISIONS

The covenants, conditions, provisions and warranties contained in this Agreement shall not merge or terminate upon completion of the transactions contemplated herein but to the extent that they have not been fulfilled and satisfied or are capable of having effect shall remain in full force and effect.

PARTIES TO DO ALL THINGS NECESSARY

The Parties agree to do all things and execute all documents necessary to give effect to the terms of this Agreement.

The Parties must cooperate in the execution of all documents necessary to complete any action to be taken under this Agreement, including in relation to termination.  

COSTS

Each Party will bear its own legal and accounting costs incurred in respect of the preparation, negotiation and execution of this Agreement.

SEVERABILITY

If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then it must be interpreted as narrowly as necessary to allow it to be enforceable or valid.

If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then:

the provision (or part of it) must be severed from this Agreement;

the remaining provisions (and remaining part of the provision) are valid and enforceable.

NEW SOUTH WALES LAW AND JURISDICTION

The law of New South Wales as amended from time to time governs this Agreement.

The Parties agree to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of New South Wales, the Federal Court of Australia, and of courts entitled to hear appeals from those courts.

AMENDING THIS AGREEMENT

An amendment or change is only effective once it is:

made in writing; and

executed by all Parties.

THIS IS THE ENTIRE AGREEMENT

This is the entire Agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement.

There is no other Agreement, understanding, warranty or representation, whether oral or written, binding the Parties concerning any aspect of this Agreement.

NO WAIVER

A Party may exercise its rights at any time and does not waive those even if that Party:

previously waived a breach or default of all or part of the same or other provision; or

delayed or omitted to exercise its rights.

A waiver is only effective:

if it is signed by the Party granting the waiver; and

to the extent set out in the waiver.

INTERPRETATION

When interpreting this Agreement:

headings are for convenience only and do not affect the interpretation of this Agreement;

the singular includes the plural and the plural includes the singular;

any gender includes any other gender;

any thing required to be done on a day that is not a Business Day, may be done on the next Business Day; 

reference to a person includes a natural person, corporation, partnership, association or other legal entity;

reference to legislation includes that legislation as amended or modified or re-enacted; and

reference to a provision or clause means a provision or clause in this Agreement.

Effective Date:  March / 8th  / 2021

Unless subject to a Variation under clause 6.1(d), all Services shall be provided solely in relation to the FE04 HVAC Energy Efficiency project – applicable to the SGT and Waratah fleets and subject to the modification approval by Sydney Trains.

The diagram below provides a high-level overview of the process activities associated with the generation of Energy Saving Certificates for energy saving modifications to the Sydney Trains fleet.  

For avoidance of doubt, with the exception of the scope assigned to Downer Group listed below, all works and costs are the responsibility of CBS Group.

Figure 1	Process Activities

CBS Group is responsible for all works associated with the generation of Energy Saving Certificates for the modifications, including:

Development of energy saving models

Management of data, process and records in accordance with the requirements of the scheme

Appointment and fees associated audits, certificates, application and transactions.

Management, communication (reporting) and administration to Downer Group in accordance with an agreed project management plan.

During the Term and in accordance with this Agreement, CBS Group must:

licence and provide the necessary CBS Group Tools and guidance to assist Downer record the Evidence and other information necessary to meet the obligations under the Environmental Scheme;

guide Downer through the steps necessary to meet the requirements of the Environmental Scheme;

provide to Downer timely communication on changes to the Environmental Scheme:

keep confidential, or depersonalise prior to disclosure, details of all of Downer’s Obligations proposed by Downer under the Agreement and only disclose the information required under the relevant Environmental Scheme;

provide and review quality assurance procedures to aid the accurate creation of Certificates;

perform a quality assurance procedure and alert Downer’s to any missing information within 30 Business Days of receipt of information confirming completion of Downer’s Obligations provided to a Customer;

provide training and support as may be necessary covering the relevant Environmental Scheme and the CBS Group Tools; and

retain all records arising from the provision of Downer’s Obligations for a period of at least six years or as specified by the Scheme requirements; and

With respect to the creation of Certificates, CBS Group must:

Register a Certificate creator under the relevant Scheme;

promptly create Certificates attributable to Downer following receipt of the relevant information; and

CBS Group must make payments to Downer based on Schedule Item 4

With respect to the creation of Environmental Products, if these are deemed ineligible for due to fraud or negligence by the Customer or Downer, Downer has no claim against CBS Group; and

Promptly make all disbursements to Downer arising from the receipt of proceeds from the sale of Energy Certificates in accordance with Schedule Item 4

The following list CBS Group and Downer agree to work exclusivity with each other in order to offer funding from Environment Certificates to the end.  CBS Group cannot pursue these opportunities with another partner nor direct with the Customer and Downer cannot pursue these opportunities with another business offering Environment Certificate services.

* Expiry Date - if contract not signed with the Customer to proceed by this date or exclusivity not agreed to be extended by that date.

| APPENDIX C – PAYMENT |



| Times for submitting payment claims | Unless otherwise stated in Schedule Item 4, CBS Group must submit payment claims on a monthly basis, by no later than the 5th day of each month and will include all Services provided by CBS Group in the month immediately prior to the month in which the payment claim is submitted. |

| Time for Payment | Unless otherwise stated in Schedule Item 4, Downer must pay CBS Group by no later than 30 days after the end of the month in which a valid invoice for those Services is received by Downer for the amount certified by Downer pursuant to clause 6.1(b). |

| CBS Group Failure Event | If a CBS Group Failure Event occurs, CBS Group must reimburse Downer for all Fees for Services paid by Downer to CBS Group as at the date of the CBS Group Failure Event.  Payment must be made to Downer within 30 days of the date up which the CBS Group Failure Event occurred. |

| Certificate Sale | Upon Certificate Sale, CBS Group must make payments to Downer for each project, at an ‘as sold’ price,  as follows:
100% reimbursement of Fees for Services (Item 3) received by CBS Group from Downer as at the date of the first Certificate Sale; 
AND
70% of all gross sales where the Certificate value is equal to or less than $90.00 at the time of sale; and
100% of all gross sales value of amounts where the Certificate value is greater than $90.00
For the avoidance of doubt consider the following example:
Where the Certificate value is $92 at the time of sale, the payment value will be calculated based on 70% of $90 multiplied by the number of Certificates plus 100% of $2 multiplied by the number of certificates.  Therefore, the payment owing to Downer for a single Certificate in this instance is $65 ($63 + $2)
:
Payments to Downer must be made in accordance with clause 6.2. |
