## Master Agreemnet Environmental Energy Finance.docx

*File: `Downer CBS Contract/Master Agreemnet Environmental Energy Finance.docx`*

Environmental Energy Finance – Master Services Agreement

CBS GROUP* PTY LTD  ABN 20164870080 of Level 3, 480 Collins Street, MELBOURNE VIC 3000  

Downer* 

*Includes all agents, parties, contractors and suppliers acting for named Principals

SCHEDULE

BACKGROUND

CBS Group are accredited under various Environment Schemes and carries on a business involving capturing, ensuring compliance and submitting information as required for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates), the trading and sale of the Certificates, and consulting services associated with these activities.

Downer carries on a business that at times involves consulting and advice on  the procurement, design, installation, servicing and/or maintenance of efficient end-user equipment by their Customers which may be entitled for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates) under the Environmental Schemes.  These are Scheme Activities performed using Approved Products & Services.

CBS Group has agreed to provide Services to Downer and Downer’s Customers to enable funding from Australian Environment Schemes.  CBS Group will work with Downer to collect evidence. Downer will receive payments based on  the successful registration of certificates for the nominated projects (Scheme Activities)

OPERATIVE PART

Definitions

In this Agreement:

Accreditations means any and all accreditations, qualifications and approvals that Downer and its personnel must hold at law as required by the Scheme and as otherwise required by CBS Group;

Accredited (Certificate) Providers means the party holds an accreditation under one or more of the Environment Schemes.

ACL means the Australian Consumer Law Schedule of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth).

Activity (Scheme Activity) means the installation, upgrade, or modification or energy-efficiency or renewable-energy equipment or processes using Approved Products & Services that may be entitled to Environment Certificates under the Environment Schemes.

Agreement means this agreement (including any schedule or annexure), and the Forward Contracts;

Approved Products & Services means products, services and materials necessary for the provision of Downer Services that are expressly approved for use under the Environmental Schemes or as otherwise set outby CBS Group;

Business Day means Monday to Friday excluding public holidays;

Certificates means the Environmental Products, as appropriate;

Confidential Information means:

all business and financial information, sales and supply details, marketing strategies, customer and supplier listings, business listings, pricing information, information concerning CBS Group or its customers and business associates; 

all (if any) other information relating to CBS Group or its business which, by its nature, places or potentially places CBS Group  at an advantage over its present or future business competitors, including but not limited to, the CBS Manuals and CBS Tools, processes and workflows; and

any other information that would otherwise at law be considered secret or confidential information of CBS Group ;

whether or not marked "Confidential" BUT does not include information which:

at the time of first disclosure by CBS Group  to Downer is already in the public domain;

after disclosure by CBS Group, becomes part of the public domain otherwise than by disclosure in breach of the terms of this Agreement; and

Downer can prove that the information was in its possession before the time of first disclosure by or discovery from CBS Group .

Customer means the energy user (end-customer) to whom Downer provides the installation of efficient end-user equipment (Approved Products) in accordance with the Scheme;

Certificates see Environmental Products

Default Interest Rate means the rate per annum of two percent (2%) higher than the rate fixed for the time being under Section 2 of the Penalty Interest Rates Act 1983 (Vic);

Documentation means CBS Group’s checklists and forms relevant to the Scheme, the Requirements and the Specifications, as provided to Downer and updated from time to time;

Effective Date means the date of execution of this Agreement;

Encumbrance means:

any:

legal or equitable interest or power created, arising in or reserved in or over an interest in any property or asset; or

security for payment of money, performance of obligations or protection against default;

anything or preferential interest or arrangement of any kind giving a person priority or preference over claims or other persons with respect to any property or asset;

a PPSA Security Interest; or

an agreement or arrangement, regardless of whether or not it is enforceable, to grant or create anything referred to in (i), or (ii) above;

Environmental Products (Certificates) means certificates created under the Environmental Schemes, including but not limited to ESCs, VEECs, STCs, LGCs, and ACCUs as relevant;

Environmental Scheme (Schemes) means each of the Government Energy Efficiency, Renewable, and Carbon Abatement Schemes; including, but not limited to the ESS, VEU, REES, EEIS, RET, CFI, as relevant;  ESS (NSW Energy Saving Scheme), VEET (Victorian Energy Efficiency Target), REES (South Australian Retailer Energy Efficient Scheme), EEIS (ACT Energy Efficiency Improvement Scheme), RET (Federal Renewable Energy Target), CFI (Federal Carbon Farming Initiative).

Evidence means any and all documents, requests, quotations, receipts, job sheets, time sheets, notes and confirmations relating to provision of Downer Services by Downer; 

Exclusivity Agreements means a list of customers, opportunities, projects or other group of activities that Downer and CBS Group agree to work exclusively with each other for the purpose of Environment Certificate creation under the Environment Schemes

Forward Contracts means any and all forward certificate delivery agreements between CBS Group  and Downer entered in to, or otherwise in operation, during the Term;  

GST means any tax imposed on the supply of goods or services, consumption or value added tax, including without limitation any tax imposed in the A New Tax System (Goods and Services Tax) Act (Cth);

Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, the CBS Group  Manuals, the CBS Group  Tools, software, workflow, and all copyright, trade mark rights, design rights, patent rights and confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to CBS Group  or that relates to the CBS Group ’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it;

Invoice Date means the dates for invoicing the Services as set out in Item 2 of the Schedule;

Licence means the CBS Group ’s licence of CBS Group  Tools and CBS Group  Manuals to Downer for the purposes of this Agreement as set out in clause 6 of this Agreement;

PPSA Security Interest means a security interest as defined in the Personal Property Securities Act 2009 (Cth);

Partner Confidential Information means:

all business and financial information, sales and supply details, marketing strategies, customer and supplier listings, business listings, pricing information, information concerning Downer its customers and business associates; 

any other information that would otherwise at law be considered secret or confidential information of Downer;

any other Items listed in the Item 5 Partner Confidential Information in this contract.

Partner Instructions (Item 8) means guidance from Downer regarding any aspects of the relationship, such as working with Downers customers.

Partner Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, and all copyright, trade mark rights, design rights, patent rights and confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to Downer or that relates to Downer’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it; and any other Items listed in Item 6 in this contract.

Partner Services means the services provided by Downer under clause 3, and such further services agreed from time to time;  This includes services provided to end Customers, and services provide to CBS Group  to facilitate CBS Group ’s registration of Environment Certificates on behalf the Customer.

Requirements means any and all processes, procedures, duties and obligations that apply to the Scheme and as otherwise set out in the CBS Group  Manuals;

Scheme see Environmental Scheme

Security Amount means the amount payable by Downer to CBS Group  as set out in Item 2 of the Schedule;

Specifications means any and all specifications, standards and terms that set out the minimum standards required under the Scheme, and as otherwise set out in the CBS Group  Manuals;

Term means the period from the Effective Date until this Agreement is ended in accordance with clause 15; 

CBS Group  Tools means CBS Group ’s systems, data collection tools and record keeping tools designed to assist Downer to meet obligations under the Scheme, including but not limited to workflows, forms, checklists, spreadsheets, software and related documents;

CBS Group  Manuals means the manuals, checklists, training materials, and guidelines authored by or on behalf of CBS Group  as published and updated from time to time, setting out, without limitation, the Accreditations, the Requirements, the Specifications, the Approved Products and the Documentation;

CBS Group  Services means the services provided by CBS Group  under clause 5; and

Year means the period commencing 1 July in one year and ending on 30 June in the following year.

Engagement

Partner engages CBS Group  to provide CBS Group  Services to Downer and to the end Customer, and Downer agrees to provide Partner Services to Watly for the Term on the terms of this Agreement

CBS Group  and Downer may in writing agree to vary Downer Services or the CBS Group  Services, or provide additional services, from time to time.  Any varied or additional services will be provided on the terms and conditions of this Agreement and such other terms as may be agreed.

Partner Services to CBS Group 

Downer Services incorporate all services provided by Downer to CBS Group  and required to give effect to the Environmental Schemes.

During the Term of this Agreement, Downer must provide Downer Services on the terms set out in this Agreement.

Downer’s obligations

Downer warrants that it has the qualifications, experience and expertise appropriate and necessary to perform Downer Services offered to End Customers (Item 7).

Downer warrants that it has the qualifications, accreditations, experience and expertise appropriate and necessary to provide Downer Services to CBS Group  under this Agreement.

In providing Downer Services, Downer must, and must ensure that its staff must:

act honestly, ethically and with due care and skill;

comply with the Environmental Scheme Requirements as directed by CBS Group ;

use Approved Products when a Customer is claiming Environmental Certificates;

ensure Downer Services are performed in accordance with the Scheme requirements and are eligible to create Environmental Products as appropriate and directed by CBS Group ;

provide Evidence to CBS Group  of provision of Downer Services to a particular Customer within 30 Business Days of completion of provision of a Scheme Activity for that Customer; 

submit to CBS Group  for its approval any additional activities or services it wishes to include as Partner Services; 

not cause, assist it or otherwise allow the creation of any Encumbrance over any Certificate.

During the Term Downer must:

observe and comply with the CBS Group  Manuals, as modified from time to time, in every respect and ensure that its employees and contractors also do so;

ensure that all advertising materials it uses relating to the provision of Scheme Activities or otherwise relevant to this Agreement identifies CBS Group  as the Accredited Person (Victoria) or Accredited Certificate Provider (New South Wales), as appropriate, under the Scheme;

effect and maintain throughout the Term, at its own expense, and ensure any subcontractors also maintain:

public liability insurance with a minimum cover of $10,000,000 per claim; and

product liability insurance with a minimum cover of $10,000,000 per claim;

not make any false or misleading representations about the Scheme, CBS Group , Downer, the CBS Group  Services or Downer Services; 

undertake and participate in training for the Environmental Schemes, as directed by CBS Group ;

upon request by CBS Group  or a Scheme auditor, provide access to Documentation, Evidence and other information relevant to Downer Services and the Scheme for the purposes of inspection or audit, and provide its full assistance to CBS Group  and the Scheme auditor in any inspection or audit or audit activity or the implementation of any direction arising from an audit finding;

facilitate and assist CBS Group  to access the Customer and the Customer’s site for the purpose of proving compliance with the Scheme requirements. 

This includes providing evidence, assurance, and facilitating visits or communication with the Customer for third-party auditors as well as the Scheme regulatory staff.

adhere to all applicable policies of CBS Group  in force, as amended from time to time, including as set out in the CBS Group  Manuals, updated by CBS Group  Newsletters;

comply with all occupational health and safety requirements relevant to the Scheme and generally at law, including but not limited to any requirement to obtain and maintain any registration, licences or permits; and

ensure its employees and contractors are paid in accordance with the law; and

Downer must not:

use the CBS Group  Tools other than in providing Downer Services;

use the CBS Group  Tools to create Certificates itself or assist third parties to create Certificates;

reverse engineer any of the CBS Group  Tools; or

disclose to third parties the CBS Group  Tools or the details of any CBS Group  Tools.

CBS Group  Services

During the Term, CBS Group  must provide the following assistance to Downer as CBS Group  considers reasonably necessary and appropriate:

Sales guides and quoting tools and estimates for value of Environment Certificates to allow Downer to estimate the value of a Customers upgrade under the Environment Scheme; 

keep confidential details of all Partner sales opportunities and Customer details;

licence and provide the CBS Group  Tools to assist Downer’s Customers to record the Evidence and other information necessary to meet the obligations under the Scheme;

provide Documentation to help guide Downer’s Customer through the steps of the Scheme;

provide to Downer timely communication on changes to the CBS Group  Manuals, procedures or systems under the relevant Scheme or software upgrades by CBS Group ;

keep confidential, or depersonalise prior to disclosure, details of all Partner Services proposed by Downer under the Agreement and only disclose the information required under the relevant Scheme;

provide and review quality assurance procedures to aid the accurate creation of Certificates;

perform a quality assurance procedure and alert Downer’s Customer to any missing information within 20 Business Days of receipt of information confirming completion of Downer Services provided to a Customer;

provide training covering the relevant Scheme and the CBS Group  Tools; and

retain all records of Downer Services for a period of at least six years or as specified by the Scheme requirements; and

With respect to the creation of Environmental Products, CBS Group  must:

register as a Certificate creator under the relevant Scheme;

promptly create Certificates attributable to Downer Services following receipt of the relevant information; and

CBS Group  must either make payment to Downer’s Customers based on 

With respect to the creation of Environmental Products, if these are deemed ineligible for due to fraud or negligence by the Customer or Partner

Downer has no claim against CBS Group ; and

Downer must indemnify and keep CBS Group  indemnified from all claims, suits, demands, loss, damage, cost or liability in relation to the ineligible Environmental Products.

Licence of CBS Group  Tools

Subject to the provisions set out in clause 16.1(a), CBS Group  licences the CBS Group  Tools to Downer during the Term of this Agreement.

Downer must only use the CBS Group  Tools for the purposes of providing Downer Services under the terms of this Agreement.

CBS Group  may, as it considers necessary, maintain, delete or modify the CBS Group  Tools from time to time.

CBS Group  Manuals

CBS Group  will publish or provide access to the CBS Group  Manuals, which among other things, sets out various Requirements, Specifications, and responsibilities of Downer, and its provision of Downer Services.

CBS Group  may, in its absolute discretion, vary the CBS Group  Manuals from time to time by giving Downer at least 7 days notice in writing.

The CBS Group  Manuals form part of this Agreement, and where there is a conflict, the terms of the CBS Group  Manuals shall prevail to the extent of the inconsistency.

Any breach by Downer of any provision contained in the CBS Group  Manuals is deemed to be a breach of this Agreement.

The effective date of any policy or obligation contained in the CBS Group  Manuals shall be the date nominated in the CBS Group  Manuals or updated part or, if no date is nominated, then the first date that the particular amended CBS Group  Manual is published on CBS Group ’s website.

The CBS Group  Manuals remain at all times the property of CBS Group , and must be returned to CBS Group  immediately upon request or upon termination of this Agreement.

Downer must not copy, reproduce, retain, store, record, computerise, document or duplicate any part of the Manuals other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement or with written permission from CBS Group .

Security

This clause is not applicable to Referral or Commission Agreements.

Payment 

The Payment to the Customer is payable in accordance with the terms agreed on a per Customer and per Project basis or as specified in Item 4 Pricing. 

Commission Payment is payable to Downer in accordance with the terms agreed in Item 4 Pricing

Start-up costs and expenses

Downer must ensure that it has the necessary resources and staff to provide Downer Services.  

General Relationship

Downer is an independent of CBS Group  and no agency, employment, partnership or other relationship will exist between Downer and CBS Group .  Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed or construed to render CBS Group  liable for any costs obligations or expenditures pursuant to any relevant employment laws.

Downer must not at any time represent to any other party that it is an employee, servant or agent of CBS Group , but for the purpose of complying with the Scheme:

Downer’s agents must at all times identify themselves to potential or existing Customers including identifying;

the company they represent, and 

that the Accredited Person or Accredited Certificate Provider, as appropriate depending on the Scheme, is CBS Group ; and

Downer’s sales and installation documentation provided to the Customer must identify that the Accredited Person or Accredited Certificate Provider, as appropriate depending on the Scheme, is CBS Group .  

Downer will have no authority to bind CBS Group  legally with respect to any third party unless expressly authorised in writing to do so by CBS Group .

Downer must not:

incur any liability on behalf of CBS Group  or in any way pledge or purport to pledge CBS Group ’s credit; and

accept any order nor make any contract binding upon CBS Group  without CBS Group ’s prior written approval.

During the term, Downer retains the right to enter into any contract with any other person or persons, company or firm to perform services similar to those contemplated by this Agreement, provided that they do not interfere with Downer’s ability to comply with this Agreement.

Intellectual Property

Downer has no proprietary right or interest in the Intellectual Property.

Downer must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world the Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trade marks or designs derived from or similar to the Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.

Any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from any party of the information comprised in the Intellectual Property whether attributable in whole or in part to Downer, will become and remain the property of CBS Group .

Downer hereby assigns and conveys to CBS Group  any intellectual property rights that Downer may acquire arising from any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from the Intellectual Property.

CBS Group  has no proprietary right or interest in Downer Intellectual Property.

CBS Group  must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world Downer Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from or similar to the Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.

Confidential Information

Downer must:

maintain strict confidentiality in relation to the Confidential Information, and must take all reasonable, physical and legal steps necessary to prevent its disclosure;

not divulge any aspect of the Confidential Information to any other person other than pursuant to this Agreement;

not use or exploit the Confidential Information other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement;

not make any written notes, copy, reproduce, retain, store, record, computerise, document or duplicate any part of the Confidential Information which appears in any written or computerised or other recorded form other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement;

not make any improvements or modifications to or derivations or discoveries from any of the Confidential Information other than in accordance with the terms of this Agreement; and

if it consider it may be necessary to disclose any of the Confidential Information to any other person, obtain prior written authorisation from CBS Group  before making such disclosure.

If Downer is uncertain whether any information comprises part of the Confidential Information then it must seek direction from CBS Group  before divulging the information.

Downer may disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its officers and employees, and its legal, financial or other advisers and their respective employees and officers who:

have a need to know for the purposes of this Agreement, and only to the extent that each has a need to know;

are aware that the Confidential Information must be kept confidential; and

have agreed in writing to comply with substantially the same obligations in respect of the Confidential Information as those imposed on Downer under this Agreement.

The obligations on Downer in relation to Confidential Information survive termination of the Agreement.

CBS Group  and any subcontractors engagement by CBS Group  to fulfil CBS Group  Services under this agreement must: 

maintain strict confidentiality in relation to Downer Confidential Information, as set out in the Schedule, and must take all reasonable, physical and legal steps necessary to prevent its disclosure; and

not divulge any aspect of Downer Confidential Information to any other person other than pursuant to this Agreement.

Partner’s Employees and Contractors

Downer warrants that it will make a full and detailed check of all personnel providing Downer Services to ensure that they are fully qualified, accredited and suitable to perform Downer Services.

Downer’s staff will be trained by Downer to diligently, efficiently and properly carry out Downer Services. 

Downer will be liable for any breach of this Agreement by its officers, employees, contractors and advisers.  Downer must provide CBS Group  with details of any contactors it intends to utilise, for approval.

During the Term, Downer must:

ensure that its employees, agents or approved subcontractors are competent and have the necessary skills to provide Downer Services; and

adequately and properly manage, direct and control its employees, agents and subcontractors in providing Downer Services.

Downer may disclose Confidential Information to those of its officers and employees and its legal, financial or other advisers and their respective employees and officers who:

have a need to know (but only if and to the extent that they have a need to know) to assist Downer to properly market or sell its products and provide Downer Services;

are aware that Confidential Information must be kept confidential; and

in the case of employees and officers, have agreed in writing to comply with the terms of this Agreement as if they were a party to this Agreement.

Termination

Either party may terminate this Agreement upon the provision of 90 days notice to the other party in writing.

If either party ("Defaulting Party"):

commits a breach of this Agreement and fails to remedy such breach within fourteen (14) days of receipt of a notice in writing requiring such breach to be remedied; or

becomes insolvent or is made subject to an application for winding up or resolves to wind up voluntarily (other than for the purposes of a solvent reconstruction), or has a receiver (or similar functionary) or an administrator appointed in respect of any of its assets, or ceases to carry on business or enters into a composition or arrangement with its creditors generally; or

commits a breach of this Agreement or the Scheme, and such breach is deemed by the other party to involve fraud or dishonesty; or

there is a Change of Control in relation to the Default Party;

the other party may terminate this Agreement immediately by giving written notice of termination to the Defaulting Party.

Consequences of Termination

On termination of this Agreement, for whatever reason:

the Licence granted by CBS Group  to Downer to use the CBS Group  Tools is immediately terminated, and Downer must cease to use the CBS Group  Tools and as soon as reasonably practical return to CBS Group  or destroy any and all of the CBS Group  Tools;

the both parties must, as soon as reasonably practical, return to the other party or destroy any and all of any their Confidential Information and their Intellectual Property in its possession or control including any variation, modification or improvement; and

Downer must render its account for all Partner Services properly provided up until the date of termination.  CBS Group  must make payment, provided it is reasonably satisfied with the accuracy of the account and the quality and compliance of Downer Services, within 7 days of receipt of the account.

Upon termination of this Agreement:

Downer must not accept any new requests from Customers for it to provide them with any Partner Services under the terms of this Agreement; and

in respect of Partner Services scheduled at the time of termination but not yet provided (Scheduled Works), Downer must at CBS Group ’s election:

provide Downer Services to the Customer; or

assign the Scheduled Works to CBS Group , and CBS Group  may arrange for an alternate service provider to provide the Scheduled Works to the Customer.

Termination of the Agreement shall not relieve a party of liability to the other party in respect of the rights or remedies of the other party which have accrued prior to termination.

Liabilities and Indemnities

Except as the terms of this Agreement specifically state, this Agreement does not include by implication any other term, condition or warranty in respect of the quality, merchantability, acceptability, fitness for purpose, condition, description or performance of the either parties services (CBS Group  Services or Partner Services) offered to the other party or any contractual remedy for their failure.

Neither party is liable to the other party in any way arising under or in connection with the provision of the Services;

for any indirect or consequential losses or expenses suffered by either party or any third party, howsoever caused, including but not limited to loss of turnover, profits, business or goodwill or any liability to any other party.

Nothing in this Agreement is to be interpreted as excluding, restricting or modifying or having the effect of excluding, restricting or modifying the application of any State or Federal legislation applicable to the sale of goods or supply of services which cannot be excluded, restricted or modified.

Indemnity

Except as otherwise caused or contributed to by the other party,  each party must indemnify and keep the other party indemnified from all claims, suits, demands, loss, damage, cost or liability in relation to or incidental to any of its acts or omissions under this Agreement, including but not limited to: 

any certificate surrenders, fine or penalty imposed on the indemnified party by reason of a negligent act or omission on the part of the indemnifying party.; 

Assignment

Downer must not assign, charge or deal with the Agreement without the prior written consent of CBS Group , which consent may be withheld in its absolute discretion.

CBS Group  may assign its interests under this Agreement provided that any assignee must first covenant to be bound by CBS Group ’s rights and obligations hereunder.

Duration of Provisions

The covenants, conditions, provisions and warranties contained in this Agreement shall not merge or terminate upon completion of the transactions contemplated herein but to the extent that they have not been fulfilled and satisfied or are capable of having effect shall remain in full force and effect.

Parties to do all things necessary

The parties agree to do all things and execute all documents necessary to give effect to the terms of this Agreement.

Downer must cooperate in the execution of all documents necessary to complete any action to be taken under this Agreement, including in relation to termination.  

For the purposes of clause 21.2, if Downer is unable for any reason or if Downer fails, refuses or neglects to execute any such document upon the reasonable request of CBS Group , then Downer hereby appoints CBS Group  as its irrevocable attorney in its name, and on its behalf, with the right to do any and all actions and things necessary to complete the action to be taken under this Agreement, including in relation to termination, and to execute and deliver all documents necessary or required to be completed.

Costs

Each party will bear its own legal and accounting costs incurred in respect of the preparation, negotiation and execution of this Agreement.

Severability

If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then it must be interpreted as narrowly as necessary to allow it to be enforceable or valid.

If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then:

the provision (or part of it) must be severed from this Agreement;

the remaining provisions (and remaining part of the provision) are valid and enforceable.

Victorian law and jurisdiction

The law of Victoria from time to time governs this Agreement.

The parties agree to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Victoria, the Federal Court of Australia, and of courts entitled to hear appeals from those courts.

Amending this Agreement

An amendment or change is only effective once it is:

made in writing; and

executed by all parties.

This is the entire Agreement

This is the entire Agreement of the parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement.

There is no other Agreement, understanding, warranty or representation, whether oral or written, binding the parties concerning any aspect of this Agreement.

No waiver

A party may exercise its rights at any time and does not waive those even if that party:

previously waived a breach or default of all or part of the same or other provision; or

delayed or omitted to exercise its rights.

A waiver is only effective:

if it is signed by the party granting the waiver; and

to the extent set out in the waiver.

Interpretation

When interpreting this Agreement:

headings are for convenience only and do not affect the interpretation of this Agreement;

the singular includes the plural and the plural includes the singular;

any gender includes any other gender;

any thing required to be done on a day that is not a Business Day, may be done on the next Business Day; 

reference to a person includes a natural person, corporation, partnership, association or other legal entity;

reference to legislation includes that legislation as amended or modified or re-enacted; and

reference to a provision or clause means a provision or clause in this Agreement.

EXECUTED AS A DEED

Effective Date:1 August 2020

 BACKGROUND & Acknowledgements
      A. Downer carries on a business that at times involves advice and services on the procurement,
         design, installation, servicing and/or maintenance of efficient end-user equipment by their
         Customers which may be entitled for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates)
         under the Environmental Schemes.

      B. Downer and CBS Group have agreed to partner in the novel application of Environmental
         Schemes to mobile plant. A particular application of this partnership is the pursuit of Energy
         Saving Certificates from the NSW Energy Savings Scheme resulting from modifications to the
         Sydney Trains SGT and Waratah fleets maintained by Downer.

      C. Downer wishes to engage CBS Group to provide the services required to generate value from
         Energy Savings Certificates under the NSW Energy Savings Scheme, and CBS Group has
         agreed to provide the Services on the terms set out in this Agreement. CBS Group will work
         with Downer to collect evidence. Downer will receive payments based on the successful
         registration of certificates for the nominated projects (Scheme Activities)

      D. CBS Group has represented to Downer that it has the skill, experience, personnel, material
         and capacity to supply the Services in accordance with this Agreement.

 OPERATIVE PART
1.      DEFINITIONS
1.1     In this Agreement:
Agreement means this agreement (including any schedules, appendices or annexures);
Arbitration is a method of dispute resolution whereby a dispute raised under clause 19 is finally
resolved as follows:
        (a)      the arbitration will be finally resolved under the Resolution Institute Arbitration Rules;
        (b)      there will be one arbitrator, appointed in accordance with the Resolution Institute
                 Arbitration Rules;
        (c)      the arbitrator must give written reasons for their award;
        (d)      the seat of the arbitration will be Sydney, New South Wales;
        (e)      the arbitration will be conducted in English.
Business Day means Monday to Friday excluding public holidays;
CBS Group Failure Event Date means 5pm on the 15th day after the final Monthly Project
Management Fee was paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with Schedule Item 3 and 4.
CBS Group Failure Event occurs when:
        (a)      Downer has satisfied its obligations to perform any agreed actions recorded in accordance
                 with clause 12.2(c) and 12.2(d); and
        (b)      CBS Group fails to obtain and sell any Environmental Products before the CBS Group
                 Failure Event Date.
CBS Group Tools means CBS Group ’s systems, data collection tools and record keeping tools
designed to assist Downer to meet obligations under the Scheme, including but not limited to
workflows, forms, checklists, spreadsheets, software and related documents;
CBS Group Workbooks means the Workbooks, checklists, training materials, and guidelines
authored by or on behalf of CBS Group as published and updated from time to time, setting out,
without limitation, the Accreditations, the Requirements, the Specifications, the Approved Products
and the Documentation;
Certificates means the Environmental Products, as appropriate;
Certificate Registration means the process of registering ownership of the Certificates with the
Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) Registry following notification by the ESS Registry that the Certificates
are available for registration.

Confidential Information means:
        (a)      all business and financial information, sales and supply details, marketing strategies,
                 customer and supplier listings, business listings, pricing information, information
                 concerning each respective Party or its customers and business associates;
        (b)      all (if any) other information relating to each Party or its respective business which, by its
                 nature, places or potentially places that Party at an advantage over its present or future
                 business competitors. To avoid ambiguity, in the case of CBS Group, this will include, but
                 not be limited to, the CBS Workbooks and CBS Tools, processes and workflows; and
        (c)      any other information that would otherwise at law be considered secret or confidential
                 information of respective Party;
whether or not marked "Confidential" BUT does not include information which:
        (d)      at the time of first disclosure by one Party to the other Party is already in the public
                 domain;
        (e)      after disclosure by a Party, becomes part of the public domain otherwise than by
                 disclosure in breach of the terms of this Agreement; and
        (f)      the receiving Party can prove to the disclosing Party that the information was in its
                 possession before the time of first disclosure by or discovery from the other Party.
Customer means the energy user (end-customer) to whom Downer provides the installation of
efficient end-user equipment (Approved Products) in accordance with the Scheme;
Certificates see Environmental Products
Dispute means any dispute or difference between the Parties to this Agreement arising from or in
connection with this Agreement including any dispute or difference arising as a consequence of a
failure by CBS Group to reach agreement on any matter;
Documentation means CBS Group’s checklists and forms relevant to the Scheme, the Requirements
and the Specifications, as provided to Downer and updated from time to time;
Downer Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, and all copyright, trademark rights,
design rights, patent rights and confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to Downer
or that relates to Downer’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it;
Downer’s Obligations means the obligations of Downer prescribed in clause 3, and such further
services agreed from time to time;
Effective Date means the date of execution of this Agreement by all Parties;
Energy Savings Nomination means an approved Energy Savings Scheme nomination form
completed by the Original Energy Saver.
Environmental Products (Certificates) means certificates created under the Environmental
Schemes, including but not limited to ESCs, VEECs, STCs, LGCs, and ACCUs as relevant;
Environmental Scheme (Schemes) means each of the Government Energy Efficiency, Renewable,
and Carbon Abatement Schemes; including, but not limited to the ESS, VEU, REES, EEIS, RET, CFI,
as relevant; ESS (NSW Energy Saving Scheme), VEET (Victorian Energy Efficiency Target), REES
(South Australian Retailer Energy Efficient Scheme), EEIS (ACT Energy Efficiency Improvement
Scheme), RET (Federal Renewable Energy Target), CFI (Federal Carbon Farming Initiative).
Evidence means all data and documents necessary to satisfy the requirements of the Energy Savings
Scheme.
Exclusivity Agreements means a list of customers, opportunities, projects or other group of activities
that Downer and CBS Group agree to work exclusively with each other for the purpose of Environment
Certificate creation under the Environment Schemes
Fees for Services means the amount payable by Downer to CBS Group as set out in Schedule Item
3;
GST means any tax imposed on the supply of goods or services, consumption or value added tax,
including without limitation any tax imposed in the A New Tax System (Goods and Services Tax) Act
(Cth);

Intellectual Property means the Confidential Information, the CBS Group Workbooks, the CBS Group
Tools, software, workflow, and all copyright, trade mark rights, design rights, patent rights and
confidential information or know-how owned by or licensed to CBS Group or that relates to the CBS
Group ’s business and any variation, modification or improvement to any of it;
Intended Purpose means the purpose or purposes for which the Services are intended, including any
purpose(s) which is described in, or may be reasonably inferred from, this Agreement and any
purpose notified by Downer to CBS Group (whether before or after the execution of this Agreement);
Legal Requirements means the rights conferred on authors of works by Part IX of the Copyright Act
1968 (Cth) and all present and future rights of integrity of authorship, rights of attribution of authorship,
rights not to have authorship falsely attributed, rights to alter or delete all or part of the Services, rights

anywhere in the world;
Licence means the CBS Group ’s licence of CBS Group Tools and CBS Group Workbooks to Downer
for the purposes of this Agreement as set out in clause 5 of this Agreement;
Original Energy Saver (OES) means the party that is paying or has paid for the activities that
increase the energy efficiency without reducing production or service levels, and which will become
the Original Energy Saver (OES) upon signing the approved Energy Savings Nomination form.
Probity Event means any event or thing which occurs before or after the date of this Agreement
which:
              a) has a material adverse effect on, or on the perception of, the character, integrity or
                 honesty of a Relevant Person;
              b) relates to a Relevant Person and has or may have a material adverse effect on the
                 public interest or public confidence in the Services; or
              c) involves a material failure of a Relevant Person or subcontractor to achieve or maintain:
                          a. reasonable standards of ethical behaviour
                          b. the avoidance of conflicts of interest which will have a material adverse effect
                             on the ability of the Relevant Person or subcontractor (as applicable) to carry
                             out and observe its obligations in connection with the Services; or
                          c.   other standards of conduct that would otherwise be expected of a Party
                               involved in a project of the same nature or type as the Project;
Project means the projects specified in Appendix D on which Downer and CBS have agreed to work
exclusively together for the purposes of securing Energy Saving Credits.
Reimbursable Expenses means expenses that are in addition to the Fees for Services for which
Downer has agreed in writing to reimburse CBS Group. If Downer has not provided prior written
approval I relation to a Reimbursable Expense, Downer will have no liability for (and CBS Group will
not be entitled to any payment for) that Reimbursable Expense.
Relevant Person means any officer, employee, consultant, representative, subcontractor, sub-
subcontractor or agent of CBS Group.
Requirements means any and all processes, procedures, duties and obligations that apply to the
Scheme and as otherwise set out in the CBS Group Workbooks;
Scheme see Environmental Scheme
Services means the services provided by CBS Group prescribed in Schedule Item 2;
Specifications means any and all specifications, standards and terms that set out the minimum
standards required under the Scheme, and as otherwise set out in the CBS Group Workbooks;
Term means the period from the Effective Date until this Agreement is ended in accordance with
clause 15;
Variation means:

2.      FUNDAMENTAL TERMS
2.1     Downer engages CBS Group to:
        (a)      provide the Services to Downer, and meet any applicable Legal Requirements and
                 Downers health and safety policies, procedures and requirements; and
        (b)      make payments to Downer as required by and in the manner specified in Schedule Item 4;
2.2     Downer agrees to satisfy Downer’s Obligations for the Term on the terms of this Agreement;
2.3     CBS Group is providing the Services as an independent contractor. CBS Group (including its
        employees and agents) is not in partnership or in a joint venture relationship with Downer and is
        not an employee, agent or servant of Downer. CBS Group will not and will ensure that its
        employees and agents to not, bind or commit, or purport to bind or commit, Downer in any way.
3.      DOWNER’S OBLIGATIONS
During the Term:
3.1     Downer must:
        (a)      pay the Fees for Services and Reimbursable Expenses when due; and
        (b)      give reasonable assistance, information, instructions and access to Downer’s premises
                 as reasonably required by CBS Group to enable CBS Group to provide the Services.
3.2     Downer will use best endeavours to:
        (a)      facilitate the support of the Customer to assist in meeting the requirements of the
                 Environmental Scheme as advised by CBS from time to time;
        (b)      provide relevant technical information and support for the Project delivered to the
                 Customer in order to assist in meeting the requirements of the Environmental Scheme;
        (c)      upon request by CBS Group or a Scheme auditor, provide access to Documentation,
                 Evidence and other information relevant to Downer’s Obligations and the Environmental
                 Scheme for the purposes of inspection or audit, and provide its full assistance to CBS
                 Group and the Scheme auditor in any inspection or audit or audit activity or the
                 implementation of any direction arising from an audit finding;
3.3     Downer must not:
        (a)      use the CBS Group Tools other than in satisfying Downer’s Obligations in accordance
                 with this Agreement;
        (b)      use the CBS Group Tools to create Certificates itself or assist third parties to create
                 Certificates;
        (c)      reverse engineer any of the CBS Group Tools; or
        (d)      disclose to third parties the CBS Group Tools or the details of any CBS Group Tools.
4.      CBS GROUP WARRANTIES
4.1      CBS Group acknowledges and agrees that in providing the Services;
        (a)      it has the requisite skills, experience and expertise and has reviewed, investigated,
                 analysed and understood all of the information provided to it; and
        (b)      it will at all times comply with all applicable Legal Requirements.
4.2     CBS Group must co-operate with Downer and any of its employees or other consultants
        engaged by Downer to complete the Services and co-ordinate the performance of the Services
        with any services being performed by other Consultants.

        (b)      are fit for the Intended Purpose;
        (c)      comply with all applicable Legal Requirements; and
        (d)      do not infringe any General Intellectual Property Rights of any person.
4.4     CBS Group must:
        (a)      ensure that each person engaged by CBS Group would not, in doing the work for which
                 they are engaged, be an Illegal Worker;
        (b)      remove, or cause to be removed, any Illegal Worker from any involvement in carrying out
                 the Services; and
        (c)      when requested in writing, provide evidence within 14 days that it has taken all
                 reasonable steps to ensure that it has complied with, and is complying with, its obligations
                 under this clause 4.4.
                 Illegal Worker means a person who is an Unlawful Non-Citizen who is working without a
                 visa, or a Non-Citizen who is performing work in breach of a Visa Work Condition;
                 Non-Citizen has the same meaning as under the Migration Act 1958 (Cth);
                 Unlawful Non-Citizen has the same meaning as under the Migration Act 1958 (Cth); and
                 Visa Work Condition means a condition (as set out in Schedule 8 of the Migration
                 Regulations 1994 (Cth) attached to a visa restricting the work that the Non-Citizen may do
                 in Australia.
5.      LICENCE OF CBS GROUP TOOLS
5.1     Subject to the provisions set out in clause 16.1(a) of this Agreement, CBS Group licences the
        CBS Group Tools to Downer during the Term of this Agreement.
5.2     Downer must only use the CBS Group Tools for the purposes of satisfying Downer’s Obligations
        under the terms of this Agreement.
5.3     CBS Group may, as it considers necessary, maintain, delete or modify the CBS Group Tools
        from time to time.
6.      PAYMENT AND VARIATIONS
6.1     Payments to CBS Group
        (a)      CBS Group must submit claims for payment of the Fees for Services strictly at the time
                 stated in, and strictly in accordance with Schedule Item 4 of this Agreement. CBS Group
                 is not entitled to make, and must not make, any claim for payment of the Fees for
                 Services otherwise than in accordance with Schedule Item 4. Each claim for payment of
                 the Fees for Services must be accompanied by details in support of the claim, certificates
                 of currency for each policy of insurance required to be held by CBS Group and such other
                 information as Downer may reasonably request.
        (b)      Within 10 Business Days of receipt of a claim for payment made by CBS Group in
                 accordance with clause 6.1, Downer will issue to CBS Group a payment schedule setting
                 out the amount it proposes to pay to CBS Group and the reasons for any difference.
        (c)      Provided that it has received a tax invoice for the amount assessed pursuant to clause
                 (b), subject to clause 6.1(f), Downer will pay to CBS Group the amount of the assessment
                 by the time for payment identified in Schedule Item 4.
        (d)      The Parties may mutually agree to a Variation to this Agreement, with such Variations
                 executed by both Parties in writing.
        (e)      Subject to clause 6.1(f), where the Parties have agreed a Variation in accordance with
                 clause (d), the adjustment (if any) to the Fees for Services will be as follows:
                 (i)      to the extent that the Parties have agreed the amount, then the amount agreed; or
                 (ii)     otherwise, using prices included in this Agreement and otherwise arriving at a
                          reasonable amount having regard to any increase or decrease in the costs that have
                          been or will be incurred by CBS Group as a direct result of the Variation.

7.      FAILURE EVENT
7.1     If a CBS Group Failure Event occurs, CBS Group must pay to Downer the amounts as specified
        in Schedule Item 4.
8.      DOWNER RESOURCES
8.1     Downer must ensure that it has the necessary resources and staff to satisfy Downer’s
        Obligations.
9.      GENERAL RELATIONSHIP
9.1     The Parties are independent of each other and no agency, employment, partnership or other
        relationship will exist between the Parties. Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed or
        construed to render either Party liable for any of the other Party’s costs obligations or
        expenditures pursuant to any relevant employment laws.
9.2     Downer retains the right to enter into any contract with any other person or persons, company or
        firm to perform services similar to those contemplated by this Agreement, provided that they do
        not interfere with Downer’s ability to comply with this Agreement.
10.     INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY & DOWNER INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
10.1 Downer has no proprietary right or interest in the Intellectual Property.
10.2 Downer must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world the
     Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from or
     similar to the Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.
10.3 Any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from any party of the information
     comprised in the Intellectual Property whether attributable in whole or in part to Downer, will
     become and remain the property of CBS Group.
10.4 CBS Group has no proprietary right or interest in Downer Intellectual Property.
10.5 CBS Group must not register or record or attempt to register or record anywhere in the world
     Downer Intellectual Property or any part of it or any patents, trademarks or designs derived from
     or similar to the Downer Intellectual Property or assist any other party to do so.
10.6 Any improvement or modification to or derivation or discovery from any Party of the information
     comprised in the Downer Intellectual Property whether attributable in whole or in part to CBS
     Group, will become and remain the property of Downer.
11.     GST
11.1 Unless otherwise expressly stated, prices, or other sums payable or consideration to be
     provided under or in accordance with this Agreement are exclusive of GST.
11.2 If a Party makes a taxable supply under or in connection with this Agreement, the other Party
     must pay to the supplier at the same time, and in addition to the GST-exclusive consideration,
     an amount equal to the GST payable on that supply.
11.3 The supplier must, as a precondition to the payment of GST, give the other Party a tax invoice
     by the end of the month in which the supply is made.
11.4 If an adjustment event arises in connection with a supply made under this Agreement, the
     supplier must give the other Party an adjustment note in accordance with the GST law.
11.5 If this Agreement requires one Party to pay for, reimburse or contribute to any expense, loss or
     outgoing suffered or incurred by the other Party, the amount required to be paid, reimbursed or

12.     PROGRESS REPORTS AND MEETINGS
12.1 Upon reasonable request by Downer, but not less than on a monthly basis, the Parties must
     participate in a meeting to review and discuss the following (for the avoidance of doubt the list
     below is not exhaustive):
     (a)    CBS Group’s program for the provision of the Services;
     (b)    CBS Group’s progress in supplying the Services, relative to the dates by which they are
            required to be performed under this Agreement;
     (c)    Downer’s compliance with its obligations and any issues that Downer can reasonably be
            required to rectify to allow CBS Group to perform the Services;
     (d)    any additional projects that may be added to Schedule Item 6 to generate value for both
            Parties; and
     (e)    any other matter related to this Agreement which is notified to CBS Group by Downer from
            time to time.
12.2 The Parties must record and maintain minutes of all meetings held in accordance with clause
     12.1, which must record at a minimum:
     (a)    the date the meeting was held;
     (b)    the names of the individuals who attended the meeting;
     (c)    any actions agreed to be performed by the Parties arising from the meeting; and
     (d)    the agreed timeframe to perform those actions recorded in accordance with clause 12.2(c).
12.3 CBS Group must deliver a report to Downer whenever CBS Group considers the services will not
     be provided within the time prescribed by this Agreement.
12.4 Upon request by Downer, CBS Group and Downer, at Downer’s discretion, must meet to review
     the progress of the performance of this Agreement including the matters described in clause 12.1.
13.     INSURANCE
13.1 Unless Downer agrees otherwise in writing, CBS Group must maintain:
     (a)     worker's compensation insurance as required by applicable law where the Services are
             provided;
     (b)     professional indemnity insurance providing coverage for CBS Group’s professional
             responsibilities to Downer with an insured amount of $[10,000,000]; and
     (c)     public and products liability insurance with an insured amount of $20,000,000 for any one
             occurrence, which covers the liability of CBS Group in respect of:
                    (A) bodily injury to any third party;
                    (B) third party property damage; and
                    (C) advertising liability
13.2 CBS Group must ensure that the public and products liability insurance:
     (a)     Provides that Downer is protected by a “Principal’s” indemnity extension covering Downer’s
             vicarious liability for the acts or omissions of other “Named Insureds” under such policy;
             and
     (b)     Includes a cross liability clause in which the insurer agrees that the policy applies as if a
             separate policy was issued to each “Named Insured” (with the exception of limits of liability).
13.3 CBS Group must ensure that each policy required by this Agreement is in force prior to the
     Effective Date and maintained for the duration of the Agreement Term, except for professional
     indemnity insurance, which shall be maintained for 6 years after the conclusion of the Agreement
     Term.
13.4 CBS Group must, when requested by Downer, promptly satisfy Downer that each contract of
     insurance it is required to procure under this clause 13 is current by providing to Downer
     certificates of currency or other evidence reasonably required by Downer.

15.     TERMINATION
15.1 Subject to clause 15.6 and 15.7, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon the provision
     of 90 days’ notice to the other Party in writing.
15.2 If either Party ("Defaulting Party"):
        (a)      commits a breach of this Agreement and fails to remedy such breach within fourteen (14)
                 days of receipt of a notice in writing requiring such breach to be remedied; or
        (b)      becomes insolvent or is made subject to an application for winding up or resolves to wind
                 up voluntarily (other than for the purposes of a solvent reconstruction), or has a receiver
                 (or similar functionary) or an administrator appointed in respect of any of its assets, or
                 ceases to carry on business or enters into a composition or arrangement with its creditors
                 generally; or
        (c)      commits a breach of this Agreement or the Scheme, and such breach is deemed by the
                 other Party to involve fraud or dishonesty; or
        (d)      there is a Change of Control in relation to the Default Party;
the other Party may terminate this Agreement immediately by giving written notice of termination to the
Defaulting Party.

15.4 Termination under this clause 15.3 will be effective immediately and will not affect any other
     rights available to Downer.
15.5 If Downer terminates this Agreement under clause 15.3, Downer is not required to make any
     further payment to CBS Group, and the rights and liabilities of the Parties shall be the same as
     they would be at common law if CBS Group had wrongfully repudiated this Agreement and
     Downer had elected to treat this Agreement as at an end and recover damages.
15.6 If CBS Group terminates this Agreement in accordance with clause 15.1, CBS Group must
     reimburse Downer for all amounts paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with clause 6.1
     as at the date of termination of this Agreement.
15.7 If Downer terminates this Agreement in accordance with clause 15.1, Downer is not entitled to
     claim reimbursement of amounts paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with clause 6.1
     as at the date of termination of this Agreement.
15.8 If Downer terminates this agreement in accordance with clause 15.2 or 15.3, CBS Group must
     reimburse Downer for all amounts paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with clause 6.1
     as at the date of termination of this Agreement.
16.     CONSEQUENCES OF TERMINATION
16.1 On termination of this Agreement, for any reason listed in Clause 15:
        (a)      the Licence granted by CBS Group to Downer to use the CBS Group Tools is
                 immediately terminated, and Downer must cease to use the CBS Group Tools and as
                 soon as reasonably practical return to CBS Group or destroy any and all of the CBS
                 Group Tools; and
        (b)      both Parties must, as soon as reasonably practical, return to the other Party or destroy
                 any and all of any their Confidential Information and their Intellectual Property in its
                 possession or control including any variation, modification or improvement; and
16.2 Termination of the Agreement shall not relieve a Party of liability to the other Party in respect of
     the rights or remedies of the other Party which have accrued prior to termination. For the
     avoidance of doubt, Downer’s right to proceeds from the sale of Environmental Products
     (Certificate) in accordance with clause 6.2 survive termination of this Agreement.

     acceptability, fitness for purpose, condition, description or performance of the either Parties
     services (the Services or Downer’s Obligations) offered to the other Party or any contractual
     remedy for their failure.
17.2 Neither Party is liable to the other Party in any way arising under or in connection with the
     provision of the Services;
        (a)      for any indirect or consequential losses or expenses suffered by either Party or any third
                 party, howsoever caused, including but not limited to loss of turnover, profits, business or
                 goodwill or any liability to any other party.
17.3 Nothing in this Agreement is to be interpreted as excluding, restricting or modifying or having
     the effect of excluding, restricting or modifying the application of any State or Federal legislation
     applicable to the sale of goods or supply of services which cannot be excluded, restricted or
     modified.

20.1 Any Dispute between Downer and CBS Group may be notified by a Party to the other Party and
     the Parties must:
        (a)      within 10 days of the notification of the Dispute, meet to negotiate, in good faith, a
                 resolution of the Dispute; and
        (b)      if negotiation fails to achieve a resolution of the Dispute within 10 days of the notification
                 of Dispute, participate in a mediation to attempt to resolve the Dispute, in which case:

                       and appointed by agreement of the Parties or, failing agreement within seven days

21.     TAXES, EMPLOYEE BENEFITS AND WORKERS
21.1 CBS Group acknowledges that it has sole liability in relation to:
        (a)      wages, salaries, allowances, incentive payments, bonuses, sick leave, long service leave,
                 annual leave, personal leave, termination or redundancy payments and any other
                 employment related liabilities; and
        (b)      all group tax, payroll tax, PAYE, superannuation, workers compensation and other taxes,
                 levies, deductions, charges, penalties, duties or withholdings,
        in respect of CBS Group’s personnel.
21.2 CBS Group further acknowledges that it does not have (and its personnel do not have),
     pursuant to this Agreement or otherwise, any entitlement from Downer in relation to any form of
     employment benefit. CBS Group will not communicate or otherwise represent to its personnel
     that any of CBS Group’s personnel have any entitlement from Downer in relation to any form of
     employment related benefit.
21.3 CBS Group indemnifies Downer from and against any liability for the Indemnified Taxes,
     together with any other amount determined by Downer to be reasonably necessary to
     compensate Downer for any income tax liability, or reduction in income tax losses available to
     be carried forward or available income tax credits or rebates, to ensure that the after tax position
     of Downer is the same (or substantially the same) as it would have been had Downer never
     been liable for any Indemnified Taxes.
21.4 If Downer is, becomes or reasonably forms the view that it may be or become, liable for the
     payment of any Indemnified Taxes, Downer may deduct the amount of its liability or prospective
     liability for the Indemnified Taxes from any amount due by Downer to CBS Group, whether
     under this Agreement or otherwise.
22.     CONFLICT OF INTEREST AND BUSINESS ETHICS
22.1 CBS Group will at all times comply with:
        (a)      all Legal Requirements relating to anti-bribery, anti-corruption and anti-money laundering,
                 including the Criminal Code Act 1995 (Cth), the Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of the United
                 States of America, and the Bribery Act 2010 of the United Kingdom (and must not do
                 anything, or omit to do anything, to cause Downer to be in breach of any such Legal
                 Requirements); and
        (b)      Downer’s ‘Standards of Business Conduct’ and ‘Anti Bribery and Corruption Policy’ as
                 published from time to time.

     governmental, administrative or regulatory body regarding any offence or alleged offence, under
     any applicable Legal Requirements (including the Criminal Code Act 1995 (Cth), the Foreign
     Corrupt Practices Act of the United States of America, and the Bribery Act 2010 of the United
     Kingdom) relating to anti-bribery, anti-corruption and anti-money laundering.
22.3 At any time before the expiry of three years from the completion or termination of this
     Agreement, CBS Group must permit Downer, its nominee, or the Principal (in this clause, each
     the Auditor) to conduct an audit and investigation:
        (a)      to verify CBS Group’s compliance with this clause 21 (irrespective of whether there has
                 been a breach of suspected breach); and/or
        (b)      in relation to a Probity Event (or suspected Probity Event)
22.4 As part of an audit or investigation under this clause, CBS Group must:
        (a)      make available to the Auditor (and allow the Auditor to make and retain copies of) any
                 documents, records and other information in the power, possession or control of CBS
                 Group; and
        (b)      meet with the Auditor and make available CBS Group’s personnel to meet with the Auditor
                 and answer reasonable questions in relation to the subject matter of the audit.
22.5 CBS Group must give Downer notice immediately upon becoming aware of a Probity Event or
     any breach (or suspected breach) of this clause 21.
23.     MODERN SLAVERY
23.1 CBS Group must take (and must ensure that each of its Relevant Persons take) all reasonable
     steps to ensure that no modern slavery, human trafficking, servitude, forced labour, bonded
     labour, child labour, slavery or forced marriage or deceptive recruiting for labour or services
     (Modern Slavery) is present in CBS Group’s, or its Relevant Persons’, supply chains, or in any
     part of their businesses.
23.2 CBS Group warrants that neither it nor any of its Relevant Persons:
        (a)      has been convicted of any offence involving Modern Slavery or Modern Slavery-like
                 practices; and
        (b)      having made reasonable enquiries, has been or is, to the best of its knowledge, the subject
                 of any investigation, inquiry or enforcement proceedings by any governmental,
                 administrative or regulatory body regarding any offence of alleged offence of or in
                 connection to any form of Modern Slavery.
23.3 CBS Group must implement due diligence procedures for its Relevant Persons to minimise the
     risk of Modern Slavery in its supply chains.
23.4 CBS Group must implement a system (to a standard that is consistent with good industry
     practices) for monitoring and evaluating the effectiveness of CBS Group’s actions taken under
     clauses 23.1 and 23.3.
23.5 CBS Group must promptly notify Downer:
        (a)      as soon as it becomes aware of any actual or suspected Modern Slavery in its or any of its
                 Relevant Persons’ supply chain which is connected to this Agreement (Suspected
                 Slavery); and
        (b)      its approach to address and remediate the Suspected Slavery.
23.6 CBS Group must, on request from Downer (from time to time), provide all assistance and
     information reasonably necessary for Downer to prepare a modern slavery statement and
     otherwise comply with its obligations at law and Legal Requirements.
23.7 CBS Group must maintain a complete set of records to trace the supply chain of all goods and
     services provided to Downer in connection with this Agreement, including:
        (a)      the steps taken by CBS Group under clause 23.1; and
        (b)      the due diligence processes taken under clauses 23.3 and 23.4.
23.8 Downer will at any time have the right to inspect and to copy the whole or part of any record
     referred to in clause 23.7.

     23.7, CBS Group must promptly provide a copy of that record to Downer.
23.10 CBS Group must train all of its officers and employees on the risks of Modern Slavery and on
      Downer’s policies and procedures in relation to Modern Slavery, including the principles set out
      in Downer’s ‘Standards of Business Conduct’.
23.11 CBS Group must keep a record of all training with respect to modern slavery completed by its
      officers and employees and shall make a copy of the training record available to Downer on
      request.
24.     ASSIGNMENT
24.1 Neither Party may assign, charge or deal with the Agreement without the prior written consent of
     the other Party, which must not be unreasonably withheld.
24.2 Subject to 24.1, if a Party assigns its interests under this Agreement, the assigning Party must
     ensure that any assignee first covenants to be bound by the respective Party’s rights and

The covenants, conditions, provisions and warranties contained in this Agreement shall not merge or
terminate upon completion of the transactions contemplated herein but to the extent that they have not
been fulfilled and satisfied or are capable of having effect shall remain in full force and effect.

26.1 The Parties agree to do all things and execute all documents necessary to give effect to the
     terms of this Agreement.

     to be taken under this Agreement, including in relation to termination.

Each Party will bear its own legal and accounting costs incurred in respect of the preparation,
negotiation and execution of this Agreement.

28.1 If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then it must be interpreted as
     narrowly as necessary to allow it to be enforceable or valid.
28.2 If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then:
        (a)      the provision (or part of it) must be severed from this Agreement;
        (b)      the remaining provisions (and remaining part of the provision) are valid and enforceable.

29.2 The Parties agree to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of New South Wales, the
     Federal Court of Australia, and of courts entitled to hear appeals from those courts.

30.1 An amendment or change is only effective once it is:
        (a)      made in writing; and
        (b)      executed by all Parties.

31.1 This is the entire Agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement.
31.2 There is no other Agreement, understanding, warranty or representation, whether oral or

When interpreting this Agreement:
        (a)      headings are for convenience only and do not affect the interpretation of this Agreement;

        (c)      any gender includes any other gender;

        (e)      reference to a person includes a natural person, corporation, partnership, association or

        (f)      reference to legislation includes that legislation as amended or modified or re-enacted;
                 and
        (g)      reference to a provision or clause means a provision or clause in this Agreement.

    APPENDIX A –SERVICES
Unless subject to a Variation under clause 6.1(d), all Services shall be provided solely in relation to the
FE04 HVAC Energy Efficiency project – applicable to the SGT and Waratah fleets and subject to the
modification approval by Sydney Trains.
The diagram below provides a high-level overview of the process activities associated with the
generation of Energy Saving Certificates for energy saving modifications to the Sydney Trains fleet.
For avoidance of doubt, with the exception of the scope assigned to Downer Group listed below, all
works and costs are the responsibility of CBS Group.



Figure 1           Process Activities




CBS Group is responsible for all works associated with the generation of Energy Saving Certificates
for the modifications, including:
       a.    Development of energy saving models
       b.    Management of data, process and records in accordance with the requirements of the scheme
       c.    Appointment and fees associated audits, certificates, application and transactions.
       d.    Management, communication (reporting) and administration to Downer Group in accordance
             with an agreed project management plan.
During the Term and in accordance with this Agreement, CBS Group must:
§      licence and provide the necessary CBS Group Tools and guidance to assist Downer record the
       Evidence and other information necessary to meet the obligations under the Environmental
       Scheme;
§      guide Downer through the steps necessary to meet the requirements of the Environmental
       Scheme;
§      provide to Downer timely communication on changes to the Environmental Scheme:
§      keep confidential, or depersonalise prior to disclosure, details of all of Downer’s Obligations
       proposed by Downer under the Agreement and only disclose the information required under the
       relevant Environmental Scheme;
§      provide and review quality assurance procedures to aid the accurate creation of Certificates;
§      perform a quality assurance procedure and alert Downer’s to any missing information within 30
       Business Days of receipt of information confirming completion of Downer’s Obligations provided to
       a Customer;
    CBS Group Pty Ltd                                                               Environmental Energy Finance – Master Services
    Level 3, 480 Collins Street, Melbourne VIC 3000                                 Agreement                                        Page 18

Melbourne ● Sydney ● Gold Coast ● Singapore ● Auckland ● San Francisco ● New York
§      provide training and support as may be necessary covering the relevant Environmental Scheme
       and the CBS Group Tools; and
§      retain all records arising from the provision of Downer’s Obligations for a period of at least six
       years or as specified by the Scheme requirements; and
With respect to the creation of Certificates, CBS Group must:
§      Register a Certificate creator under the relevant Scheme;
§      promptly create Certificates attributable to Downer following receipt of the relevant information;
       and
§      CBS Group must make payments to Downer based on Schedule Item 4
§      With respect to the creation of Environmental Products, if these are deemed ineligible for due to
       fraud or negligence by the Customer or Downer, Downer has no claim against CBS Group; and
Promptly make all disbursements to Downer arising from the receipt of proceeds from the sale of
Energy Certificates in accordance with Schedule Item 4
