 BACKGROUND & Acknowledgements
      A. Downer carries on a business that at times involves advice and services on the procurement,
         design, installation, servicing and/or maintenance of efficient end-user equipment by their
         Customers which may be entitled for the creation of Environmental Products (Certificates)
         under the Environmental Schemes.

      B. Downer and CBS Group have agreed to partner in the novel application of Environmental
         Schemes to mobile plant. A particular application of this partnership is the pursuit of Energy
         Saving Certificates from the NSW Energy Savings Scheme resulting from modifications to the
         Sydney Trains SGT and Waratah fleets maintained by Downer.

      C. Downer wishes to engage CBS Group to provide the services required to generate value from
         Energy Savings Certificates under the NSW Energy Savings Scheme, and CBS Group has
         agreed to provide the Services on the terms set out in this Agreement. CBS Group will work
         with Downer to collect evidence. Downer will receive payments based on the successful
         registration of certificates for the nominated projects (Scheme Activities)

      D. CBS Group has represented to Downer that it has the skill, experience, personnel, material
         and capacity to supply the Services in accordance with this Agreement.

 OPERATIVE PART
1.      DEFINITIONS
1.1     In this Agreement:
Agreement means this agreement (including any schedules, appendices or annexures);
Arbitration is a method of dispute resolution whereby a dispute raised under clause 19 is finally
resolved as follows:
        (a)      the arbitration will be finally resolved under the Resolution Institute Arbitration Rules;
        (b)      there will be one arbitrator, appointed in accordance with the Resolution Institute
                 Arbitration Rules;
        (c)      the arbitrator must give written reasons for their award;
        (d)      the seat of the arbitration will be Sydney, New South Wales;
        (e)      the arbitration will be conducted in English.
Business Day means Monday to Friday excluding public holidays;
CBS Group Failure Event Date means 5pm on the 15th day after the final Monthly Project
Management Fee was paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with Schedule Item 3 and 4.
CBS Group Failure Event occurs when:
        (a)      Downer has satisfied its obligations to perform any agreed actions recorded in accordance
                 with clause 12.2(c) and 12.2(d); and
        (b)      CBS Group fails to obtain and sell any Environmental Products before the CBS Group
                 Failure Event Date.
CBS Group Tools means CBS Group ’s systems, data collection tools and record keeping tools
designed to assist Downer to meet obligations under the Scheme, including but not limited to
workflows, forms, checklists, spreadsheets, software and related documents;
CBS Group Workbooks means the Workbooks, checklists, training materials, and guidelines
authored by or on behalf of CBS Group as published and updated from time to time, setting out,
without limitation, the Accreditations, the Requirements, the Specifications, the Approved Products
and the Documentation;
Certificates means the Environmental Products, as appropriate;
Certificate Registration means the process of registering ownership of the Certificates with the
Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) Registry following notification by the ESS Registry that the Certificates
are available for registration.

which:
              a) has a material adverse effect on, or on the perception of, the character, integrity or
                 honesty of a Relevant Person;

clause 15;

2.      FUNDAMENTAL TERMS
2.1     Downer engages CBS Group to:
        (a)      provide the Services to Downer, and meet any applicable Legal Requirements and
                 Downers health and safety policies, procedures and requirements; and
        (b)      make payments to Downer as required by and in the manner specified in Schedule Item 4;
2.2     Downer agrees to satisfy Downer’s Obligations for the Term on the terms of this Agreement;
2.3     CBS Group is providing the Services as an independent contractor. CBS Group (including its
        employees and agents) is not in partnership or in a joint venture relationship with Downer and is
        not an employee, agent or servant of Downer. CBS Group will not and will ensure that its
        employees and agents to not, bind or commit, or purport to bind or commit, Downer in any way.
3.      DOWNER’S OBLIGATIONS
During the Term:
3.1     Downer must:
        (a)      pay the Fees for Services and Reimbursable Expenses when due; and
        (b)      give reasonable assistance, information, instructions and access to Downer’s premises
                 as reasonably required by CBS Group to enable CBS Group to provide the Services.
3.2     Downer will use best endeavours to:
        (a)      facilitate the support of the Customer to assist in meeting the requirements of the
                 Environmental Scheme as advised by CBS from time to time;
        (b)      provide relevant technical information and support for the Project delivered to the
                 Customer in order to assist in meeting the requirements of the Environmental Scheme;
        (c)      upon request by CBS Group or a Scheme auditor, provide access to Documentation,
                 Evidence and other information relevant to Downer’s Obligations and the Environmental
                 Scheme for the purposes of inspection or audit, and provide its full assistance to CBS
                 Group and the Scheme auditor in any inspection or audit or audit activity or the
                 implementation of any direction arising from an audit finding;
3.3     Downer must not:
        (a)      use the CBS Group Tools other than in satisfying Downer’s Obligations in accordance
                 with this Agreement;
        (b)      use the CBS Group Tools to create Certificates itself or assist third parties to create
                 Certificates;
        (c)      reverse engineer any of the CBS Group Tools; or
        (d)      disclose to third parties the CBS Group Tools or the details of any CBS Group Tools.

4.1      CBS Group acknowledges and agrees that in providing the Services;
        (a)      it has the requisite skills, experience and expertise and has reviewed, investigated,
                 analysed and understood all of the information provided to it; and
        (b)      it will at all times comply with all applicable Legal Requirements.

        engaged by Downer to complete the Services and co-ordinate the performance of the Services
        with any services being performed by other Consultants.

12.     PROGRESS REPORTS AND MEETINGS
12.1 Upon reasonable request by Downer, but not less than on a monthly basis, the Parties must
     participate in a meeting to review and discuss the following (for the avoidance of doubt the list
     below is not exhaustive):
     (a)    CBS Group’s program for the provision of the Services;
     (b)    CBS Group’s progress in supplying the Services, relative to the dates by which they are
            required to be performed under this Agreement;
     (c)    Downer’s compliance with its obligations and any issues that Downer can reasonably be
            required to rectify to allow CBS Group to perform the Services;
     (d)    any additional projects that may be added to Schedule Item 6 to generate value for both
            Parties; and
     (e)    any other matter related to this Agreement which is notified to CBS Group by Downer from
            time to time.
12.2 The Parties must record and maintain minutes of all meetings held in accordance with clause
     12.1, which must record at a minimum:
     (a)    the date the meeting was held;
     (b)    the names of the individuals who attended the meeting;
     (c)    any actions agreed to be performed by the Parties arising from the meeting; and
     (d)    the agreed timeframe to perform those actions recorded in accordance with clause 12.2(c).
12.3 CBS Group must deliver a report to Downer whenever CBS Group considers the services will not
     be provided within the time prescribed by this Agreement.
12.4 Upon request by Downer, CBS Group and Downer, at Downer’s discretion, must meet to review
     the progress of the performance of this Agreement including the matters described in clause 12.1.

                    (C) advertising liability
13.2 CBS Group must ensure that the public and products liability insurance:

             separate policy was issued to each “Named Insured” (with the exception of limits of liability).

15.     TERMINATION
15.1 Subject to clause 15.6 and 15.7, either Party may terminate this Agreement upon the provision
     of 90 days’ notice to the other Party in writing.
15.2 If either Party ("Defaulting Party"):
        (a)      commits a breach of this Agreement and fails to remedy such breach within fourteen (14)
                 days of receipt of a notice in writing requiring such breach to be remedied; or
        (b)      becomes insolvent or is made subject to an application for winding up or resolves to wind
                 up voluntarily (other than for the purposes of a solvent reconstruction), or has a receiver
                 (or similar functionary) or an administrator appointed in respect of any of its assets, or
                 ceases to carry on business or enters into a composition or arrangement with its creditors
                 generally; or
        (c)      commits a breach of this Agreement or the Scheme, and such breach is deemed by the
                 other Party to involve fraud or dishonesty; or
        (d)      there is a Change of Control in relation to the Default Party;
the other Party may terminate this Agreement immediately by giving written notice of termination to the
Defaulting Party.

     reimburse Downer for all amounts paid by Downer to CBS Group in accordance with clause 6.1

16.     CONSEQUENCES OF TERMINATION
16.1 On termination of this Agreement, for any reason listed in Clause 15:
        (a)      the Licence granted by CBS Group to Downer to use the CBS Group Tools is
                 immediately terminated, and Downer must cease to use the CBS Group Tools and as
                 soon as reasonably practical return to CBS Group or destroy any and all of the CBS
                 Group Tools; and
        (b)      both Parties must, as soon as reasonably practical, return to the other Party or destroy
                 any and all of any their Confidential Information and their Intellectual Property in its
                 possession or control including any variation, modification or improvement; and
16.2 Termination of the Agreement shall not relieve a Party of liability to the other Party in respect of
     the rights or remedies of the other Party which have accrued prior to termination. For the
     avoidance of doubt, Downer’s right to proceeds from the sale of Environmental Products
     (Certificate) in accordance with clause 6.2 survive termination of this Agreement.

21.1 CBS Group acknowledges that it has sole liability in relation to:

                 annual leave, personal leave, termination or redundancy payments and any other
                 employment related liabilities; and

                 levies, deductions, charges, penalties, duties or withholdings,
        in respect of CBS Group’s personnel.

     pursuant to this Agreement or otherwise, any entitlement from Downer in relation to any form of
     employment benefit. CBS Group will not communicate or otherwise represent to its personnel
     that any of CBS Group’s personnel have any entitlement from Downer in relation to any form of
     employment related benefit.
21.3 CBS Group indemnifies Downer from and against any liability for the Indemnified Taxes,
     together with any other amount determined by Downer to be reasonably necessary to
     compensate Downer for any income tax liability, or reduction in income tax losses available to
     be carried forward or available income tax credits or rebates, to ensure that the after tax position
     of Downer is the same (or substantially the same) as it would have been had Downer never
     been liable for any Indemnified Taxes.
21.4 If Downer is, becomes or reasonably forms the view that it may be or become, liable for the
     payment of any Indemnified Taxes, Downer may deduct the amount of its liability or prospective
     liability for the Indemnified Taxes from any amount due by Downer to CBS Group, whether

                 Group; and
        (b)      meet with the Auditor and make available CBS Group’s personnel to meet with the Auditor
                 and answer reasonable questions in relation to the subject matter of the audit.
22.5 CBS Group must give Downer notice immediately upon becoming aware of a Probity Event or
     any breach (or suspected breach) of this clause 21.
23.     MODERN SLAVERY
23.1 CBS Group must take (and must ensure that each of its Relevant Persons take) all reasonable
     steps to ensure that no modern slavery, human trafficking, servitude, forced labour, bonded
     labour, child labour, slavery or forced marriage or deceptive recruiting for labour or services
     (Modern Slavery) is present in CBS Group’s, or its Relevant Persons’, supply chains, or in any
     part of their businesses.
23.2 CBS Group warrants that neither it nor any of its Relevant Persons:
        (a)      has been convicted of any offence involving Modern Slavery or Modern Slavery-like
                 practices; and
        (b)      having made reasonable enquiries, has been or is, to the best of its knowledge, the subject
                 of any investigation, inquiry or enforcement proceedings by any governmental,
                 administrative or regulatory body regarding any offence of alleged offence of or in
                 connection to any form of Modern Slavery.
23.3 CBS Group must implement due diligence procedures for its Relevant Persons to minimise the
     risk of Modern Slavery in its supply chains.
23.4 CBS Group must implement a system (to a standard that is consistent with good industry
     practices) for monitoring and evaluating the effectiveness of CBS Group’s actions taken under
     clauses 23.1 and 23.3.
23.5 CBS Group must promptly notify Downer:
        (a)      as soon as it becomes aware of any actual or suspected Modern Slavery in its or any of its
                 Relevant Persons’ supply chain which is connected to this Agreement (Suspected
                 Slavery); and
        (b)      its approach to address and remediate the Suspected Slavery.
23.6 CBS Group must, on request from Downer (from time to time), provide all assistance and
     information reasonably necessary for Downer to prepare a modern slavery statement and
     otherwise comply with its obligations at law and Legal Requirements.
23.7 CBS Group must maintain a complete set of records to trace the supply chain of all goods and
     services provided to Downer in connection with this Agreement, including:
        (a)      the steps taken by CBS Group under clause 23.1; and
        (b)      the due diligence processes taken under clauses 23.3 and 23.4.
23.8 Downer will at any time have the right to inspect and to copy the whole or part of any record
     referred to in clause 23.7.

23.9 Upon receiving a request in writing from Downer for a copy of any record referred to in clause
     23.7, CBS Group must promptly provide a copy of that record to Downer.
23.10 CBS Group must train all of its officers and employees on the risks of Modern Slavery and on
      Downer’s policies and procedures in relation to Modern Slavery, including the principles set out
      in Downer’s ‘Standards of Business Conduct’.
23.11 CBS Group must keep a record of all training with respect to modern slavery completed by its
      officers and employees and shall make a copy of the training record available to Downer on
      request.
24.     ASSIGNMENT
24.1 Neither Party may assign, charge or deal with the Agreement without the prior written consent of
     the other Party, which must not be unreasonably withheld.
24.2 Subject to 24.1, if a Party assigns its interests under this Agreement, the assigning Party must
     ensure that any assignee first covenants to be bound by the respective Party’s rights and
     obligations hereunder.
25.     DURATION OF PROVISIONS
The covenants, conditions, provisions and warranties contained in this Agreement shall not merge or
terminate upon completion of the transactions contemplated herein but to the extent that they have not
been fulfilled and satisfied or are capable of having effect shall remain in full force and effect.
26.     PARTIES TO DO ALL THINGS NECESSARY
26.1 The Parties agree to do all things and execute all documents necessary to give effect to the
     terms of this Agreement.
26.2 The Parties must cooperate in the execution of all documents necessary to complete any action
     to be taken under this Agreement, including in relation to termination.
27.     COSTS
Each Party will bear its own legal and accounting costs incurred in respect of the preparation,
negotiation and execution of this Agreement.
28.     SEVERABILITY
28.1 If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then it must be interpreted as
     narrowly as necessary to allow it to be enforceable or valid.
28.2 If a provision (or part of it) is held to be unenforceable or invalid, then:
        (a)      the provision (or part of it) must be severed from this Agreement;
        (b)      the remaining provisions (and remaining part of the provision) are valid and enforceable.
29.     NEW SOUTH WALES LAW AND JURISDICTION
29.1 The law of New South Wales as amended from time to time governs this Agreement.
29.2 The Parties agree to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of New South Wales, the
     Federal Court of Australia, and of courts entitled to hear appeals from those courts.
30.     AMENDING THIS AGREEMENT
30.1 An amendment or change is only effective once it is:
        (a)      made in writing; and
        (b)      executed by all Parties.
31.     THIS IS THE ENTIRE AGREEMENT
31.1 This is the entire Agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter of this Agreement.
31.2 There is no other Agreement, understanding, warranty or representation, whether oral or
     written, binding the Parties concerning any aspect of this Agreement.
32.     NO WAIVER
32.1 A Party may exercise its rights at any time and does not waive those even if that Party:

        (b)      delayed or omitted to exercise its rights.
32.2 A waiver is only effective:
        (a)      if it is signed by the Party granting the waiver; and
        (b)      to the extent set out in the waiver.
33.     INTERPRETATION
When interpreting this Agreement:
        (a)      headings are for convenience only and do not affect the interpretation of this Agreement;
        (b)      the singular includes the plural and the plural includes the singular;
        (c)      any gender includes any other gender;

                                     will be calculated based on 70% of $90 multiplied by the number of

                                     certificates. Therefore, the payment owing to Downer for a single

## SJT_Pakenham Executive Deck Speakers Notes_062722.docx

*File: `ESS Nomination Form/SJT_Pakenham Executive Deck Speakers Notes_062722.docx`*

Speaker Notes

Slide One

Pakenham site exists within several important Federal and State Policy and Infrastructure contexts:

Policy: REZ, M1 Strategic Corridor Plan, Victorian Hydrogen Plan and State has announced that Capacity Market will use only Renewables.

Infrastructure: 

M1: 60m from Melbourne CBD, Adjacent to heavily trafficked M1 – Section 6 corridor: Nar Nar Goon to Traralgon with key towns of 10k Pop along the route. (Morewell, Moe, Warragul, Drouin,  

Access to stable capacity HV lines (Traralgon to CBD), Adjacent to major gas pipelines, 56km to Port of Hastings via South Gippsland Hwy, Access to Desalination at Wonthaggi.

Our approach: evaluate the opportunity for additional PV power generation and expanded use of power behind and beyond the meter.

Doubling the current solar and investigating wind generation shows the potential for the site to be a Large-Scale Energy provider. 

Consider the use of PV power and site water (and/or desalination water) to establish a Green Hydrogen plant: Energy outputs may include Hydrogen pumped directly to Gas Pipeline, Capacity Power Market participation via the existing HV transmission lines, Hydrogen fuel (M1) and Hydrogen export (Port of Hastings)

Slide Two:

Political Context. Victoria is the at the forefront of Treaty discussions with Indigenous groups. 

Historical Context Pakenham/Nar Nar Goon is low lying swamp lands that provided a rich fishing and hunting ground for the tribes and clans of the Kulin Nation for millennia. Today, the protected fauna and flora recall this history.

Our approach: Respect and re-integrate the cultural history of the site into carbon plots that become eligible for Gold Standard ™ certification. Gold Standard projects layer additional value by contributing to the achievement of UD Sustainability Development Goals in addition to decarbonisation.  The nearby Cardinia Environmental Coalition is the largest native plant nursery in Victoria. Early feasibility shows the potential for 50 Hectares to be regenerated using Native or Mallee Planting (FullCAM method) in accordance with the requirements of the Emissions Reductions Fund.

Site Climate and Weather – the site is well suited to wind generation either rooftop turbines or freestanding as (in contrast to solar) the cut in of 10mph for turbines is available more often than the sunlight hours required to generate solar. 

The site is relatively suitable for large scale battery storage (requires  design for optimal operating environment as 40% loss in cold weather.

The wind patterns are also suited to the passive air flow requirements for Mechanical Trees.

Slide Three: Options Review

Additional Solar

Context: current solar provides @30% of the 1000 MGh used annually. Majority of power usage is at night. 

Hypothesis: we can ‘close the gap’ and generate an additional 700MWh per annum by doubling the existing rooftop array and providing battery storage capacity (15 + Tesla Powerwall2’s)

We could also expand PV and storage to create surplus power for use behind the meter (EV charging, Green Hydrogen) or beyond the meter (Green Hydrogen or a Virtual Energy Network)

Notes/Issues

Cost to double PV and 100% battery storage Circa 800k. Eligibility for Large Scale Energy Certificates (paid retrospectively) could contribute circa 250k.

Wind Turbines

Context: The site is better suited to wind generation than solar – see climate notes – however there has been strong community opposition to prior wind projects so stakeholder consultation is critical.

Hypothesis: Roof mounted or freestanding turbines to be investigated

Notes/Issues

Price circa 1m – payback period estimated at 9 – 12months

Battery Storage

Context: No battery storage on site

Hypothesis: Site energy needs require storage to service night and peak demands. (TBC). Current options are from China or Tesla. (Circa 15k/unit x 15 – 20 units)

Notes/Issues Battery install will need to be designed to avoid frosts and temps below 10 degrees.

EV Charging

Context: M1 adjacency

Hypothesis: May work with a Downer Fleet strategy or for a Downer Client, or General Public. (GP)

Notes: established technology, there is also a Shell service station nearby that could be used for GP delivery.

Reafforestation, Future Carbon Plots, Gold Standard Credits

Context:

Site is eligible for Native Planting or Mallee and consequent ACCU’s. Estimated yields are 290tCO2e/Ha and 7000 ACCU’s. 

Carbon heat map shown using FullCAM methodology

Future Carbon Plots – are 1 Hectare plots planted to mimic the future climate. They assist in preserving fauna who use these plots to begin adaptation to future conditions.

Gold Standard certified projects contribute to the achievement of UD Sustainability Development Goals in addition to decarbonisation.  

Hypothesis: develop and certify a Gold Standard project that creates higher value credits, a Target price of $65 – 70 v. ACCU of $38 – 50. This can be achieved using indigenous land care practices and in consultation with the community.

Note that the Kulin Nation Seasonal Calendar has three seasons related to activity of the Pakenham site: Kangaroo Apple Season (December), Dry Season (Jan-Feb), Eel Season (March), Wombat Season (April-August), Orchid Season (September), Tadpole Season (October), Grass-flowering Season (November).

## Transport and Downer - ESS Side Deed Downer Comments - Sydney Trains Response(800744152.1) (1) copy.docx

*File: `ESS Nomination Form/Transport and Downer - ESS Side Deed Downer Comments - Sydney Trains Response(800744152.1) (1) copy.docx`*

ESS Side Deed Downer Comments (23 August 2022) – Sydney Trains Response

We set out in the table below our responses to Downer's comments on and requested amendments to the ESS Side Deed. We have not included the minor tidy up changes requested by Downer in the table below. These have been incorporated directly into the revised draft of the ESS Side Deed.

The clause references in the table below are to the clauses in the revised draft of the ESS Side Deed.

ESS Side Deed – Additional Amendments by Sydney Trains

In addition to the changes we have noted in the table above as being incorporated into the revised draft of the ESS Side Deed, we have also made the following amendments of note. We have not included minor tidy up changes (such as corrections to cross-references and definitions) in the table below. These have been incorporated directly into the revised draft of the ESS Side Deed.



|  | Clause Ref. | Downer Comment / Requested Amendment | Sydney Trains Response | Downer Response |

|  | Definitions of 'ACP's Fee' and 'Net ESC Sale Proceeds' | For simplicity:
the 'Supplier's Fee' should embed the ACP's Fee and all other associated costs; and
the 'Net ESC Sale Proceeds' should be calculated as the ESC Sale Proceeds minus the Supplier's Fee (rather than the ACP's Fee). | The proposed amendments suggest that the ACP will pass through to Downer the total amount of ESC Sale Proceeds (without deduction for the ACP's Fee), with Downer to then repay the ACP for its costs.
Our drafting was based on the assumption that, as the ACP would be receiving the ESC Sale Proceeds, it would keep 30% of those proceeds to cover its ACP's Fee, and then pay the balance to Downer, from which Downer would then keep the Supplier's Fee and pay the balance to TfNSW. We understand this is how the payments are intended to flow in practice, and the drafting should therefore reflect this. On this basis, we have not incorporated this amendment into the revised draft of the Side Deed. | DC - Okay, closed. |

|  | Definition of 'Supplier's Fee' | In response to TfNSW's query as to what is covered by the Supplier's Fee that is over and above what is covered by the ACP's Fee, Downer has advised that the Supplier's Fee broadly covers the activities outlined in clauses 5.1(b) (particularly (iii) to (viii)), 7 and 8.
Downer will provide an estimate breakdown in dollars, however the Supplier's Fee will be converted to a % of ESC Sale Proceeds, for consistency with this Deed (and the principle of TfNSW holding no risk as to costs). | [*]
[Note: TfNSW to confirm whether a % of ESC Sale Proceeds for the Supplier's Fee is acceptable.] | DC – Downer to provide further information by 7th September. |

|  | Cl 4(b)(iii) | Downer has requested that the conditions to be satisfied before the Amended Variation Order is implemented includes all other reasonably required steps which are necessary to enable the ESCs to be created. | We have incorporated this 'reasonably required' drafting, however we have made this all other steps reasonably required by TfNSW (see our comments at item 20 below). | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(i)(C) | In implementing the ESS Variation Proposal, Downer is required to appoint a Scheme Auditor. Downer notes that the Scheme Auditor is only appointed once the relevant evidence is provided. | We agree with Downer's comment, and had not included a timeframe on this obligation for this reason.
In the revised draft of the Side Deed, we have clarified this obligation by including the words 'in accordance with the requirements of, and within the timeframe prescribed by, the Act, Regulation or Rule (as applicable)'. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii) | In implementing the ESS Variation Proposal, Downer is required to procure that NGE takes the steps set out in this clause. Downer has amended this obligation to procuring that NGE use reasonable endeavours to take those steps. | This amendment effectively converts NGE's obligations from strict obligations to only reasonable endeavours obligations, and therefore is not acceptable to Sydney Trains. We have not incorporated this amendment into the revised draft of the Side Deed. | DC - We will need to confirm with NGE that they can comply with the requirements of clause 5.1 as per the latest amendments. This is in progress. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii)(A) | On advice of its ESS Consultant that this step is not required, Downer has deleted the requirement that NGE applies to the Scheme Administrator as soon as reasonably practicable after the Effective Date to become accredited as a certificate provider in relation to the implementation of the HVAC Software Update as a Recognised Energy Saving Activity. | Based on the current list of ACPs published on IPART's website, NGE is already registered as an ACP in respect of a number of RESAs. The Act is clear that an ACP may only create ESCs in relation to those activities for which it has been accredited. It may be the case that NGE considers that its current accreditation covers the implementation of the HVAC Software Update, such that it does not need to apply to the Scheme Administrator to amend its accreditation to cover this activity.
In any event, we have amended this step to say that to the extent that NGE is not already accredited in relation to the implementation of the HVAC Software Update as a RESA, NGE must become accredited for this activity. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii)(B) | Downer must procure that NGE creates ESCs promptly after the energy savings occur and in any event within the timeframe prescribed by the Act, Regulation or Rule (as applicable). 
Downer has deleted 'promptly after the energy savings occur', as in its view, ESCs are not created promptly. Downer notes that the current program requires 12 months of data, plus modelling and auditor approval (approx. 12-18 months in total). | We have replaced 'promptly' with 'as soon as reasonably practicable', to account for the practical modelling and audit requirements.
For completeness, we note that the Act states that ESCs may be created by no later than 6 months after the end of the year in which the energy savings occur. | Okay. Closed 
DC - Okay |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii)(D) | Downer has requested clarification of the requirement that NGE applies to the Scheme Administrator for registration of the creation of the ESCs, noting that the act of 'registering' is what creates the ESCs. | Our reading of the Act is that the act of 'creating' an ESC is separate from, and different to, the registration of that ESC. Specifically, s46 of Sch 4A to the Act states that:
an ESC has no force or effect until its creation is registered by the Scheme Administrator in the register of ESCs; and
an application for registration of the creation of an ESC may be made to the Scheme Administrator by an ACP.
There are also a number of provisions in the Act that refer to 'creation' only, without 'registration'. Our drafting was intended to reflect this distinction.
In any event, based on Downer's view that the act of registering the ESCs is what creates them, both obligations (to create and register) should be satisfied by a single act.
We have not made any changes to the revised draft of the Side Deed in this respect. | DC - We will need to confirm with NGE that they can comply with the requirements of clause 5.1 as per the latest amendments. This is in progress. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii)(E) | Downer must procure that NGE sells the ESCs promptly after the registration of the ESCs, and maximises the price received for the ESCs. Downer has flagged this point as for discussion with TfNSW, noting that 'promptly selling' may not 'maximise the price' as it potentially floods the market. | We accept the deletion of the word 'promptly', however we have included a cut-off date of 6 months (or such other timeframe as may be agreed by TfNSW) by which the ESCs must be sold. We consider that this is a sufficient period of time to address the market flooding issue. | Commercial to comment. Is 6 months sufficient for us? Does our consultant agreement also give a cut-off to ACP, for selling off the certificates? This should be a back-to-back provision as we do not want a circumstance where ACP ‘run late’ on selling ‘because they can’ and we don’t meet our 6-month deadline on the transport side. 
DC – May be worth increasing this to 12 months (subject to CBS advice). We will need to confirm with NGE that they can comply with the requirements of clause 5.1 as per the latest amendments. This is in progress. |

|  | Cl 5.1(c) | Downer has requested clarification on the purpose of this clause, which relates to Downer providing information and reasonable access to the SGT Fleet to a representative of the ESS or a member of the ESS Audit Services Panel. | The template Nomination Form published on IPART's website provides that the original energy saver (TfNSW) agrees to provide information regarding the energy saving activities, and reasonable access to the implementation sites (SGT Fleet), to a representative of the ESS or a member of the ESS Audit Services Panel, upon their request. This clause was included in the Side Deed to pass this obligation through to Downer, to enable TfNSW to comply with its obligations in this respect.
Separately, the ACP will also have statutory obligations to provide information, assistance and access for monitoring, audit and reporting purposes. This obligation therefore also facilitates the ACP's compliance with its obligations. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 5.4(e) and (f) | Firstly, in relation to TfNSW's right to revoke NGE's nomination as the ACP at any time, Downer has proposed that:
the nomination should cease at the end of the requirements for record-keeping, on the basis that if the nomination is revoked or expires earlier, this will invalidate the ESCs that were created under that nomination; and
revocation should only be for cause and under exceptional, irredeemable circumstances.
Secondly, Downer has deleted the reference to NGE as the ACP 'owning' all energy savings arising from the implementation of the HVAC Software Update. | In relation to the first point, we accept that revocation should only be for cause and under exceptional, irredeemable circumstances. We also agree that the nomination should cease at the end of the Term of the Side Deed, which is intended to cover record-keeping requirements (see our comments at item 15 below in relation to the Term). We have incorporated this drafting into clauses 5.4(e) and (f). 
For completeness, we note that the legislation does not deal with the consequences of a revocation of an energy saver nomination, and does not provide that ESCs created under that nomination will be invalidated as a result of such revocation.
In relation to the second point, the template Nomination Form published on IPART's website states that the nomination entitles the ACP to create and own all energy savings in respect of the energy saving activities. The 'ownership' language was included for this reason, and we have therefore retained it. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 6.1(b) | Downer is proposing that it pay TfNSW the TfNSW Share of Proceeds within 30 BDs after the Payment Trigger Date (currently 2 BDs), noting that 2 BDs is practically unrealistic and that the payment terms should align with the SGT TLS Contract. | We have amended this in the revised draft of the Side Deed to be 10 Business Days. | DC – Downer’s position is 20 Business Days, in line with SGT payment terms. Especially given this is now linked to Termination, with only 5 business days grace. |

|  | Cl 7(a) | Downer has deleted the requirement that its monthly reports must be in a form acceptable to TfNSW, on the basis that this requirement is too generic and the minimum reporting requirements are well outlined in clause 7(a). 
Downer also notes that monthly reporting should be incorporated in the SGT TLSPPR. | We have incorporated this amendment into the revised draft of the Side Deed. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 13 | Downer has noted clause 13 as for discussion with TfNSW, noting that whilst the intent of the clause is clear (to say that Downer bears scope and cost risk with regards to the ESS application process), it unintentionally introduces ambiguity around Downer’s ongoing obligation to continue pursuing the application in circumstances where the scheme application is unsuccessful. This is a real risk given the novel application and requires consideration. | We understand Downer's concern, and have included an additional provision to the effect that if Downer has complied with its obligations under clause 13 to take all steps and do all things necessary to meet the requirements for the ESS, and after doing so, it is of the view that ESCs cannot be created, then it will notify TfNSW and the parties may, by mutual agreement, terminate the Side Deed. | Commercial to comment. I think we are okay, as long as we have priced in undertaking step in clause 13 (iv) – (vi). 
DC – Okay closed. |

|  | Cl 15 | Downer has included an initial term that expires on the earlier of: (a) 4 years from execution; and (b) 12 months following the last distribution of Net ESC Sale Proceeds, with an option to extend by mutual agreement (although the option period has been left blank). | We have proposed an alternative 'Term' clause in the revised draft of the Side Deed (see clause 3). We are proposing that the Deed continues for so long as ESCs can be created in respect of the implementation of the HVAC Software Update and all reporting, record-keeping and audit obligations have been completed, up to a limit of 10 years (unless otherwise agreed by the parties). The 10-year cap is intended to reflect reporting, record-keeping and audit requirements. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 16 | Downer has deleted TfNSW's right to terminate the Side Deed immediately by written notice if the SGT Project Delivery Deed or the SGT TLS Deed is terminated or expires. | Given the Amended Variation Order and ESS Variation Proposal are issued under the SGT TLS Deed, it is difficult to conceive how these (and the Side Deed) could stay on foot if the SGT TLS Deed is terminated. We have therefore not incorporated this amendment into the revised draft of the Side Deed. | Okay. Closed. |



|  | Clause Ref. | Topic | Description of Sydney Trains Amendment | Downer Response |

|  | Parties, definition of 'Related Entity', cl 5.4(b) and 18 | Counterparty to Side Deed | We have made TfNSW the counterparty to the Side Deed, rather than Sydney Trains, to align with legislative requirements. The counterparty to the Side Deed should be the entity that is the 'Purchaser' and 'Energy Saver' under the ESS Rule. This is the entity that purchases the services from Downer that enable the relevant energy savings to be made, which in our view is TfNSW, as the counterparty to the SGT TLS Deed with Downer under which the variation order for the implementation of the HVAC Software Update has been issued.
However, in the event that this changes, we have also included clause 5.4(b) to deal with a situation where the Purchaser or Energy Saver under the ESS Rule should instead be a Related Entity of TfNSW (Sydney Trains or Transport Asset Holding Entity of NSW (TAHE)), to ensure that the Side Deed is still given effect and the Related Entity is recognised as the Purchaser or Energy Saver, instead of TfNSW.
We have also made the following consequential changes, in light of the above:
deleted the previous Recital H, which is no longer relevant, as it applied to Sydney Trains as the counterparty; and
included a right for TfNSW to assign or novate the Side Deed. | DC – Okay, closed. |

|  | Cl 18 | Vesting of SGT Fleet in TAHE | The SGT Fleet, while currently owned by TfNSW, will be vested in TAHE at a future point in time. As such, we have included a right for TfNSW to assign or novate the Side Deed, to facilitate this future vesting. | DC – Okay, closed. |

|  | Definition of 'ACP Contract', 
cl 5.1(b)(i)(A) and 5.4(f)(ii) | ACP Contract | We consider that TfNSW will need to be a party to the contract between the ACP and Downer, to ensure that TfNSW receives the benefit of the ACP's appointment and to put in place arrangements for the ACP's nomination as energy saver by TfNSW, as well as for GST purposes to give effect to invoicing and remittance requirements. | We will need to confirm with NGE that they can comply with the requirements of clause 5.1 as per the latest amendments. This is in progress.
Regardless Downer cannot accept “on terms acceptable to TfNSW”. Downer has broad obligations under the proposed Side Deed and will ensure such obligations are discharged, including through our subcontractors / consultants where necessary. |

|  | Cl 4(b)(iii) and 4(d) | Timing for implementation of Amended Variation Order | As raised by Downer (see item 3 in the table above), on reflection we also thought that 'all other steps which are necessary to enable ESCs to be created' was too broad and may have led to uncertainty as to when this condition was satisfied. In the revised draft of the Side Deed, we have amended this to be 'all other steps reasonably required by TfNSW'.
To give the parties further certainty, we have also included a sunset date of 6 months within which the initial conditions set out in clauses 4(b)(i)-(iii) must be satisfied. If the initial conditions are not satisfied within this timeframe, TfNSW may terminate the Side Deed by notice to Downer and neither party will be liable to the other as a result of such termination. | DC – Okay, closed. |

|  | Cl 6.2(b) | No claims under SGT TLS Deed or SGT Project Delivery Deed | We have bolstered the language in this clause to reflect that the Side Deed is intended to be an entirely separate arrangement, distinct from both the SGT TLS Deed and the SGT Project Delivery Deed. Our amendments are intended to clarify that Downer will not be able to bring any claim or commence any dispute against TfNSW or its Related Entities under the SGT TLS Deed or SGT Project Delivery Deed, in respect of matters arising out of the Side Deed. | DC – Okay, but @Maria should this be reciprocal? |

|  | Cl 16(b) | Termination by TfNSW | We have included clause 16(b) to clarify that if TfNSW exercises any of its termination rights under clause 16(a), it will not be liable to Downer for any claim arising out of such termination. | DC – Okay, closed. |

|  | Definition of 'TfNSW's Representative', cl 19 | Notices and TfNSW's Representative | We have included a standard notices provision, which was missing from the previous draft of the Side Deed. TfNSW's Representative under the SGT TLS Deed should also be the recipient of notices to TfNSW under the Side Deed. | DC – Okay, closed. |

---


## Transport and Downer - ESS Side Deed Downer Comments - Sydney Trains Response(800744152.1) (1).docx

*File: `ESS Nomination Form/Transport and Downer - ESS Side Deed Downer Comments - Sydney Trains Response(800744152.1) (1).docx`*

ESS Side Deed Downer Comments (23 August 2022) – Sydney Trains Response

We set out in the table below our responses to Downer's comments on and requested amendments to the ESS Side Deed. We have not included the minor tidy up changes requested by Downer in the table below. These have been incorporated directly into the revised draft of the ESS Side Deed.

The clause references in the table below are to the clauses in the revised draft of the ESS Side Deed.

ESS Side Deed – Additional Amendments by Sydney Trains

In addition to the changes we have noted in the table above as being incorporated into the revised draft of the ESS Side Deed, we have also made the following amendments of note. We have not included minor tidy up changes (such as corrections to cross-references and definitions) in the table below. These have been incorporated directly into the revised draft of the ESS Side Deed.



|  | Clause Ref. | Downer Comment / Requested Amendment | Sydney Trains Response | Downer Response |

|  | Definitions of 'ACP's Fee' and 'Net ESC Sale Proceeds' | For simplicity:
the 'Supplier's Fee' should embed the ACP's Fee and all other associated costs; and
the 'Net ESC Sale Proceeds' should be calculated as the ESC Sale Proceeds minus the Supplier's Fee (rather than the ACP's Fee). | The proposed amendments suggest that the ACP will pass through to Downer the total amount of ESC Sale Proceeds (without deduction for the ACP's Fee), with Downer to then repay the ACP for its costs.
Our drafting was based on the assumption that, as the ACP would be receiving the ESC Sale Proceeds, it would keep 30% of those proceeds to cover its ACP's Fee, and then pay the balance to Downer, from which Downer would then keep the Supplier's Fee and pay the balance to TfNSW. We understand this is how the payments are intended to flow in practice, and the drafting should therefore reflect this. On this basis, we have not incorporated this amendment into the revised draft of the Side Deed. | DC - Okay, closed. |

|  | Definition of 'Supplier's Fee' | In response to TfNSW's query as to what is covered by the Supplier's Fee that is over and above what is covered by the ACP's Fee, Downer has advised that the Supplier's Fee broadly covers the activities outlined in clauses 5.1(b) (particularly (iii) to (viii)), 7 and 8.
Downer will provide an estimate breakdown in dollars, however the Supplier's Fee will be converted to a % of ESC Sale Proceeds, for consistency with this Deed (and the principle of TfNSW holding no risk as to costs). | [*]
[Note: TfNSW to confirm whether a % of ESC Sale Proceeds for the Supplier's Fee is acceptable.] | DC – Downer to provide further information by 7th September. |

|  | Cl 4(b)(iii) | Downer has requested that the conditions to be satisfied before the Amended Variation Order is implemented includes all other reasonably required steps which are necessary to enable the ESCs to be created. | We have incorporated this 'reasonably required' drafting, however we have made this all other steps reasonably required by TfNSW (see our comments at item 20 below). | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(i)(C) | In implementing the ESS Variation Proposal, Downer is required to appoint a Scheme Auditor. Downer notes that the Scheme Auditor is only appointed once the relevant evidence is provided. | We agree with Downer's comment, and had not included a timeframe on this obligation for this reason.
In the revised draft of the Side Deed, we have clarified this obligation by including the words 'in accordance with the requirements of, and within the timeframe prescribed by, the Act, Regulation or Rule (as applicable)'. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii) | In implementing the ESS Variation Proposal, Downer is required to procure that NGE takes the steps set out in this clause. Downer has amended this obligation to procuring that NGE use reasonable endeavours to take those steps. | This amendment effectively converts NGE's obligations from strict obligations to only reasonable endeavours obligations, and therefore is not acceptable to Sydney Trains. We have not incorporated this amendment into the revised draft of the Side Deed. | DC - We will need to confirm with NGE that they can comply with the requirements of clause 5.1 as per the latest amendments. This is in progress. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii)(A) | On advice of its ESS Consultant that this step is not required, Downer has deleted the requirement that NGE applies to the Scheme Administrator as soon as reasonably practicable after the Effective Date to become accredited as a certificate provider in relation to the implementation of the HVAC Software Update as a Recognised Energy Saving Activity. | Based on the current list of ACPs published on IPART's website, NGE is already registered as an ACP in respect of a number of RESAs. The Act is clear that an ACP may only create ESCs in relation to those activities for which it has been accredited. It may be the case that NGE considers that its current accreditation covers the implementation of the HVAC Software Update, such that it does not need to apply to the Scheme Administrator to amend its accreditation to cover this activity.
In any event, we have amended this step to say that to the extent that NGE is not already accredited in relation to the implementation of the HVAC Software Update as a RESA, NGE must become accredited for this activity. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii)(B) | Downer must procure that NGE creates ESCs promptly after the energy savings occur and in any event within the timeframe prescribed by the Act, Regulation or Rule (as applicable). 
Downer has deleted 'promptly after the energy savings occur', as in its view, ESCs are not created promptly. Downer notes that the current program requires 12 months of data, plus modelling and auditor approval (approx. 12-18 months in total). | We have replaced 'promptly' with 'as soon as reasonably practicable', to account for the practical modelling and audit requirements.
For completeness, we note that the Act states that ESCs may be created by no later than 6 months after the end of the year in which the energy savings occur. | Okay. Closed 
DC - Okay |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii)(D) | Downer has requested clarification of the requirement that NGE applies to the Scheme Administrator for registration of the creation of the ESCs, noting that the act of 'registering' is what creates the ESCs. | Our reading of the Act is that the act of 'creating' an ESC is separate from, and different to, the registration of that ESC. Specifically, s46 of Sch 4A to the Act states that:
an ESC has no force or effect until its creation is registered by the Scheme Administrator in the register of ESCs; and
an application for registration of the creation of an ESC may be made to the Scheme Administrator by an ACP.
There are also a number of provisions in the Act that refer to 'creation' only, without 'registration'. Our drafting was intended to reflect this distinction.
In any event, based on Downer's view that the act of registering the ESCs is what creates them, both obligations (to create and register) should be satisfied by a single act.
We have not made any changes to the revised draft of the Side Deed in this respect. | DC - We will need to confirm with NGE that they can comply with the requirements of clause 5.1 as per the latest amendments. This is in progress. |

|  | Cl 5.1(b)(ii)(E) | Downer must procure that NGE sells the ESCs promptly after the registration of the ESCs, and maximises the price received for the ESCs. Downer has flagged this point as for discussion with TfNSW, noting that 'promptly selling' may not 'maximise the price' as it potentially floods the market. | We accept the deletion of the word 'promptly', however we have included a cut-off date of 6 months (or such other timeframe as may be agreed by TfNSW) by which the ESCs must be sold. We consider that this is a sufficient period of time to address the market flooding issue. | Commercial to comment. Is 6 months sufficient for us? Does our consultant agreement also give a cut-off to ACP, for selling off the certificates? This should be a back-to-back provision as we do not want a circumstance where ACP ‘run late’ on selling ‘because they can’ and we don’t meet our 6-month deadline on the transport side. 
DC – May be worth increasing this to 12 months (subject to CBS advice). We will need to confirm with NGE that they can comply with the requirements of clause 5.1 as per the latest amendments. This is in progress. |

|  | Cl 5.1(c) | Downer has requested clarification on the purpose of this clause, which relates to Downer providing information and reasonable access to the SGT Fleet to a representative of the ESS or a member of the ESS Audit Services Panel. | The template Nomination Form published on IPART's website provides that the original energy saver (TfNSW) agrees to provide information regarding the energy saving activities, and reasonable access to the implementation sites (SGT Fleet), to a representative of the ESS or a member of the ESS Audit Services Panel, upon their request. This clause was included in the Side Deed to pass this obligation through to Downer, to enable TfNSW to comply with its obligations in this respect.
Separately, the ACP will also have statutory obligations to provide information, assistance and access for monitoring, audit and reporting purposes. This obligation therefore also facilitates the ACP's compliance with its obligations. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 5.4(e) and (f) | Firstly, in relation to TfNSW's right to revoke NGE's nomination as the ACP at any time, Downer has proposed that:
the nomination should cease at the end of the requirements for record-keeping, on the basis that if the nomination is revoked or expires earlier, this will invalidate the ESCs that were created under that nomination; and
revocation should only be for cause and under exceptional, irredeemable circumstances.
Secondly, Downer has deleted the reference to NGE as the ACP 'owning' all energy savings arising from the implementation of the HVAC Software Update. | In relation to the first point, we accept that revocation should only be for cause and under exceptional, irredeemable circumstances. We also agree that the nomination should cease at the end of the Term of the Side Deed, which is intended to cover record-keeping requirements (see our comments at item 15 below in relation to the Term). We have incorporated this drafting into clauses 5.4(e) and (f). 
For completeness, we note that the legislation does not deal with the consequences of a revocation of an energy saver nomination, and does not provide that ESCs created under that nomination will be invalidated as a result of such revocation.
In relation to the second point, the template Nomination Form published on IPART's website states that the nomination entitles the ACP to create and own all energy savings in respect of the energy saving activities. The 'ownership' language was included for this reason, and we have therefore retained it. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 6.1(b) | Downer is proposing that it pay TfNSW the TfNSW Share of Proceeds within 30 BDs after the Payment Trigger Date (currently 2 BDs), noting that 2 BDs is practically unrealistic and that the payment terms should align with the SGT TLS Contract. | We have amended this in the revised draft of the Side Deed to be 10 Business Days. | DC – Downer’s position is 20 Business Days, in line with SGT payment terms. Especially given this is now linked to Termination, with only 5 business days grace. |

|  | Cl 7(a) | Downer has deleted the requirement that its monthly reports must be in a form acceptable to TfNSW, on the basis that this requirement is too generic and the minimum reporting requirements are well outlined in clause 7(a). 
Downer also notes that monthly reporting should be incorporated in the SGT TLSPPR. | We have incorporated this amendment into the revised draft of the Side Deed. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 13 | Downer has noted clause 13 as for discussion with TfNSW, noting that whilst the intent of the clause is clear (to say that Downer bears scope and cost risk with regards to the ESS application process), it unintentionally introduces ambiguity around Downer’s ongoing obligation to continue pursuing the application in circumstances where the scheme application is unsuccessful. This is a real risk given the novel application and requires consideration. | We understand Downer's concern, and have included an additional provision to the effect that if Downer has complied with its obligations under clause 13 to take all steps and do all things necessary to meet the requirements for the ESS, and after doing so, it is of the view that ESCs cannot be created, then it will notify TfNSW and the parties may, by mutual agreement, terminate the Side Deed. | Commercial to comment. I think we are okay, as long as we have priced in undertaking step in clause 13 (iv) – (vi). 
DC – Okay closed. |

|  | Cl 15 | Downer has included an initial term that expires on the earlier of: (a) 4 years from execution; and (b) 12 months following the last distribution of Net ESC Sale Proceeds, with an option to extend by mutual agreement (although the option period has been left blank). | We have proposed an alternative 'Term' clause in the revised draft of the Side Deed (see clause 3). We are proposing that the Deed continues for so long as ESCs can be created in respect of the implementation of the HVAC Software Update and all reporting, record-keeping and audit obligations have been completed, up to a limit of 10 years (unless otherwise agreed by the parties). The 10-year cap is intended to reflect reporting, record-keeping and audit requirements. | Okay. Closed. |

|  | Cl 16 | Downer has deleted TfNSW's right to terminate the Side Deed immediately by written notice if the SGT Project Delivery Deed or the SGT TLS Deed is terminated or expires. | Given the Amended Variation Order and ESS Variation Proposal are issued under the SGT TLS Deed, it is difficult to conceive how these (and the Side Deed) could stay on foot if the SGT TLS Deed is terminated. We have therefore not incorporated this amendment into the revised draft of the Side Deed. | Okay. Closed. |



|  | Clause Ref. | Topic | Description of Sydney Trains Amendment | Downer Response |

|  | Parties, definition of 'Related Entity', cl 5.4(b) and 18 | Counterparty to Side Deed | We have made TfNSW the counterparty to the Side Deed, rather than Sydney Trains, to align with legislative requirements. The counterparty to the Side Deed should be the entity that is the 'Purchaser' and 'Energy Saver' under the ESS Rule. This is the entity that purchases the services from Downer that enable the relevant energy savings to be made, which in our view is TfNSW, as the counterparty to the SGT TLS Deed with Downer under which the variation order for the implementation of the HVAC Software Update has been issued.
However, in the event that this changes, we have also included clause 5.4(b) to deal with a situation where the Purchaser or Energy Saver under the ESS Rule should instead be a Related Entity of TfNSW (Sydney Trains or Transport Asset Holding Entity of NSW (TAHE)), to ensure that the Side Deed is still given effect and the Related Entity is recognised as the Purchaser or Energy Saver, instead of TfNSW.
We have also made the following consequential changes, in light of the above:
deleted the previous Recital H, which is no longer relevant, as it applied to Sydney Trains as the counterparty; and
included a right for TfNSW to assign or novate the Side Deed. | DC – Okay, closed. |

|  | Cl 18 | Vesting of SGT Fleet in TAHE | The SGT Fleet, while currently owned by TfNSW, will be vested in TAHE at a future point in time. As such, we have included a right for TfNSW to assign or novate the Side Deed, to facilitate this future vesting. | DC – Okay, closed. |

|  | Definition of 'ACP Contract', 
cl 5.1(b)(i)(A) and 5.4(f)(ii) | ACP Contract | We consider that TfNSW will need to be a party to the contract between the ACP and Downer, to ensure that TfNSW receives the benefit of the ACP's appointment and to put in place arrangements for the ACP's nomination as energy saver by TfNSW, as well as for GST purposes to give effect to invoicing and remittance requirements. | We will need to confirm with NGE that they can comply with the requirements of clause 5.1 as per the latest amendments. This is in progress.
Regardless Downer cannot accept “on terms acceptable to TfNSW”. Downer has broad obligations under the proposed Side Deed and will ensure such obligations are discharged, including through our subcontractors / consultants where necessary. |

|  | Cl 4(b)(iii) and 4(d) | Timing for implementation of Amended Variation Order | As raised by Downer (see item 3 in the table above), on reflection we also thought that 'all other steps which are necessary to enable ESCs to be created' was too broad and may have led to uncertainty as to when this condition was satisfied. In the revised draft of the Side Deed, we have amended this to be 'all other steps reasonably required by TfNSW'.
To give the parties further certainty, we have also included a sunset date of 6 months within which the initial conditions set out in clauses 4(b)(i)-(iii) must be satisfied. If the initial conditions are not satisfied within this timeframe, TfNSW may terminate the Side Deed by notice to Downer and neither party will be liable to the other as a result of such termination. | DC – Okay, closed. |

|  | Cl 6.2(b) | No claims under SGT TLS Deed or SGT Project Delivery Deed | We have bolstered the language in this clause to reflect that the Side Deed is intended to be an entirely separate arrangement, distinct from both the SGT TLS Deed and the SGT Project Delivery Deed. Our amendments are intended to clarify that Downer will not be able to bring any claim or commence any dispute against TfNSW or its Related Entities under the SGT TLS Deed or SGT Project Delivery Deed, in respect of matters arising out of the Side Deed. | DC – Okay, but @Maria should this be reciprocal? |

|  | Cl 16(b) | Termination by TfNSW | We have included clause 16(b) to clarify that if TfNSW exercises any of its termination rights under clause 16(a), it will not be liable to Downer for any claim arising out of such termination. | DC – Okay, closed. |

|  | Definition of 'TfNSW's Representative', cl 19 | Notices and TfNSW's Representative | We have included a standard notices provision, which was missing from the previous draft of the Side Deed. TfNSW's Representative under the SGT TLS Deed should also be the recipient of notices to TfNSW under the Side Deed. | DC – Okay, closed. |
