                       Manual for some methods.




iv   IPART Record Keeping Guide
Contents




1   Introduction                                                                1

2   Legal requirements for record keeping under the Energy Savings
    Scheme                                                                      2

3   Record keeping requirements under the Energy Savings Scheme                 3
    3.1 Records to be kept                                                      3
4   Record keeping arrangements                                                 4
    4.1 Document register                                                       4
    4.2 Processes and/or procedures for keeping records                         5
    4.3 Quality assurance – controls to ensure records are accurate             6

5   Continuing arrangements for record keeping                                  6
    A    Australian Standard on Records Management                              7




                                                        Record Keeping Guide IPART   v
1        Introduction
The NSW Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) seeks to reduce energy consumption in
NSW by creating financial incentives for organisations to invest in energy saving
projects.

The other objects of the ESS are to:
   assist households and businesses to reduce energy costs
   make the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions achievable at a lower cost,
    and
   reduce the cost of, and need for, additional energy generation, transmission
    and distribution infrastructure. 1

Electricity retailers and other mandatory participants (Scheme Participants) are
obliged to meet energy saving targets. Energy savings are achieved by installing,
improving or replacing energy saving equipment. Companies that become
Accredited Certificate Providers (ACPs) can create energy savings certificates
(ESCs) from these activities and then sell those ESCs to Scheme Participants. The
Independent Pricing and Regulatory Tribunal of NSW (IPART) is both the
Scheme Administrator and Scheme Regulator of the ESS. 2

Collecting and retaining the required records to support the methodology, data
and assumptions used to calculate energy savings is an accreditation condition
for ACPs and is critical for the proper creation of ESCs. In order to comply with
this condition of accreditation, ACPs must have complete records before the
creation of ESCs. ESCs created without the required supporting records may be
found to be improperly created.

Section 3 of the Record Keeping Guide (Guide) identifies the records that ACPs
are required to keep. Section 4 of this Guide sets out the Scheme Administrator
requirements for appropriate record keeping arrangements. Sections 3 and 4 of
this Guide are incorporated as requirements in an ACP’s accreditation notice.

This Guide is also a useful reference for applicants who are preparing their
application for accreditation. Applicants should make sure that all the
requirements mentioned in this Guide are adequately addressed in any
application for accreditation.

This Guide should be used in conjunction with the ESS Application Guide 3, the
Method Guides 4, Evidence Manuals 5 and Evidence Packs 6 relevant to your
accreditation or your application.

1   Electricity Supply Act 1995, section 98(2)
2   Electricity Supply Act 1995, sections 153(2) and 151(2) respectively
3   See www.ess.nsw.gov.au/How_to_apply_for_accreditation
4   See www.ess.nsw.gov.au/Methods_for_calculating_energy_savings
5   See www.ess.nsw.gov.au/Methods_for_calculating_energy_savings



                                                                     Record Keeping Guide IPART   1
    The Scheme Administrator may update this Guide from time to time.
    Notification of updates to this Guide will be published at www.ess.nsw.gov.au.



    2       Legal requirements for record keeping under the
            Energy Savings Scheme
    One of the eligibility requirements for accreditation as an ACP in respect of your
    activity is that your record keeping arrangements have been approved by the
    Scheme Administrator, or that the Scheme Administrator is satisfied that you will
    have appropriate record keeping arrangements in place at the time of
    implementation.

    Once you are accredited and carrying out your activity, you are required to keep
    specific records in order to support your ESC creation. These records are specific
    to the activity for which you are accredited and are identified in section 3 of this
    Guide.

    It is a condition of accreditation that you collect and retain these records and to
    ensure these records are complete prior to the creation of ESCs. Compliance with
    this requirement will be assessed through audit. A failure to keep the required
    records may be a breach of your accreditation conditions, which could result in a
    range of penalties including suspension or cancellation of your accreditation.

    Table 1 provides a summary of the key record keeping requirements in the
    Electricity Supply (General) Regulation 2014 (the Regulation). It is a summary only
    and does not constitute legal advice.

    Table 1        Record keeping requirement for the ESS
    Requirement                                                             Regulation clause
    The person is eligible for accreditation if the person has record keeping Clause 38(1)
    arrangements with respect to the activity approved by the Scheme
    Administrator or (for a proposed activity) the Scheme Administrator is
    satisfied that the person will have appropriate record keeping
    arrangements in respect of that activity when the activity is carried out.
    The ACP must keep a record of:                                          Clause 46(1)
     the location in which the activity occurred,
     the energy savings (calculated in accordance with the scheme
      rules) arising from that activity, and
     the methodology, data and assumptions used to calculate those
      energy savings.
    An ACP must keep any records that the Scheme Administrator has, by Clause 46(2)
    notice in writing to the ACP, required the ACP to keep.




    6   See www.ess.nsw.gov.au/Methods_for_calculating_energy_savings



2   IPART Record Keeping Guide
Requirement                                                               Regulation clause
A record required to be kept by a person must be retained by the          Clause 46(3)
person for at least 6 years after it is made.
Records are to be kept in a form and manner approved by the Scheme Clause 46(4)
Administrator.




3       Record keeping requirements under the Energy
        Savings Scheme
This section sets out the Scheme Administrator’s standard requirements
regarding records that must be kept by ACPs. You will need to maintain record
keeping arrangements that meet the Scheme Administrator’s requirements as
long as you are accredited under the Scheme and will need to retain all records
for a period of 6 years after ESC creation, whether or not you are accredited.


3.1     Records to be kept

You are required to collect and retain the evidence identified in the guidance
document relevant to the calculation methodology for which you are accredited
as listed in Table 2 below. The guidance documents are published on the ESS
website at www.ess.nsw.gov.au. We will update these documents and develop
new guidance from time to time.

Table 2        Evidence required to be kept
Method                                           Guidance document
Deemed Energy Savings Method – Sale of           Method Guide - Sale of New Appliances
New Appliances
Deemed Energy Savings Method –                   Evidence Manual – Commercial Lighting
Commercial Lighting Energy Savings               Energy Savings Formula
Formula
Deemed Energy Savings Method – High              Method Guide – High Efficiency Motors
Efficiency Motors Energy Savings Formula         Energy Savings Formula
Deemed Energy Savings Method – Power             Method Guide – Power Factor Correction
Factor Correction Formula                        Formula
Deemed Energy Savings Method – Removal           Method Guide – Removal of Old Appliances
of Old Appliances
Deemed Energy Savings Method –                   Method Guide – Installation of High Efficiency
Installation of High Efficiency Appliances for   Appliances for Business
Business
Deemed Energy Savings Method – Home              Method Guide – Home Energy Efficiency
Energy Efficiency Retrofits                      Retrofits
Metered Baseline Method - NABERS                 Method Guide - NABERS baseline
baseline




                                                                  Record Keeping Guide IPART      3
    Method                                       Guidance document
    Metered Baseline Method – Method 1,          Method Guide – Metered Baseline Method
    Method 2 and Method 3

    It is an ACP’s responsibility to ensure they comply with the most recent version
    of the relevant guidance document at the time of implementing an activity and
    creating ESCs. Auditors will audit compliance using the version of the guidance
    document that was current at the time of your ESC creation.

    There are several methods for which records will be proposed and assessed at the
    time of application for accreditation, rather than specified in detail in the
    guidance documents. The proposed records will be approved as part of your
    accreditation. These methods include:
       Aggregated Metered Baseline
       Metered Baseline Method (Methods 1, 2 and 3), and
       Project Impact Assessment with Measurement and Verification.




    4       Record keeping arrangements
    ACPs must maintain appropriate record keeping arrangements to ensure that
    they are able to collect the evidence required. You must ensure that you have
    record keeping arrangements that will:
       facilitate the collection of the required records
       allow accurate records to be provided to the Scheme Administrator or to an
        auditor at any time, and
       retain the records for at least 6 years after ESC creation.


    4.1     Document register

    You must have a document register that lists the records that must be collected
    and retained to support your ESC creation. For most methods, this list has been
    provided in the method guide relevant to your accreditation (see Table 2).

    Where a list of evidence has not been developed, your document register must
    identify the evidence that will be collected and retained to support ESC creation.
    The evidence required will depend on the calculation methodology for which
    you are accredited but in general, you should include:
       nomination form, where you are to be nominated as the energy saver




4   IPART Record Keeping Guide
   details of the location of the Recognised Energy Saving Activity 7 (RESA)
   evidence to show that the RESA has been, or will be, implemented eg, sales
    invoices
   evidence to support inputs to the calculation of energy savings
   records of the calculation of energy savings arising from the RESA
   records of the calculation of the number of ESCs to be created
   records that demonstrate that the RESA remains implemented and
    functioning for the life of the proposed energy savings, and
   any other records identified as part of your application for accreditation.


4.2      Processes and/or procedures for keeping records

You need to explain the step-by-step details of how records will be obtained,
processed, maintained and controlled as well as identify the position(s) of the
person(s) responsible for each of these steps in a process/procedure document.

Applicants with multiple business units involved in the RESA may have separate
(but interlinked) record keeping processes for each business unit.

Record keeping processes and procedures must include:
   a description of where data was sourced, how the data is identified and
    recorded (within the relevant information systems)
   how each record in the document register is created, approved and updated
   details of any information systems, databases, and/or spreadsheets used to
    collate, manage or store records listed in the document register
   how each record in the document register is identified (via the record
    identification protocol/naming protocols) and where each record is located
   the position of the person responsible for each type of record in the document
    register
   processes for archiving and retrieving documents, and
   a description of how records will be retained for a minimum of six years.

In support of the above requirements, you need to have a process map at the time
of application that identifies and illustrates the key steps and platforms for the
flow of a record from its initiation to its eventual archival.




7   A Recognised Energy Saving Activity (RESA) is an activity that reduces electricity consumption in
    respect of which ESCs may be created.



                                                                     Record Keeping Guide IPART         5
    4.3     Quality assurance – controls to ensure records are accurate

    You must demonstrate that you have adequate mechanisms in place to ensure
    that the records are complete, accurate and reliable. You must also show the
    processes and responsibilities for checking records, especially calculations and
    steps in the creation and registration of ESCs.

    To do this, you should provide a quality assurance document describing:
       the documented control procedures in place for each record
       details of any internal audit and reconciliation procedures you have
        developed to support your record keeping
       the procedure for checking energy savings calculations prior to creation and
        registration of ESCs, and
       the position of the person who is responsible for performing these actions.




    5       Continuing arrangements for record keeping
    Once you have been accredited for an activity, you will be issued with an
    accreditation notice listing conditions that have been imposed by the Scheme
    Administrator.      Some of those accreditation conditions may relate to
    requirements for record keeping. You must comply with all accreditation
    conditions. You should read your accreditation notice and contact the ESS team
    if you do not fully understand the requirements.

    You are also responsible for ensuring that your record keeping arrangements
    continue to meet any additional Scheme Administrator requirements, as
    described in Sections 3 and 4 of this Guide. These requirements may be updated
    from time to time and you will be asked to update your record keeping
    arrangements accordingly.

    Your record keeping arrangements will be tested at audit, as prescribed in your
    accreditation notice, or as required by the Scheme Administrator. The Scheme
    Administrator may also require you to provide copies of your record keeping
    documentation for review at any time.




6   IPART Record Keeping Guide
A      Australian Standard on Records Management
It is strongly suggested that record keeping arrangements be consistent with the
Australian Standard on Records Management AS/ISO 15489:2002. This provides
guidance on developing auditable record keeping procedures. You should apply
this Standard when developing and maintaining your record keeping
arrangements. It is published by Standards Australia International Ltd
(Standards Australia) and copies can be purchased from the Standards Australia
website - www.standards.com.au.

You may also find it useful to refer to the:
   Australian Standard on Environmental Management Systems AS/NZS ISO
    14001:2004 for specific guidance on environmental records; and
   Australian Standard on Quality Management Systems AS/NZS ISO 9001:2008
    for guidance on developing auditable record keeping procedures.

If your organisation is accredited under any of these standards, please provide
evidence in your application for accreditation.




                                                       Record Keeping Guide IPART   7

## Notice-012013-Requirements-for-all-methods-Minimum-requirements-of-conduct-V3.0 (1).pdf

*File: `ACP_PART A (Application)/Notice-012013-Requirements-for-all-methods-Minimum-requirements-of-conduct-V3.0 (1).pdf`*

ESS Notice 01/2013


V3.0 effective 15 March 2019


Minimum requirements of conduct 1
IPART, as Scheme Administrator of the NSW Energy Savings Scheme (ESS), has established
the Minimum Requirements of Conduct (Minimum Requirements) which Accredited
Certificate Providers (ACPs) must meet.
The Minimum Requirements have been established to help ensure that ACPs and their
representatives act in a way that protects the interests of customers and maintains the integrity
of the ESS. Representatives who engage with customers are effectively the public face of the
ESS.
The Minimum Requirements relate to:
    Establishing and maintaining contractual relationships
    The provision of training
    Establishing and maintaining a register of representatives, and
    Customer engagement and management.

Who must comply with the Minimum Requirements?
All ACPs are required to meet the Minimum Requirements. This is set out in an ACP’s
accreditation notice.
The Minimum Requirements apply to ACPs, in respect of their own conduct and the conduct
of any person participating in any aspect of a Recognised Energy Savings Activity (RESA) on
behalf of an ACP. We call these persons ‘representatives’ (as that term is defined in Table 1),
and the Minimum Requirements apply to all representatives.
The Minimum Requirements apply from the initial engagement with customers 2 through to the
final quality assurance of documents.
IPART assesses ACP compliance with the Minimum Requirements when you apply for
accreditation as an ACP, and during implementation of your RESA.




1 Previously titled Minimum requirements for conduct of persons acting on behalf of Accredited Certificate Providers
2 For the purposes of the ESS, a customer refers to the original energy saver or potential original energy saver
who may nominate the ACP as the nominated energy saver for the purposes of creating ESCs. The only exception
is the removal of old appliances method, where the customer is the small business owner or householder from
where the appliance is removed.




Independent Pricing and Regulatory Tribunal                                                                       1
                                                                            ESS Notice 01/2013 – V3.0




ACP responsibilities
ACPs are responsible for all activities undertaken as part of their RESA. This includes, but is
not limited to:
      Contacting or approaching customers in relation to an ESS activity
      Conducting the initial design or assessment for a project
      Providing quotes to a customer
      Modifying, replacing, installing or removing End-User Equipment (EUE)
      Obtaining a nomination,
      Obtaining other documentation necessary to support ESC creation, and
      Providing appropriate after sales customer service for EUE installed as part of an activity.
ACPs are responsible and accountable for ensuring the Minimum Requirements are
implemented in an appropriate manner, and must maintain evidence to demonstrate ongoing
compliance.



1.1 Contractual Arrangements
ACPs must exercise effective control over all activities conducted by their representatives.
The Scheme Administrator requires ACPs to have contractual arrangements with their
representatives in place to ensure the RESA complies with ESS requirements.
The contractual arrangements must be in place before the representative conducts any activity
on the ACP’s behalf. Contractual arrangements form a record and must be retained and be
available for review during an audit or at any time requested by IPART.
ACPs are accountable for compliance with ESS requirements, and cannot avoid this
accountability through a contract with their representative. ACPs are accountable for any
activity conducted by a representative on their behalf, deliberate or otherwise.
The Scheme Administrator will hold ACPs responsible for all actions, omissions and
information provided by representatives acting on their behalf, regardless of any contract or
agreement between the representative and other parties. Minimum Requirements for
contractual arrangements are set out in Table 1 below.

Table 1: Minimum Requirements for contractual arrangements with representatives
                    Requirement
    Representatives Must be:
                      An employee of the ACP or direct contractor of the ACP, or
                      An employee or contractor of another company or business that has a legally
                       binding contract with either the ACP or a direct contractor of the ACP.
    Contractual     Must ensure that all representatives:
    arrangements      Conduct activities in accordance with all ESS requirements
                      Be appropriately trained prior to performing any activities under the ESS (see
                       section 1.2)




Independent Pricing and Regulatory Tribunal                                                             2
                                                                                                  ESS Notice 01/2013



                         Requirement
                              Abide by the minimum requirements for customer engagement (see section 1.4)
                              Cooperate with any reasonable request from the Scheme Administrator, auditor or
                               ACP to provide information in relation to any implementation for which ESCs have
                               been or will be created, and
                              Are 18 years or older.a
a Unless the representative is an apprentice working under the supervision of a licensed tradesperson as per the relevant
legislation.



1.2 Training for Representatives
ACPs are responsible for ensuring that appropriate training is provided to all representatives,
so that they can conduct themselves in a professional and competent manner and comply with
ESS requirements. ACPs need to tailor the training to their specific activity, needs and
processes. Training for representatives must include all the items outlined in Table 2 below.

Table 2: Minimum Requirements for training representatives
  Item         Applicability         Content                                                               Timing
  1            All representatives      An overview of the ESS and its legislative framework,             Before a
               (all RESAs)               including the function and importance of the nomination           representative
                                         form                                                              is involved in
                                        An overview of the activities that are being undertaken           the RESA for
                                         under the RESA                                                    the ACP
                                        Any of the ACP’s internal procedures relevant to the
                                         representative’s role in the RESA
                                        Any co-payment requirement relevant to the method
                                        Procedures that need to be followed to ensure that
                                         each implementation under the RESA complies with the
                                         Electricity Supply Act 1995 (NSW), the Electricity
                                         Supply (General) Regulation 2014 (NSW) and the ESS
                                         Rule of 2009 (NSW)
                                        Any training necessary to undertake an implementation
                                        The records that the representative needs to collect for
                                         each implementation to be eligible to create ESCs, and
                                        Relevant legislative or regulatory requirements with
                                         which the representative must comply in performing its
                                         role, including provisions for telemarketing and door-to-
                                         door sales under the Australian Consumer Law,
                                         consumer rights, occupational work health & safety, the
                                         Gas and Electrical (Consumer Safety) Act 2017 (NSW)
                                         and other statutory requirements.
  2            Where the ACP’s       ACPs must have a procedure in place which ensures the                 Before the
               customer is using     customer’s staff/contractors are trained to undertake the             customer’s
               its own               activity in accordance with ESS requirements.                         staff/contractors
               staff/contractors                                                                           undertake any
               for an                                                                                      activities under
               implementation of                                                                           the ESS
               the RESA




Independent Pricing and Regulatory Tribunal                                                                                 3
                                                                                    ESS Notice 01/2013



1.3 Registers
ACPs must maintain a register of all representatives conducting activities for each RESA.

Table 3: Minimum Requirements for registers of representatives
 Minimum fields in register
     Name
     Contact details
     Training completed in relation to the ESS and the RESA
     Other training completed relevant to activity (eg. WHS)
     Formal qualifications, licenses and certifications (eg. electrician, plumber)
     Date of completion of ESS training
     Date of completion of any other training/qualifications
     Licence numbers (where applicable, eg. specialist trade work, refrigerant handling)
     Insurance details
     Relationship to the ACP (eg. employee, contractor, third party)
     Date of employment or commencement of contract/agreement, and
     Details of any responsibility for any process sign-off in delivering the RESA.


1.4 Customer engagement
ACPs must have documented customer engagement processes in place to address all the
items outlined in Table 4 below.

Table 4: Minimum Requirements for customer engagement

## CBS DST Training Form DRAFT Rev2.docx

*File: `Downer Alt Funding Proposals/CBS DST Training Form DRAFT Rev2.docx`*

Representative Acknowledgment Form

NSW Energy Saving Scheme (ESS) - Project Impact Assessment with Measurement and Veriﬁcation (PIAM&V) method for CBS, Downer, Sydney Train representatives 

I acknowledge I have the following responsibilities:

PIAM&V Method 

The Project Impact Assessment with Measurement and Veriﬁcation (PIAM&V) method is based on internationally recognised measurement and veriﬁcation principals to calculate energy savings. It allows “equipment level” energy savings to be deemed for up to ten years, using persistence and conﬁdence factors to discount initial certiﬁcate creation. In this method, the term ‘energy’ may refer to electricity, gas, or both.

The PIAM&V method uses regression analysis to model the energy consumption of a system against an independent variable or variables before and after the project is implemented, and then applies this to a “normal year” of operation to calculate a statistically valid difference in energy consumption.

Proof of Accurate and Correct Data 

Northmore Gordon strongly recommend that a procedure for data collection and handling should be developed during the establishment of the project.

The Energy Saver is responsible for providing correct and accurate data to Northmore Gordon.  The proof required for different types of data are detailed below:

Failure to provide proof with the data risks forfeit of some or all of the certificates. 

Geo-Tagged Photos

Instructions on taking and downloading geo-tagged photos from a smart phone can be found at www.northmoregordon.com/articles/take-geo-tagged-photos-for-evidence



| My company has a contractual agreement with Northmore Gordon Environmental Pty Ltd (NGE) (either directly or via the contract with CBS Group Australia and/or Downer Group and/or Sydney Trains), where claims are made, to perform the activity compliant with the NSW Energy Saving Scheme (NSW ESS). | My company has a contractual agreement with Northmore Gordon Environmental Pty Ltd (NGE) (either directly or via the contract with CBS Group Australia and/or Downer Group and/or Sydney Trains), where claims are made, to perform the activity compliant with the NSW Energy Saving Scheme (NSW ESS). |

| I understand that the NSW ESS places an obligation on the Energy Retailers (eg AGL, Origin, etc) to acquired Energy Saving Certificates (ESCs) and that provides the funding for ESCs.  It is not a government paid rebate. | I understand that the NSW ESS places an obligation on the Energy Retailers (eg AGL, Origin, etc) to acquired Energy Saving Certificates (ESCs) and that provides the funding for ESCs.  It is not a government paid rebate. |

| I understand the ESS is administered by the Independent Pricing and Regulatory Tribunal (IPART). | I understand the ESS is administered by the Independent Pricing and Regulatory Tribunal (IPART). |

| I understand that an ESC represents a MWh of energy saved (and approx. 1 ton of CO2 not emitted). | I understand that an ESC represents a MWh of energy saved (and approx. 1 ton of CO2 not emitted). |

| I understand that Sydney Trains is paying for the equipment upgrade, has the right to nominate the energy savings and is known under the ESS as the Energy Saver. | I understand that Sydney Trains is paying for the equipment upgrade, has the right to nominate the energy savings and is known under the ESS as the Energy Saver. |

| I understand that by implementing the Recognised Energy Saving Activity (RESA) (software and/or hardware upgrades on Sydney Trains trains), the RESA saves energy from which the number of ESCs is calculated using the PIAMV Method (see below PIAM&V Method). | I understand that by implementing the Recognised Energy Saving Activity (RESA) (software and/or hardware upgrades on Sydney Trains trains), the RESA saves energy from which the number of ESCs is calculated using the PIAMV Method (see below PIAM&V Method). |

| I understand that this form is a required by Northmore Gordon Environmental Pty Ltd under the NSW Energy Saving Scheme | I understand that this form is a required by Northmore Gordon Environmental Pty Ltd under the NSW Energy Saving Scheme |

| I understand the Accredited Certificate Provider (ACP) under the NSW ESS is Northmore Gordon Environmental Pty Ltd | I understand the Accredited Certificate Provider (ACP) under the NSW ESS is Northmore Gordon Environmental Pty Ltd |

| I understand that we cannot keep the equipment that has been replaced in the implementation.  It must be permanently decommissioned and removed from site. | I understand that we cannot keep the equipment that has been replaced in the implementation.  It must be permanently decommissioned and removed from site. |

| I understand that the evidence provided to Northmore Gordon Environmental Pty Ltd will be used to validate the ESS claim and I have an obligation to ensure the evidence provide is a truth and accurate reflection of the works undertaken and inaccurate evidence can result in the failure of the claim or forfeiture later. | I understand that the evidence provided to Northmore Gordon Environmental Pty Ltd will be used to validate the ESS claim and I have an obligation to ensure the evidence provide is a truth and accurate reflection of the works undertaken and inaccurate evidence can result in the failure of the claim or forfeiture later. |

| I understand that IPART or ESS auditors may request additional information to verify ESCs are properly calculated. | I understand that IPART or ESS auditors may request additional information to verify ESCs are properly calculated. |

| I confirm that I and any subcontractor hired, hold the appropriate qualifications to perform the installation work the parties are covered by public liability insurance of at least $5 million, and product liability of $5 million. | I confirm that I and any subcontractor hired, hold the appropriate qualifications to perform the installation work the parties are covered by public liability insurance of at least $5 million, and product liability of $5 million. |

| If applicable. | Not applicable |

|  | I, a representative of Sydney Trains, must supply correct data to NGE with proof, the nature of proof required differs for each type of data (see below Proof of Accurate and Correct Data). The truth and accuracy of the data and documentation will be checked during the audit of the Recognised Energy Saving Activity | I, a representative of Sydney Trains, must supply correct data to NGE with proof, the nature of proof required differs for each type of data (see below Proof of Accurate and Correct Data). The truth and accuracy of the data and documentation will be checked during the audit of the Recognised Energy Saving Activity |

Instructions provided in Geo-Tagged Photos. | I, a representative of INSERT COMPANY NAME THAT IS IMPLEMENTING THE RESAs, must provide time-stamped and geo-tagged photographs of the equipment or upgrade before and after the implementation, with clear annotations to indicate the train number and car ID. | Data Type | Examples of Proof |

| Utility data (incl. interval data and invoices) | Original data files from Energy Retailer or Distributor.
Original invoice(s) from Energy Retailer. |

| Manual meter reads | A photo of the relevant meter/ measurement indicator for each reading (a geo-tagged is strongly preferred but not required)
or
A photo of the log sheet that the number is written upon that is signed by the person taking the reading and countersigned by a colleague. |

| Raw data extracted from SCADA or PLC or similar | Original file(s) as extracted from the system with a screen shot of data extraction code |

| Other data that has been manipulated from raw data | Raw data from which the data was manipulated from.
In some (very select) cases, it may be acceptable to provide a written note (e.g. email) from the data provider that the data is correct, accurate, and complete, and an additional written note (e.g. email) from a colleague also stating that the same data is correct, accurate and complete. |

## CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support Rev A.docx

*File: `Downer Alt Funding Proposals/CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support Rev A.docx`*

6 December 2019

Mr David Rees

T3, Triniti Business Campus, 

39 Delhi Road
North Ryde NSW 2113 

Dear Dave,

Re: Alternate Funding Mechanisms | Proposal for CBS Group Support

As we discussed last week, the following is a proposal for CBS Group to work with you to further qualify the potential for a range of alternate funding mechanisms to be used to support current operations and initiatives – initially focused upon the TfNSW Fleet Enhancements.

Context

Situation

Downer has a range of innovations and improvements that they propose to their clients for mutual benefit.   A recent example of this is the development of the suite of enhancements for the TfNSW Rolling Stock fleet that included improvements leading to reduced maintenance, improved passenger experience & safety, and reductions in energy usage.

Complication

As is often the case, these innovation and improvement initiatives struggle to progress from concept to project.   Often this is due to challenges securing the up-front capital to fund the project, typically because;

the benefits are accrued by others – other departments, customers, the public, etc

the benefits in operation expense cannot be accrued to create the required capital

Question

Is there a mechanism available where we can secure the upfront capital and support a greater proportion of Downer’s innovations to progress from concept to reality.

Answer

The focus upon CO2 emissions reductions has lead to the establishment of a number of funding mechanisms designed to provide capex support for energy reduction initiatives.   It is proposed that Downer enlist the support of CBS Group to investigate the feasibility of these funding sources – making use of the Fleet Enhancement Program as the focus for the initial works, providing the basis for subsequent application of these mechanisms in other contexts.

Approach

We propose a 3 stage approach to the engagement as shown in the table below.

Nominated Persons and Terms of Engagement

CBS proposes that Jeff Dusting lead this work.   Jeff has prior experience with Fleet Enhancement Program and team, the applicable schemes, and considerable commercial acumen.  As this is a short-term, part-time engagement, a daily rate of $2200 plus GST applies, billable on a pro-rata basis using an 8 hour day. For ease of budgeting, we propose a capped fee for Stages 1 and 2 of $33,000 plus GST – based upon 15 days work effort.

All travel to/from Sydney wil be billed at cost, with a daily per diem in accordance with the applicable ATO schedule payable to cover accommodation, meals and incidental expenses.

The current CBS Group Services Agreement terms of engagement, including current 30 day payment terms will continue to apply to this proposal.

We look forward to the opportunity to progress this exciting initiative, and would welcome an opportunity to meet and clarify anything outlined in this proposal.

Sincerely,

[Original Signed]

Jeff Dusting 

CBS Group Pty Ltd

Accepted on behalf of Downer

____________________

[Name]

____________________

Date



| Stage | Scope | Days Work Effort | Period | Location |

| 1 | Work with the Fleet Enhancement Project team to identify and validate the energy savings associated with selected enhancements.
Complete analysis of the likelihood of successful funding against via the Federal Emissions Reduction Fund, Clean Energy Innovation Fund, NSW Energy Saving Scheme and via an Energy Performance Contract.
Initial registrations to ensure that scheme deadlines are met.
Present the outcomes and recommended next steps to Downer Leadership – as they relate to the Fleet Enhancement Program. | 10 | Jan 2020 | 5 Days in North Ryde Office |

| 2 | Develop presentation materials and support initial discussions with the State to determine the level of support for the approach | 5 | Feb 2020 | 3 Days in Sydney / North Ryde |

| 3 | Develop the applicable scheme documentation
Provide a paper / presentation that outlines the strategic opportunity for Downer to apply these funding mechanisms elsewhere in the business. | [TBC] | Feb-Mar 2020 | TBC |

|  |  |  |  |  |

|  | Total | 15d | Jan-March 2020 | Jan-March 2020 |

---

## CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support Rev C.docx

*File: `Downer Alt Funding Proposals/CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support Rev C.docx`*

25 February 2020

Mr David Rees

T3, Triniti Business Campus, 

39 Delhi Road
North Ryde NSW 2113 

Dear Dave,

Re: Alternate Funding Mechanisms | Proposal for CBS Group Support

As we discussed last week, enclosed is an updated proposal for CBS Group to work with you to further qualify the potential for a range of alternate funding mechanisms to be used to support current operations and initiatives – initially focused upon the TfNSW Fleet Enhancement (HVAC Upgrade).  We have completed a review of the FE04 HVAC Energy Efficiency Report, and updated the proposal accordingly.

Context

Situation

Downer has a number of innovations and improvements they want to  propose to their clients for mutual benefit.   One recent example is the development of a suite of enhancements for the TfNSW Rolling Stock fleet that included improvements with measurable benefits in reduced maintenance, improved passenger experience & safety, and energy usage.  The HVAC Energy Efficiency initiative is a well developed initiative with a great deal of pre-existing modelling already completed.

Complication

As is often the case, these innovation and improvement initiatives struggle to progress from concept to project.   Often this is due to challenges securing the up-front capital to fund the project, typically because;

the benefits are accrued by others – other departments, customers, the public, etc.

the benefits in operation expense cannot be accrued to create the required capital

Question

The focus upon CO2 emissions reductions has led to the establishment of a number of funding mechanisms designed to provide capex support for energy reduction initiatives.  Are we able to leverage these funding mechanisms to secure the upfront capital needed to mobilise the Sydney Fleet, HVAC Energy Efficiency inoitiative.   And if so, are we able to extend the learnings to determine what the boundaries are for other similar Downer initiatives.

Answer

We propose that Downer engage CBS Group to investigate the feasibility of these funding sources – using of the Fleet Enhancement HVAC Energy Efficiency Initiative (FE04) as the focus for the initial works, providing the basis for subsequent application of these mechanisms in other contexts.

Approach

We propose a 3 stage approach to the engagement as shown in the table below.

Nominated Persons and Terms of Engagement

CBS proposes that Jeff Dusting lead this work.   Jeff has prior experience with Fleet Enhancement Program and team, the applicable schemes, and considerable commercial acumen.  As this is a short-term, part-time engagement, a daily rate of $2200 plus GST applies, billable on a pro-rata basis using an 8 hour day. For ease of budgeting, we propose a capped fee for Stages 1 - 5 of $15,400 plus GST – based upon 7 days work effort.

All travel to/from Sydney is billed at cost, with a daily per diem in accordance with the applicable ATO schedule per diem payable. The current CBS Group Services Agreement terms of engagement, including current 30 day payment terms will continue to apply to this proposal.

We look forward to the opportunity to progress this exciting initiative, and would welcome an opportunity to meet and clarify anything outlined in this proposal.

Sincerely,

[Original Signed]

Jeff Dusting 

CBS Group Pty Ltd

Accepted on behalf of Downer

____________________

[Name / Signature / Date]



| Stage | Scope | Days Work Effort | Period | Location |

| 1 | Work with the Fleet Enhancement Project team to confirm the modelling that has been completed and the assumptions base for the FE04 initiative. | 1 | March 2020 | 1 Day in North Ryde Office |

| 2 | Complete analysis of the likelihood of successful funding against via the Federal Emissions Reduction Fund, Clean Energy Innovation Fund, NSW Energy Saving Scheme and via an Energy Performance Contract. | 2 | March 2020 | Offsite works |

| 3 | Develop presentation materials and support initial discussions with the State to determine the level of support for the approach (FE04) | 2 | March 2020 | TBC |

| 4 | Develop a paper / presentation that outlines the strategic opportunity for Downer to apply these funding mechanisms elsewhere in the business. | 1 | March 2020 | TBC |

| 5 | Present the outcomes and recommended next steps to Downer Leadership – as they relate to the Fleet Enhancement Program FE04, with recommendations regarding future initiatives. | 1 | April 2020 | TBC |

| 6 | Develop the applicable scheme documentation | [TBC] | April+ | TBC |

|  |  |  |  |  |

|  | Total | 7d | March – April 2020 | March – April 2020 |

Answer

We propose that Downer engage CBS Group to investigate the feasibility of these funding sources – using of the Fleet Enhancement HVAC Energy Efficiency Initiative (FE04) as the focus for the initial works, providing the basis for subsequent application of these mechanisms in other contexts.

Approach

We propose a 3 stage approach to the engagement as shown in the table below.

Nominated Persons and Terms of Engagement

CBS proposes that Jeff Dusting lead this work.   Jeff has prior experience with Fleet Enhancement Program and team, the applicable schemes, and considerable commercial acumen.  As this is a short-term, part-time engagement, a daily rate of $2200 plus GST applies, billable on a pro-rata basis using an 8 hour day. For ease of budgeting, we propose a capped fee for Stages 1 - 5 of $15,400 plus GST – based upon 7 days work effort.

All travel to/from Sydney is billed at cost, with a daily per diem in accordance with the applicable ATO schedule per diem payable. The current CBS Group Services Agreement terms of engagement, including current 30 day payment terms will continue to apply to this proposal.

We look forward to the opportunity to progress this exciting initiative and would welcome an opportunity to meet and clarify anything outlined in this proposal.

Sincerely,

[Original Signed]

Jeff Dusting 

CBS Group Pty Ltd

Accepted on behalf of Downer

____________________

| 2 | Complete analysis of the likelihood of successful funding against via the Federal Emissions Reduction Fund, Clean Energy Innovation Fund, NSW Energy Saving Scheme and via an Energy Performance Contract. | 2 | March 2020 | Offsite works |

| 3 | Develop presentation materials and support initial discussions with the State to determine the level of support for the approach (FE04)

## CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support Rev D.pdf

*File: `Downer Alt Funding Proposals/CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support Rev D.pdf`*

25 February 2020

Mr David Rees
T3, Triniti Business Campus,
39 Delhi Road
North Ryde NSW 2113



Dear Dave,

Re: Alternate Funding Mechanisms | Proposal for CBS Group Support

As we discussed last week, enclosed is an updated proposal for CBS Group to work with you to further qualify the
potential for a range of alternate funding mechanisms to be used to support current operations and initiatives –
initially focused upon the TfNSW Fleet Enhancement (HVAC Upgrade). We have completed a review of the FE04 HVAC
Energy Efficiency Report and updated the proposal accordingly.


1. Context
     Situation
     Downer has a number of innovations and improvements they want to propose to their clients for mutual
     benefit. One recent example is the development of a suite of enhancements for the TfNSW Rolling Stock fleet
     that included improvements with measurable benefits in reduced maintenance, improved passenger experience
     & safety, and energy usage. The HVAC Energy Efficiency initiative is a well-developed initiative with a great deal
     of pre-existing modelling already completed.
     Complication
     As is often the case, these innovation and improvement initiatives struggle to progress from concept to project
     execution. Often this is due to challenges securing the up-front capital to fund the project, typically because;
     •     the benefits are accrued by others – other departments, customers, the public, etc.
     •     the benefits in operation expense cannot be accrued to create the required capital
     Question
     The focus upon CO2 emissions reductions has led to the establishment of a number of funding mechanisms
     designed to provide capex support for energy reduction initiatives. Are we able to leverage these funding
     mechanisms to secure the upfront capital needed to mobilise the Sydney Fleet, HVAC Energy Efficiency
     inoitiative. And if so, are we able to extend the learnings to determine what the boundaries are for other
     similar Downer initiatives.
     Answer
     We propose that Downer engage CBS Group to investigate the feasibility of these funding sources – using of the
     Fleet Enhancement HVAC Energy Efficiency Initiative (FE04) as the focus for the initial works, providing the basis
     for subsequent application of these mechanisms in other contexts.
3. Approach
     We propose a 3 stage approach to the engagement as shown in the table below.


     Stage Scope                                                                            Days Work     Period       Location
                                                                                              Effort
       1     a) Review the FE01 initiative and work with the Fleet Enhancement Project         1        March 2020    1 Day in
                team to confirm the modelling that has been completed and the                                        North Ryde
                assumptions base for the FE04 initiative.                                                              Office

       2     Complete analysis of the likelihood of successful funding against via the         2        March 2020     Offsite
             Federal Emissions Reduction Fund, Clean Energy Innovation Fund, NSW Energy                                works
             Saving Scheme and via an Energy Performance Contract.

       3     • Develop presentation materials and support initial discussions with the         3        March 2020       TBC
               State to determine the level of support for the approach (FE04)

             • Develop a paper / presentation that outlines the strategic opportunity for
               Downer to apply these funding mechanisms elsewhere in the business.

       5     Present the outcomes and recommended next steps to Downer Leadership –            1        April 2020       TBC
             as they relate to the Fleet Enhancement Program FE04, with
             recommendations regarding future initiatives.
       6     Develop the applicable scheme documentation                                      [TBC]       April+         TBC



             Total                                                                             7d         March – April 2020




4. Nominated Persons and Terms of Engagement
     CBS proposes that Jeff Dusting lead this work. Jeff has prior experience with Fleet Enhancement Program and team, the
     applicable schemes, and considerable commercial acumen. As this is a short-term, part-time engagement, a daily rate of

## CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support STage 2 Rev B.docx

*File: `Downer Alt Funding Proposals/CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support STage 2 Rev B.docx`*

27 April 2020

Mr. David Rees
T3, Triniti Business Campus, 
39 Delhi Road
North Ryde NSW 2113

Dear Dave,

Re: Alternate Funding Mechanisms | Sydney Fleet HVAC Initiative | Stage 2

Context

As confirmed last week in our presentation to you, the NSW Energy Savings Scheme (ESS) is likely to provide $7.5m in funding to support the NSW Fleet HVAC Enhancement Proposal – regardless of the overall project funding mechanism.   

Our recommended funding method, the Analysis and Verification method, provides for funding prior to the modification implementation - this funding is an incentive to reduce energy consumption and is independent of project cost.  This mechanism will however require us to work with your team to divide the fleet or modification program into approximately 5 modification tranches.

To secure these funds as soon as possible, we recommend an immediate start to the ESS process for this project, and this proposal outlines the recommended approach.

Key Activities

Broadly, the key actions that must be completed are:

Document
Define and document the project, scope of HVAC modifications, and proposed Analysis and Verification approach.

Program ‘Tranche’ Definition
Work with the Fleet Engineering team to optimise the HVAC modification program and ESS framework to maximise net revenue (cost) of the modification for Downer

Certificate Issuer
Identify a suitable accredited ESS Certificate issuer to review, amend and approve the proposed measurement and verification methodology for this project 

Stakeholder Management
Prepare and support delivery of briefings to TfNSW on the proposed application

Application Management
Manage the application and fulfilment process including the provision of mandated measurement and verification reporting

Approach

As the level of effort and timeframe is uncertain for each of the activities described above, CBS Group proposes a weekly rate of effort engagement.   Under this model:

CBS Group partner and supporting staff will be assigned to these activities at not more than 3 days total per week, and 10 days in any month

We will invoice monthly, on a Time and Materials but not exceeding 10 days in any month without prior approval by Downer

The expected engagement timeframe is 3 months, after which time, the engagement may be extended by mutual agreement.

The following table provides an estimate of the work effort allocation to each key activity and a high level timeframe.

Nominated Persons 

CBS proposes that Jeff Dusting lead this work. Jeff has prior experience with Fleet Enhancement Program and team, the applicable schemes, and considerable commercial acumen. 

The Independent Auditor will be engaged separately at cost, under terms and conditions approved by Downer prior to commencement.

Pricing and Terms of Engagement

Based upon a blended rate of $1900 per day excluding GST, we expect a total engagement fee of $76000 excluding GST.

Any travel to/from Sydney is billed at cost, with a daily per diem in accordance with the applicable ATO schedule per diem payable.

The current CBS Group Services Agreement terms of engagement, including current 30 day payment terms will continue to apply to this proposal.

We look forward to the opportunity to progress this exciting initiative and would welcome an opportunity to clarify anything outlined in this proposal.

Sincerely, 

[Original Signed] 

____________________________________________________________

Jeff Dusting
CBS Group Pty Ltd

Accepted on behalf of Downer

____________________________________________________________

[Name / Signature / Date]



| Stage Scope | Stage Scope | Estimate CBS Days | Period | Location |

| 1 | Document | Define and document the project, scope of HVAC modifications, and proposed Analysis and Verification approach. | 5-10 | May 2020 | Virtual |

| 2 | Program ‘Tranche’ Definition | Work with the Fleet Engineering team to optimise the HVAC modification program and ESS framework to maximise net revenue (cost) of the modification for Downer within the modification schedule. | 5-10 | May – June 2020 | Virtual / North Ryde |

| 3 | Certificate Issuer } Identify a suitable accredited ESS Certificate issuer to review, amend and approve the proposed measurement and verification methodology for this project - aligned with the Program and Project requirements | 5-10 | May – June 2020 | Virtual |

| 4 | Stakeholder Management } Prepare and support delivery of briefings to TfNSW on the proposed application | 5 - 7 | May – June 2020 | TBC |

| 5 | Application Management | Manage the application and fulfilment process including the provision of mandated measurement and verification reporting | 10-15 | May – July 2020 | Virtual / North Ryde |

| Total | Total | 40 | May – July 2020 | May – July 2020 |

27 April 2020

Mr. David Rees
T3, Triniti Business Campus, 
39 Delhi Road
North Ryde NSW 2113

Dear Dave,

Re: Alternate Funding Mechanisms | Sydney Fleet HVAC Initiative | Next Steps

Context

As affirmed last week in our presentation to you, the NSW ESS Scheme, using the Analysis & Verification methodology, is most appropriate energy based funding mechanism for the Sydney Fleet HVAC Initiative.

ESS provides up to $7.5m in funding in advance of installation. This funding is an incentive to reduce energy consumption and is independent of project cost. 

This proposal outlines how CBS Group can support the follow-on actions required to access these funds.

Requirements

Broadly, the actions required to become eligible for ESS funds are:

Define and document the project and scope of HVAC modifications to optimize the application for ESS funds

Provision of an approved auditor to establish a suitable measurement and verification methodology for this project

Ensure the ESS project definition aligns with the requirements of the project implementation 

Prepare and deliver briefing materials for TfNSW on the proposed application

Manage the application and fulfilment process including the provision of mandated measurement and verification reporting*

Approach

CBS Group propose to undertake each of these actions as the second stage of work, noting the requirement to manage the application and fulfilment process including the provision of mandated measurement and verification reporting over time is currently outside the scope of this proposal and would form a third stage as the project goes live.

Nominated Persons and Terms of Engagement

CBS proposes that Jeff Dusting lead this work. Jeff has prior experience with Fleet Enhancement Program and team, the applicable schemes, and considerable commercial acumen. As this is a short-term, part-time engagement, a daily rate of $2200 plus GST applies, billable on a pro-rata basis using an 8-hour day. 

Our estimate is 23 - 35 Days work effort. Estimated cost range is $66,000 - $92,400.

The current CBS Group Services Agreement terms of engagement, including current 30 day payment terms will continue to apply to this proposal.

Any travel to/from Sydney is billed at cost, with a daily per diem in accordance with the applicable ATO schedule per diem payable.

We look forward to the opportunity to progress this exciting initiative and would welcome an opportunity to clarify anything outlined in this proposal.

Sincerely, 

[Original Signed] 

____________________________________________________________

Jeff Dusting
CBS Group Pty Ltd

Accepted on behalf of Downer

## CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support.04282020 (1).docx

*File: `Downer Alt Funding Proposals/CBS Proposal - Downer Alternate Funding Support.04282020 (1).docx`*

27 April 2020

Mr. David Rees
T3, Triniti Business Campus, 
39 Delhi Road
North Ryde NSW 2113

Dear Dave,

Re: Alternate Funding Mechanisms | Sydney Fleet HVAC Initiative | Next Steps

Context

As affirmed last week in our presentation to you, the NSW ESS Scheme, using the Analysis & Verification methodology, is most appropriate energy-based funding mechanism for the Sydney Fleet HVAC Initiative.

ESS may provide up to $7.5m in funding in advance of installation. This funding is an incentive to reduce energy consumption and is independent of project cost. 

This proposal outlines how CBS Group can support the follow-on actions required to apply for these funds.

Requirements

Broadly, the actions required to become eligible for ESS funds are:

Define and document the project and scope of HVAC modifications to optimize the application for ESS funds

Provision of an approved auditor to establish a suitable measurement and verification methodology for this project

Ensure the ESS project definition aligns with the requirements of the project implementation 

Prepare and deliver briefing materials for TfNSW on the proposed application

Upon Project establishment a further requirement is to

Manage the application and fulfilment process including the provision of mandated measurement and verification reporting. *

*This activity is not included in this proposal.

Approach

CBS Group propose with your team to project manage the ESS stream of work on your behalf. We anticipate the work effort to be approx. 2.5 days/per week for three months. This approach minimises your up-front commitment and allows an end of month review and check point that provides a go/no-go check point and a review of works outstanding.

The principal tasks are to: 

Define and document the ‘tranches’ and scope of modifications (HVAC and others TBC) to optimize the application for ESS funds. 

Ensure alignment with the requirements of the project implementation. 

Concurrently, identify and partner with an approved auditor to establish a suitable methodology that aligns with Program and Project requirements.

Prepare and deliver briefing materials for TfNSW on the proposed application.

Other tasks that may be required to ensure certification for ESS.

Nominated Persons and Terms of Engagement

CBS proposes a blended team, led by Jeff Dusting to complete this work. Jeff has prior experience with Fleet Enhancement Program and team, the applicable schemes, and considerable commercial acumen. A daily rate of $2000 plus GST is billable on a pro-rata basis using an 8-hour day. Our estimate is 23 - 35 Days work effort. Estimated cost range for CBS Services is $66,000 - $92,400.

The Independent Auditor will be engaged separately at cost, under terms and conditions approved by Downer prior to commencement.

The current CBS Group Services Agreement terms of engagement, including current 30 day payment terms will continue to apply to this proposal.

Any travel to/from Sydney is billed at cost, with a daily per diem in accordance with the applicable ATO schedule per diem payable.

We look forward to the opportunity to progress this exciting initiative and would welcome an opportunity to clarify anything outlined in this proposal.

We look forward to the opportunity to progress this exciting initiative and would welcome an opportunity
to clarify anything outlined in this proposal.


Sincerely,


[Original Signed]
____________________________________________________________
Jeff Dusting
CBS Group Pty Ltd




Accepted on behalf of Downer
